        Sid    = "SendEmail"
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = [
          "ses:SendEmail",
          "ses:SendRawEmail"
        ]
        Resource = "*"
        Condition = {
          StringEquals = {
            "ses:FromAddress" = "noreply@${var.domain_name}"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  })
}

# Attach policies to role
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "django_secrets" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.django_app.name
  policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.secrets_access.arn
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "django_s3" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.django_app.name
  policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.django_s3_access.arn
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "django_ses" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.django_app.name
  policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.ses_send.arn
}

# Django Secret Key in Secrets Manager
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "django_secret_key" {
  name = "anihpj/django-secret-key-${var.environment}"

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-django-secret-${var.environment}"
  }
}

resource "random_password" "django_secret_key" {
  length  = 50
  special = true
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "django_secret_key" {
  secret_id = aws_secretsmanager_secret.django_secret_key.id
  secret_string = jsonencode({
    DJANGO_SECRET_KEY = random_password.django_secret_key.result
  })
}

# KMS Key for Secrets
resource "aws_kms_key" "secrets" {
  description             = "KMS key for anihpj secrets encryption"
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
  enable_key_rotation     = true

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-secrets-key-${var.environment}"
  }
}
🔐
Security Best Practices
  • Use IAM roles instead of access keys
  • Store secrets in Secrets Manager, not environment variables
  • Enable KMS encryption for all data at rest
  • Use security groups as virtual firewalls
  • Enable VPC Flow Logs for network monitoring
  • Implement least-privilege access

3.9 Route 53 & DNS

Route 53 provides highly available and scalable DNS services for your Django application with custom domains.

route53.tf - DNS Configuration 
# =========================================
# Route 53 Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# Hosted Zone
resource "aws_route53_zone" "main" {
  name = var.domain_name

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-zone-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# A Record for Root Domain (Alias to ALB)
resource "aws_route53_record" "root" {
  zone_id = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
  name    = var.domain_name
  type    = "A"

  alias {
    name                   = aws_lb.main.dns_name
    zone_id                = aws_lb.main.zone_id
    evaluate_target_health = true
  }
}

# A Record for www Subdomain
resource "aws_route53_record" "www" {
  zone_id = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
  name    = "www.${var.domain_name}"
  type    = "A"

  alias {
    name                   = aws_lb.main.dns_name
    zone_id                = aws_lb.main.zone_id
    evaluate_target_health = true
  }
}

# CNAME for API subdomain
resource "aws_route53_record" "api" {
  zone_id = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
  name    = "api.${var.domain_name}"
  type    = "CNAME"
  ttl     = 300
  records = [aws_lb.main.dns_name]
}

# Health Check for ALB
resource "aws_route53_health_check" "main" {
  fqdn              = aws_lb.main.dns_name
  port              = 443
  type              = "HTTPS"
  resource_path     = "/health/"
  failure_threshold = 3
  request_interval  = 30

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-health-check-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# CloudWatch Alarm for Health Check
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "health_check" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-health-check-alarm-${var.environment}"
  comparison_operator = "LessThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = "2"
  metric_name         = "HealthCheckStatus"
  namespace           = "AWS/Route53"
  period              = "60"
  statistic           = "Minimum"
  threshold           = "1"
  alarm_description   = "This metric monitors Route53 health check status"

  dimensions = {
    HealthCheckId = aws_route53_health_check.main.id
  }

  alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]
}
Record Type Use Case Example TTL
A IPv4 address example.com → 192.0.2.1 300s
AAAA IPv6 address example.com → 2001:0db8::1 300s
CNAME Alias to another domain www → example.com 3600s
ALIAS AWS resource (ALB, CloudFront) example.com → ALB N/A
MX Mail server Priority 10, mail.example.com 3600s
TXT Verification, SPF, DKIM v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com 3600s
💡
DNS Propagation Tips
  • Use low TTL (300s) during initial setup for faster testing
  • Increase TTL (3600s+) once stable to reduce costs
  • ALIAS records are free (unlike CNAME)
  • Always use ALIAS for root domain (example.com)

3.10 CloudFront CDN

Accelerate static and media file delivery globally with CloudFront CDN integration for your Django application.

cloudfront.tf - CDN Configuration 
# =========================================
# CloudFront Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# Origin Access Control for S3
resource "aws_cloudfront_origin_access_control" "s3" {
  name                              = "anihpj-oac-${var.environment}"
  description                       = "OAC for S3 static/media buckets"
  origin_access_control_origin_type = "s3"
  signing_behavior                  = "always"
  signing_protocol                  = "sigv4"
}

# CloudFront Distribution
resource "aws_cloudfront_distribution" "main" {
  enabled             = true
  is_ipv6_enabled     = true
  comment             = "anihpj CDN - ${var.environment}"
  default_root_object = "index.html"
  price_class         = var.environment == "production" ? "PriceClass_All" : "PriceClass_100"
  aliases             = ["cdn.${var.domain_name}"]

  # S3 Static Files Origin
  origin {
    domain_name              = aws_s3_bucket.static.bucket_regional_domain_name
    origin_id                = "S3-Static"
    origin_access_control_id = aws_cloudfront_origin_access_control.s3.id
  }

  # S3 Media Files Origin
  origin {
    domain_name              = aws_s3_bucket.media.bucket_regional_domain_name
    origin_id                = "S3-Media"
    origin_access_control_id = aws_cloudfront_origin_access_control.s3.id
  }

  # ALB Origin (for dynamic content)
  origin {
    domain_name = aws_lb.main.dns_name
    origin_id   = "ALB-Django"

    custom_origin_config {
      http_port              = 80
      https_port             = 443
      origin_protocol_policy = "https-only"
      origin_ssl_protocols   = ["TLSv1.2"]
    }

    custom_header {
      name  = "X-Custom-Header"
      value = var.cloudfront_secret
    }
  }

  # Default Cache Behavior (Static Files)
  default_cache_behavior {
    allowed_methods  = ["GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS"]
    cached_methods   = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    target_origin_id = "S3-Static"

    forwarded_values {
      query_string = false
      headers      = ["Origin", "Access-Control-Request-Headers", "Access-Control-Request-Method"]

      cookies {
        forward = "none"
      }
    }

    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 3600
    max_ttl                = 86400
    compress               = true
  }

  # Media Files Cache Behavior
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern     = "/media/*"
    allowed_methods  = ["GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS"]
    cached_methods   = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    target_origin_id = "S3-Media"

    forwarded_values {
      query_string = false
      cookies {
        forward = "none"
      }
    }

    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 86400
    max_ttl                = 604800
    compress               = true
  }

  # API Cache Behavior (No caching for dynamic content)
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern     = "/api/*"
    allowed_methods  = ["DELETE", "GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PATCH", "POST", "PUT"]
    cached_methods   = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    target_origin_id = "ALB-Django"

    forwarded_values {
      query_string = true
      headers      = ["*"]

      cookies {
        forward = "all"
      }
    }

    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 0
    max_ttl                = 0
  }

  # Admin Cache Behavior (No caching)
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern     = "/admin/*"
    allowed_methods  = ["DELETE", "GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PATCH", "POST", "PUT"]
    cached_methods   = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    target_origin_id = "ALB-Django"

    forwarded_values {
      query_string = true
      headers      = ["*"]

      cookies {
        forward = "all"
      }
    }

    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 0
    max_ttl                = 0
  }

  # Geo Restrictions
  restrictions {
    geo_restriction {
      restriction_type = "none"
    }
  }

  # SSL Certificate
  viewer_certificate {
    acm_certificate_arn      = aws_acm_certificate.cdn.arn
    ssl_support_method       = "sni-only"
    minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1.2_2021"
  }

  # Custom Error Responses
  custom_error_response {
    error_code         = 404
    response_code      = 404
    response_page_path = "/404.html"
  }

  custom_error_response {
    error_code         = 500
    response_code      = 500
    response_page_path = "/500.html"
  }

  # Logging
  logging_config {
    include_cookies = false
    bucket          = aws_s3_bucket.cloudfront_logs.bucket_domain_name
    prefix          = "cloudfront/"
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-cdn-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# CloudFront Function for URL rewrites
resource "aws_cloudfront_function" "url_rewrite" {
  name    = "anihpj-url-rewrite-${var.environment}"
  runtime = "cloudfront-js-1.0"
  comment = "Rewrite URLs for Django static files"
  publish = true
  code    = file("${path.module}/functions/url-rewrite.js")
}
Path Pattern Origin Cache TTL Cookies
/static/* S3 Static 1 hour None
/media/* S3 Media 24 hours None
/api/* ALB Django 0 (no cache) All
/admin/* ALB Django 0 (no cache) All
🦄
Django CloudFront Integration
# settings.py - CloudFront Configuration
AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN = 'cdn.anihpj.com'
AWS_S3_OBJECT_PARAMETERS = {
    'CacheControl': 'max-age=86400',
}

# Static files via CloudFront
STATIC_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/static/'

# Media files via CloudFront
MEDIA_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/media/'

# CloudFront invalidation after collectstatic
AWS_CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID = 'E1234567890ABC'

3.11 ACM & SSL Certificates

Secure your Django application with free SSL/TLS certificates from AWS Certificate Manager.

acm.tf - SSL Certificate Configuration 
# =========================================
# ACM Certificate Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# Certificate for ALB (regional - us-east-1 or your region)
resource "aws_acm_certificate" "main" {
  domain_name               = var.domain_name
  subject_alternative_names = ["*.${var.domain_name}"]
  validation_method         = "DNS"

  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-cert-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Certificate for CloudFront (MUST be in us-east-1)
resource "aws_acm_certificate" "cdn" {
  provider                  = aws.global
  domain_name               = "cdn.${var.domain_name}"
  subject_alternative_names = ["*.cdn.${var.domain_name}"]
  validation_method         = "DNS"

  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-cdn-cert-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# DNS Validation Records for Main Certificate
resource "aws_route53_record" "cert_validation" {
  for_each = {
    for dvo in aws_acm_certificate.main.domain_validation_options : dvo.domain_name => {
      name   = dvo.resource_record_name
      record = dvo.resource_record_value
      type   = dvo.resource_record_type
    }
  }

  allow_overwrite = true
  name            = each.value.name
  records         = [each.value.record]
  ttl             = 60
  type            = each.value.type
  zone_id         = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
}

# Certificate Validation
resource "aws_acm_certificate_validation" "main" {
  certificate_arn         = aws_acm_certificate.main.arn
  validation_record_fqdns = [for record in aws_route53_record.cert_validation : record.fqdn]
}

# DNS Validation for CDN Certificate
resource "aws_route53_record" "cdn_cert_validation" {
  for_each = {
    for dvo in aws_acm_certificate.cdn.domain_validation_options : dvo.domain_name => {
      name   = dvo.resource_record_name
      record = dvo.resource_record_value
      type   = dvo.resource_record_type
    }
  }

  allow_overwrite = true
  name            = each.value.name
  records         = [each.value.record]
  ttl             = 60
  type            = each.value.type
  zone_id         = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
}

resource "aws_acm_certificate_validation" "cdn" {
  provider                = aws.global
  certificate_arn         = aws_acm_certificate.cdn.arn
  validation_record_fqdns = [for record in aws_route53_record.cdn_cert_validation : record.fqdn]
}
⚠️
CloudFront Certificate Region Requirement

CloudFront distributions MUST use certificates from us-east-1 region. Use provider alias aws.global for CloudFront certificates.

Validation Method Speed Automation Best For
DNS 5-10 minutes Fully automated Production (recommended)
Email Variable Manual approval Testing only

3.12 CloudWatch & Monitoring

Monitor your Django application with CloudWatch logs, metrics, and alarms for proactive issue detection.

cloudwatch.tf - Monitoring Configuration 
# =========================================
# CloudWatch Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# Log Groups
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "django_app" {
  name              = "/aws/django/anihpj-${var.environment}"
  retention_in_days = var.environment == "production" ? 30 : 7

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-app-logs-${var.environment}"
  }
}

resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "nginx" {
  name              = "/aws/nginx/anihpj-${var.environment}"
  retention_in_days = 14

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-nginx-logs-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# SNS Topic for Alerts
resource "aws_sns_topic" "alerts" {
  name = "anihpj-alerts-${var.environment}"

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-alerts-${var.environment}"
  }
}

resource "aws_sns_topic_subscription" "email" {
  topic_arn = aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn
  protocol  = "email"
  endpoint  = var.alert_email
}

# ALB Target Unhealthy Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "alb_unhealthy_hosts" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-unhealthy-hosts-${var.environment}"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = "2"
  metric_name         = "UnHealthyHostCount"
  namespace           = "AWS/ApplicationELB"
  period              = "60"
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = "0"
  alarm_description   = "Alert when targets are unhealthy"
  alarm_actions       = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]

  dimensions = {
    LoadBalancer = aws_lb.main.arn_suffix
    TargetGroup  = aws_lb_target_group.web.arn_suffix
  }
}

# RDS CPU Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "rds_cpu" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-rds-cpu-${var.environment}"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = "2"
  metric_name         = "CPUUtilization"
  namespace           = "AWS/RDS"
  period              = "300"
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = "80"
  alarm_description   = "Alert when RDS CPU exceeds 80%"
  alarm_actions       = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]

  dimensions = {
    DBInstanceIdentifier = aws_db_instance.main.id
  }
}

# RDS Storage Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "rds_storage" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-rds-storage-${var.environment}"
  comparison_operator = "LessThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = "1"
  metric_name         = "FreeStorageSpace"
  namespace           = "AWS/RDS"
  period              = "300"
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = "10000000000"  # 10 GB in bytes
  alarm_description   = "Alert when free storage below 10GB"
  alarm_actions       = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]

  dimensions = {
    DBInstanceIdentifier = aws_db_instance.main.id
  }
}

# ElastiCache CPU Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "redis_cpu" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-redis-cpu-${var.environment}"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = "2"
  metric_name         = "CPUUtilization"
  namespace           = "AWS/ElastiCache"
  period              = "300"
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = "75"
  alarm_description   = "Alert when Redis CPU exceeds 75%"
  alarm_actions       = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]

  dimensions = {
    CacheClusterId = aws_elasticache_cluster.redis.id
  }
}

# Dashboard
resource "aws_cloudwatch_dashboard" "main" {
  dashboard_name = "anihpj-${var.environment}"

  dashboard_body = jsonencode({
    widgets = [
      {
        type = "metric"
        properties = {
          metrics = [
            ["AWS/ApplicationELB", "TargetResponseTime", { stat = "Average" }],
            [".", "RequestCount", { stat = "Sum" }]
          ]
          period = 300
          stat   = "Average"
          region = var.aws_region
          title  = "ALB Performance"
        }
      },
      {
        type = "metric"
        properties = {
          metrics = [
            ["AWS/RDS", "CPUUtilization", { stat = "Average" }],
            [".", "DatabaseConnections", { stat = "Sum" }]
          ]
          period = 300
          stat   = "Average"
          region = var.aws_region
          title  = "RDS Metrics"
        }
      }
    ]
  })
}
💡
Monitoring Best Practices
  • Set up composite alarms for complex scenarios
  • Use anomaly detection for dynamic thresholds
  • Enable detailed monitoring for EC2 (1-min intervals)
  • Configure log metric filters for Django errors

3.13 Lambda & Serverless

Run background tasks and scheduled jobs with AWS Lambda - perfect for Django management commands and async processing.

lambda.tf - Lambda Functions 
# =========================================
# Lambda Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# IAM Role for Lambda
resource "aws_iam_role" "lambda" {
  name = "anihpj-lambda-role-${var.environment}"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "lambda.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "lambda_basic" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.lambda.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole"
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "lambda_vpc" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.lambda.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaVPCAccessExecutionRole"
}

# Lambda Layer (shared dependencies)
resource "aws_lambda_layer_version" "django_deps" {
  filename            = "lambda-layer.zip"
  layer_name          = "anihpj-django-deps-${var.environment}"
  compatible_runtimes = ["python3.11"]

  description = "Django and common dependencies"
}

# Lambda Function - Database Backup
resource "aws_lambda_function" "db_backup" {
  filename         = "lambda-functions/db_backup.zip"
  function_name    = "anihpj-db-backup-${var.environment}"
  role            = aws_iam_role.lambda.arn
  handler         = "handler.backup"
  source_code_hash = filebase64sha256("lambda-functions/db_backup.zip")
  runtime         = "python3.11"
  timeout         = 300
  memory_size     = 512

  environment {
    variables = {
      DB_HOST     = aws_db_instance.main.address
      DB_NAME     = aws_db_instance.main.db_name
      S3_BUCKET   = aws_s3_bucket.backups.id
      ENVIRONMENT = var.environment
    }
  }

  vpc_config {
    subnet_ids         = aws_subnet.private[*].id
    security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.lambda.id]
  }

  layers = [aws_lambda_layer_version.django_deps.arn]

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-db-backup-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Lambda Function - Cleanup Old Jobs
resource "aws_lambda_function" "cleanup_jobs" {
  filename         = "lambda-functions/cleanup.zip"
  function_name    = "anihpj-cleanup-jobs-${var.environment}"
  role            = aws_iam_role.lambda.arn
  handler         = "handler.cleanup"
  source_code_hash = filebase64sha256("lambda-functions/cleanup.zip")
  runtime         = "python3.11"
  timeout         = 60
  memory_size     = 256

  environment {
    variables = {
      DB_HOST     = aws_db_instance.main.address
      DB_NAME     = aws_db_instance.main.db_name
      DB_USER     = var.db_username
      DB_PASSWORD = var.db_password
    }
  }

  vpc_config {
    subnet_ids         = aws_subnet.private[*].id
    security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.lambda.id]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-cleanup-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Lambda Function URL (for webhooks)
resource "aws_lambda_function_url" "webhook" {
  function_name      = aws_lambda_function.cleanup_jobs.function_name
  authorization_type = "AWS_IAM"

  cors {
    allow_credentials = true
    allow_origins     = ["https://anihpj.com"]
    allow_methods     = ["POST"]
    max_age          = 86400
  }
}

# EventBridge Rule - Daily Backup
resource "aws_cloudwatch_event_rule" "daily_backup" {
  name                = "anihpj-daily-backup-${var.environment}"
  description         = "Trigger daily database backup"
  schedule_expression = "cron(0 2 * * ? *)"  # 2 AM UTC daily
}

resource "aws_cloudwatch_event_target" "backup_lambda" {
  rule      = aws_cloudwatch_event_rule.daily_backup.name
  target_id = "lambda"
  arn       = aws_lambda_function.db_backup.arn
}

resource "aws_lambda_permission" "allow_eventbridge" {
  statement_id  = "AllowExecutionFromEventBridge"
  action        = "lambda:InvokeFunction"
  function_name = aws_lambda_function.db_backup.function_name
  principal     = "events.amazonaws.com"
  source_arn    = aws_cloudwatch_event_rule.daily_backup.arn
}

# Security Group for Lambda in VPC
resource "aws_security_group" "lambda" {
  name        = "anihpj-lambda-sg-${var.environment}"
  description = "Security group for Lambda functions"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-lambda-sg-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Output Lambda URLs
output "lambda_webhook_url" {
  description = "Lambda function URL for webhooks"
  value       = aws_lambda_function_url.webhook.function_url
  sensitive   = true
}
🐍
Django Lambda Handler Example
# lambda-functions/handler.py
import json
import os
import django
from django.core.management import call_command

# Setup Django
os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'anihpj.settings')
django.setup()

def cleanup(event, context):
    """Clean up old job postings"""
    from jobpost.models import JobPost
    from datetime import timedelta
    from django.utils import timezone
    
    cutoff = timezone.now() - timedelta(days=90)
    deleted = JobPost.objects.filter(
        created_at__lt=cutoff,
        is_active=False
    ).delete()
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'body': json.dumps({
            'deleted_count': deleted[0]
        })
    }
💡
Lambda Best Practices for Django
  • Use layers for Django dependencies to reduce function size
  • Minimize cold start time by importing Django modules inside handlers
  • Set reasonable timeouts (max 15 minutes for Lambda)
  • Use VPC config to access RDS, but be aware of ENI overhead
  • Consider Lambda SnapStart for faster cold starts (Java only currently)

3.14 SQS & SNS Messaging

Implement asynchronous messaging and pub/sub patterns for Django task queues (Celery) and notifications.

messaging.tf - SQS and SNS Configuration 
# =========================================
# SQS & SNS Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# SQS Queue - Celery Default
resource "aws_sqs_queue" "celery_default" {
  name                       = "anihpj-celery-default-${var.environment}"
  delay_seconds              = 0
  max_message_size           = 262144
  message_retention_seconds  = 86400  # 24 hours
  receive_wait_time_seconds  = 10     # Long polling
  visibility_timeout_seconds = 300

  redrive_policy = jsonencode({
    deadLetterTargetArn = aws_sqs_queue.celery_dlq.arn
    maxReceiveCount     = 3
  })

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-celery-default-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# SQS Dead Letter Queue
resource "aws_sqs_queue" "celery_dlq" {
  name                      = "anihpj-celery-dlq-${var.environment}"
  message_retention_seconds = 1209600  # 14 days

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-celery-dlq-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# SQS Queue - Priority Tasks (FIFO)
resource "aws_sqs_queue" "priority_tasks" {
  name                        = "anihpj-priority-tasks-${var.environment}.fifo"
  fifo_queue                  = true
  content_based_deduplication = true
  deduplication_scope         = "messageGroup"
  fifo_throughput_limit       = "perMessageGroupId"
  
  visibility_timeout_seconds = 300
  message_retention_seconds  = 86400

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-priority-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# SNS Topic - Application Events
resource "aws_sns_topic" "app_events" {
  name              = "anihpj-app-events-${var.environment}"
  display_name      = "Anihpj Application Events"
  fifo_topic        = false
  
  delivery_policy = jsonencode({
    http = {
      defaultHealthyRetryPolicy = {
        minDelayTarget     = 20
        maxDelayTarget     = 20
        numRetries         = 3
        numMaxDelayRetries = 0
        numNoDelayRetries  = 0
        numMinDelayRetries = 0
        backoffFunction    = "linear"
      }
    }
  })

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-events-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# SNS Topic - User Notifications
resource "aws_sns_topic" "user_notifications" {
  name         = "anihpj-notifications-${var.environment}"
  display_name = "User Notifications"

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-notifications-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# SNS Subscription - Email Notifications
resource "aws_sns_topic_subscription" "email_notifications" {
  topic_arn = aws_sns_topic.user_notifications.arn
  protocol  = "email"
  endpoint  = var.notification_email
}

# SNS Subscription - SQS for Event Processing
resource "aws_sns_topic_subscription" "events_to_sqs" {
  topic_arn = aws_sns_topic.app_events.arn
  protocol  = "sqs"
  endpoint  = aws_sqs_queue.celery_default.arn

  filter_policy = jsonencode({
    event_type = ["job_created", "application_received"]
  })
}

# SQS Queue Policy (allow SNS to send messages)
resource "aws_sqs_queue_policy" "celery_default" {
  queue_url = aws_sqs_queue.celery_default.id

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "sns.amazonaws.com"
      }
      Action   = "sqs:SendMessage"
      Resource = aws_sqs_queue.celery_default.arn
      Condition = {
        ArnEquals = {
          "aws:SourceArn" = aws_sns_topic.app_events.arn
        }
      }
    }]
  })
}

# CloudWatch Alarms for Queue Monitoring
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "queue_depth" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-queue-depth-${var.environment}"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = "2"
  metric_name         = "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible"
  namespace           = "AWS/SQS"
  period              = "300"
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = "1000"
  alarm_description   = "Alert when queue depth exceeds 1000"
  alarm_actions       = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]

  dimensions = {
    QueueName = aws_sqs_queue.celery_default.name
  }
}

resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "dlq_messages" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-dlq-messages-${var.environment}"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = "1"
  metric_name         = "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible"
  namespace           = "AWS/SQS"
  period              = "60"
  statistic           = "Sum"
  threshold           = "0"
  alarm_description   = "Alert when messages appear in DLQ"
  alarm_actions       = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]

  dimensions = {
    QueueName = aws_sqs_queue.celery_dlq.name
  }
}

# Outputs
output "sqs_queue_url" {
  description = "URL of the Celery SQS queue"
  value       = aws_sqs_queue.celery_default.url
}

output "sns_topic_arn" {
  description = "ARN of the app events SNS topic"
  value       = aws_sns_topic.app_events.arn
}
Feature Standard Queue FIFO Queue
Ordering Best-effort ordering Strict FIFO ordering
Throughput Unlimited 300 TPS (batched: 3000)
Deduplication No built-in Content-based or ID-based
Delivery At-least-once Exactly-once processing
Use Case High-throughput tasks Order-critical operations
🐍
Django Celery with SQS Backend
# anihpj/celery.py
import os
from celery import Celery

os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'anihpj.settings')

app = Celery('anihpj')
app.config_from_object('django.conf:settings', namespace='CELERY')

# SQS Backend Configuration
app.conf.update(
    broker_url='sqs://',
    broker_transport_options={
        'region': 'us-east-1',
        'visibility_timeout': 300,
        'polling_interval': 10,
        'queue_name_prefix': 'anihpj-',
    },
    task_default_queue='celery-default',
    task_routes={
        'jobpost.tasks.send_email': {'queue': 'priority-tasks'},
    }
)

app.autodiscover_tasks()

# Example Task
@app.task(bind=True, max_retries=3)
def send_application_notification(self, job_id, applicant_email):
    try:
        # Send notification logic
        pass
    except Exception as exc:
        raise self.retry(exc=exc, countdown=60)

3.15 Container Services (ECS/ECR)

Run containerized Django applications on AWS ECS Fargate with automatic scaling and zero server management.

ecs.tf - Container Configuration 
# =========================================
# ECS Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# ECR Repository
resource "aws_ecr_repository" "django_app" {
  name                 = "anihpj-django-${var.environment}"
  image_tag_mutability = "MUTABLE"

  image_scanning_configuration {
    scan_on_push = true
  }

  encryption_configuration {
    encryption_type = "AES256"
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-ecr-${var.environment}"
  }
}

resource "aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy" "django_app" {
  repository = aws_ecr_repository.django_app.name

  policy = jsonencode({
    rules = [{
      rulePriority = 1
      description  = "Keep last 10 images"
      selection = {
        tagStatus     = "tagged"
        tagPrefixList = ["v"]
        countType     = "imageCountMoreThan"
        countNumber   = 10
      }
      action = {
        type = "expire"
      }
    }]
  })
}

# ECS Cluster
resource "aws_ecs_cluster" "main" {
  name = "anihpj-cluster-${var.environment}"

  setting {
    name  = "containerInsights"
    value = "enabled"
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-cluster-${var.environment}"
  }
}

resource "aws_ecs_cluster_capacity_providers" "main" {
  cluster_name = aws_ecs_cluster.main.name

  capacity_providers = ["FARGATE", "FARGATE_SPOT"]

  default_capacity_provider_strategy {
    base              = 1
    weight            = 100
    capacity_provider = "FARGATE"
  }
}

# ECS Task Execution Role
resource "aws_iam_role" "ecs_execution" {
  name = "anihpj-ecs-execution-${var.environment}"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "ecs_execution" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy"
}

# ECS Task Role (for application)
resource "aws_iam_role" "ecs_task" {
  name = "anihpj-ecs-task-${var.environment}"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

# Task Definition
resource "aws_ecs_task_definition" "django" {
  family                   = "anihpj-django-${var.environment}"
  network_mode             = "awsvpc"
  requires_compatibilities = ["FARGATE"]
  cpu                      = "512"
  memory                   = "1024"
  execution_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.arn
  task_role_arn            = aws_iam_role.ecs_task.arn

  container_definitions = jsonencode([
    {
      name  = "django"
      image = "${aws_ecr_repository.django_app.repository_url}:latest"
      
      portMappings = [{
        containerPort = 8000
        protocol      = "tcp"
      }]

      environment = [
        { name = "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", value = "anihpj.settings" },
        { name = "ENVIRONMENT", value = var.environment },
        { name = "DB_HOST", value = aws_db_instance.main.address },
        { name = "DB_NAME", value = aws_db_instance.main.db_name },
        { name = "REDIS_HOST", value = aws_elasticache_cluster.redis.cache_nodes[0].address }
      ]

      secrets = [
        {
          name      = "SECRET_KEY"
          valueFrom = aws_secretsmanager_secret.django_secret_key.arn
        },
        {
          name      = "DB_PASSWORD"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.arn}:password::"
        }
      ]

      logConfiguration = {
        logDriver = "awslogs"
        options = {
          "awslogs-group"         = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.ecs_django.name
          "awslogs-region"        = var.aws_region
          "awslogs-stream-prefix" = "django"
        }
      }

      healthCheck = {
        command     = ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://localhost:8000/health/ || exit 1"]
        interval    = 30
        timeout     = 5
        retries     = 3
        startPeriod = 60
      }
    }
  ])

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-django-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# CloudWatch Log Group for ECS
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "ecs_django" {
  name              = "/ecs/anihpj-django-${var.environment}"
  retention_in_days = 14

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-ecs-logs-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# ECS Service
resource "aws_ecs_service" "django" {
  name            = "anihpj-django-${var.environment}"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.django.arn
  desired_count   = var.environment == "production" ? 2 : 1
  launch_type     = "FARGATE"

  network_configuration {
    subnets          = aws_subnet.private[*].id
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.ecs_tasks.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }

  load_balancer {
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.ecs.arn
    container_name   = "django"
    container_port   = 8000
  }

  deployment_configuration {
    maximum_percent         = 200
    minimum_healthy_percent = 100
  }

  depends_on = [aws_lb_listener.https]

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-ecs-service-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Auto Scaling
resource "aws_appautoscaling_target" "ecs" {
  max_capacity       = 10
  min_capacity       = 1
  resource_id        = "service/${aws_ecs_cluster.main.name}/${aws_ecs_service.django.name}"
  scalable_dimension = "ecs:service:DesiredCount"
  service_namespace  = "ecs"
}

resource "aws_appautoscaling_policy" "ecs_cpu" {
  name               = "anihpj-cpu-autoscaling-${var.environment}"
  policy_type        = "TargetTrackingScaling"
  resource_id        = aws_appautoscaling_target.ecs.resource_id
  scalable_dimension = aws_appautoscaling_target.ecs.scalable_dimension
  service_namespace  = aws_appautoscaling_target.ecs.service_namespace

  target_tracking_scaling_policy_configuration {
    target_value = 70.0

    predefined_metric_specification {
      predefined_metric_type = "ECSServiceAverageCPUUtilization"
    }

    scale_in_cooldown  = 300
    scale_out_cooldown = 60
  }
}

# Security Group for ECS Tasks
resource "aws_security_group" "ecs_tasks" {
  name        = "anihpj-ecs-tasks-${var.environment}"
  description = "Security group for ECS tasks"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id

  ingress {
    from_port       = 8000
    to_port         = 8000
    protocol        = "tcp"
    security_groups = [aws_security_group.alb.id]
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-ecs-tasks-${var.environment}"
  }
}
🐍
Dockerfile for Django on ECS
# Dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim

WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    postgresql-client \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Python dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt gunicorn

# Copy application code
COPY . .

# Collect static files
RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

# Health check endpoint
EXPOSE 8000

# Run with Gunicorn
CMD ["gunicorn", "anihpj.wsgi:application", \
     "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", \
     "--workers", "4", \
     "--timeout", "120"]
💡
ECS Best Practices
  • Use FARGATE_SPOT for non-critical workloads (70% cost savings)
  • Enable Container Insights for detailed monitoring
  • Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager, reference in task definition
  • Use multi-stage Docker builds to minimize image size
  • Implement health checks for zero-downtime deployments

3.16 EKS & Kubernetes

Deploy and manage Kubernetes clusters on AWS with EKS - perfect for scaling Django microservices.

eks.tf - EKS Cluster Configuration 
# =========================================
# EKS Configuration for anihpj
# =========================================

# EKS Cluster IAM Role
resource "aws_iam_role" "eks_cluster" {
  name = "anihpj-eks-cluster-${var.environment}"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "eks.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "eks_cluster_policy" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.eks_cluster.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEKSClusterPolicy"
}

# EKS Cluster
resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" {
  name     = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  role_arn = aws_iam_role.eks_cluster.arn
  version  = "1.28"

  vpc_config {
    subnet_ids              = concat(aws_subnet.private[*].id, aws_subnet.public[*].id)
    endpoint_private_access = true
    endpoint_public_access  = true
    public_access_cidrs     = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
    security_group_ids      = [aws_security_group.eks_cluster.id]
  }

  enabled_cluster_log_types = ["api", "audit", "authenticator", "controllerManager", "scheduler"]

  depends_on = [aws_iam_role_policy_attachment.eks_cluster_policy]

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-eks-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# OIDC Provider for IRSA (IAM Roles for Service Accounts)
resource "aws_iam_openid_connect_provider" "eks" {
  client_id_list  = ["sts.amazonaws.com"]
  thumbprint_list = [data.tls_certificate.eks.certificates[0].sha1_fingerprint]
  url             = aws_eks_cluster.main.identity[0].oidc[0].issuer
}

data "tls_certificate" "eks" {
  url = aws_eks_cluster.main.identity[0].oidc[0].issuer
}

# Node Group IAM Role
resource "aws_iam_role" "eks_node_group" {
  name = "anihpj-eks-node-group-${var.environment}"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "ec2.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "eks_worker_node_policy" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.eks_node_group.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEKSWorkerNodePolicy"
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "eks_cni_policy" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.eks_node_group.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEKS_CNI_Policy"
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "eks_container_registry_policy" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.eks_node_group.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly"
}

# EKS Node Group
resource "aws_eks_node_group" "main" {
  cluster_name    = aws_eks_cluster.main.name
  node_group_name = "anihpj-nodes-${var.environment}"
  node_role_arn   = aws_iam_role.eks_node_group.arn
  subnet_ids      = aws_subnet.private[*].id

  scaling_config {
    desired_size = var.environment == "production" ? 3 : 2
    max_size     = 10
    min_size     = 1
  }

  instance_types = ["t3.medium"]
  capacity_type  = "ON_DEMAND"
  disk_size      = 20

  update_config {
    max_unavailable = 1
  }

  labels = {
    Environment = var.environment
    Application = "anihpj"
  }

  depends_on = [
    aws_iam_role_policy_attachment.eks_worker_node_policy,
    aws_iam_role_policy_attachment.eks_cni_policy,
    aws_iam_role_policy_attachment.eks_container_registry_policy,
  ]

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-node-group-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Fargate Profile (Optional - for serverless pods)
resource "aws_eks_fargate_profile" "app" {
  cluster_name           = aws_eks_cluster.main.name
  fargate_profile_name   = "anihpj-fargate-${var.environment}"
  pod_execution_role_arn = aws_iam_role.eks_fargate.arn
  subnet_ids             = aws_subnet.private[*].id

  selector {
    namespace = "anihpj"
    labels = {
      fargate = "true"
    }
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-fargate-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Fargate IAM Role
resource "aws_iam_role" "eks_fargate" {
  name = "anihpj-eks-fargate-${var.environment}"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "eks-fargate-pods.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "eks_fargate_policy" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.eks_fargate.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEKSFargatePodExecutionRolePolicy"
}

# EKS Add-ons
resource "aws_eks_addon" "vpc_cni" {
  cluster_name = aws_eks_cluster.main.name
  addon_name   = "vpc-cni"
  addon_version = "v1.15.1-eksbuild.1"
}

resource "aws_eks_addon" "coredns" {
  cluster_name = aws_eks_cluster.main.name
  addon_name   = "coredns"
  addon_version = "v1.10.1-eksbuild.2"

  depends_on = [aws_eks_node_group.main]
}

resource "aws_eks_addon" "kube_proxy" {
  cluster_name = aws_eks_cluster.main.name
  addon_name   = "kube-proxy"
  addon_version = "v1.28.1-eksbuild.1"
}

# Security Group for EKS Cluster
resource "aws_security_group" "eks_cluster" {
  name        = "anihpj-eks-cluster-${var.environment}"
  description = "Security group for EKS cluster"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-eks-cluster-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Outputs
output "eks_cluster_endpoint" {
  description = "EKS cluster endpoint"
  value       = aws_eks_cluster.main.endpoint
}

output "eks_cluster_name" {
  description = "EKS cluster name"
  value       = aws_eks_cluster.main.name
}

output "eks_cluster_certificate_authority" {
  description = "EKS cluster certificate authority data"
  value       = aws_eks_cluster.main.certificate_authority[0].data
  sensitive   = true
}
🐍
Kubernetes Manifests for Django
# django-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-django
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-django
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-django
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: anihpj-sa
      containers:
      - name: django
        image: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DB_HOST
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: db-credentials
              key: host
        - name: DB_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: db-credentials
              key: password
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-django
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  selector:
    app: anihpj-django
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
---
# IRSA Service Account
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: anihpj-sa
  namespace: anihpj
  annotations:
    eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-eks-pod-role
💡
EKS Best Practices
  • Use IRSA (IAM Roles for Service Accounts) instead of node roles
  • Enable cluster logging for audit trails
  • Use Fargate for serverless workloads (no node management)
  • Implement pod security standards (PSS)
  • Use AWS Load Balancer Controller for ALB integration
  • Configure cluster autoscaler for dynamic scaling
  • Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with External Secrets Operator
Feature ECS Fargate EKS
Orchestration AWS proprietary Kubernetes (open standard)
Complexity Lower Higher (more control)
Portability AWS-only Multi-cloud ready
Ecosystem Limited to AWS Vast K8s ecosystem
Use Case Simple containerized apps Complex microservices

3.17 Secrets Manager & SSM Parameter Store

Securely manage secrets, database credentials, and configuration parameters for Django applications.

secrets.tf - Secrets Manager Configuration 
# =========================================
# Secrets Manager for anihpj
# =========================================

# Django Secret Key
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "django_secret_key" {
  name                    = "anihpj/${var.environment}/django/secret-key"
  description             = "Django SECRET_KEY"
  recovery_window_in_days = 7

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-django-secret-key"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "django_secret_key" {
  secret_id     = aws_secretsmanager_secret.django_secret_key.id
  secret_string = random_password.django_secret_key.result
}

resource "random_password" "django_secret_key" {
  length  = 50
  special = true
}

# Database Credentials
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_credentials" {
  name                    = "anihpj/${var.environment}/rds/credentials"
  description             = "RDS PostgreSQL credentials"
  recovery_window_in_days = 7

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-db-credentials"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_credentials" {
  secret_id = aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_credentials.id
  secret_string = jsonencode({
    username = aws_db_instance.main.username
    password = aws_db_instance.main.password
    engine   = "postgres"
    host     = aws_db_instance.main.endpoint
    port     = 5432
    dbname   = aws_db_instance.main.db_name
  })
}

# Secret Rotation Configuration
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation" "db" {
  secret_id           = aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_credentials.id
  rotation_lambda_arn = aws_lambda_function.rotate_secret.arn

  rotation_rules {
    automatically_after_days = 30
  }
}

# Lambda for Secret Rotation
resource "aws_lambda_function" "rotate_secret" {
  filename      = "lambda_rotation.zip"
  function_name = "anihpj-secret-rotation-${var.environment}"
  role          = aws_iam_role.lambda_rotation.arn
  handler       = "index.handler"
  runtime       = "python3.11"

  environment {
    variables = {
      SECRETS_MANAGER_ENDPOINT = "https://secretsmanager.${var.aws_region}.amazonaws.com"
    }
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-secret-rotation"
  }
}

# IAM Role for Lambda Rotation
resource "aws_iam_role" "lambda_rotation" {
  name = "anihpj-lambda-rotation-${var.environment}"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "lambda.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "lambda_rotation" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.lambda_rotation.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaVPCAccessExecutionRole"
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "lambda_rotation_secrets" {
  name = "secrets-access"
  role = aws_iam_role.lambda_rotation.id

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = [
          "secretsmanager:DescribeSecret",
          "secretsmanager:GetSecretValue",
          "secretsmanager:PutSecretValue",
          "secretsmanager:UpdateSecretVersionStage"
        ]
        Resource = aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_credentials.arn
      },
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = [
          "rds:DescribeDBInstances",
          "rds:ModifyDBInstance"
        ]
        Resource = aws_db_instance.main.arn
      }
    ]
  })
}
ssm.tf - Parameter Store Configuration 
# =========================================
# SSM Parameter Store for anihpj
# =========================================

# Non-sensitive Configuration Parameters
resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "debug_mode" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/config/DEBUG"
  description = "Django DEBUG setting"
  type        = "String"
  value       = var.environment == "production" ? "False" : "True"

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-debug"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "allowed_hosts" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/config/ALLOWED_HOSTS"
  description = "Django ALLOWED_HOSTS"
  type        = "StringList"
  value       = join(",", [aws_lb.main.dns_name, "anihpj.com", "*.anihpj.com"])

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-allowed-hosts"
  }
}

# Secure Parameters with KMS Encryption
resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "email_password" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/email/password"
  description = "Email service password"
  type        = "SecureString"
  value       = var.email_password
  key_id      = aws_kms_key.ssm.id

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-email-password"
  }
}

resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "api_keys" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/integrations/api-keys"
  description = "Third-party API keys"
  type        = "SecureString"
  value = jsonencode({
    stripe_key    = var.stripe_api_key
    sendgrid_key  = var.sendgrid_api_key
    analytics_key = var.analytics_key
  })
  key_id = aws_kms_key.ssm.id

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-api-keys"
  }
}

# Parameter Hierarchy
resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "celery_broker" {
  name  = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/celery/broker-url"
  type  = "String"
  value = "redis://${aws_elasticache_cluster.main.cache_nodes[0].address}:6379/0"
}

resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "celery_backend" {
  name  = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/celery/result-backend"
  type  = "String"
  value = "redis://${aws_elasticache_cluster.main.cache_nodes[0].address}:6379/1"
}

resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "s3_bucket" {
  name  = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/storage/s3-bucket"
  type  = "String"
  value = aws_s3_bucket.media.bucket
}

# KMS Key for Parameter Store Encryption
resource "aws_kms_key" "ssm" {
  description             = "KMS key for SSM Parameter Store"
  deletion_window_in_days = 10
  enable_key_rotation     = true

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-ssm-key"
  }
}

resource "aws_kms_alias" "ssm" {
  name          = "alias/anihpj-ssm-${var.environment}"
  target_key_id = aws_kms_key.ssm.key_id
}

# IAM Policy for EC2/ECS to Access Parameters
resource "aws_iam_policy" "ssm_access" {
  name        = "anihpj-ssm-access-${var.environment}"
  description = "Allow access to SSM parameters"

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = [
          "ssm:GetParameter",
          "ssm:GetParameters",
          "ssm:GetParametersByPath"
        ]
        Resource = "arn:aws:ssm:${var.aws_region}:${data.aws_caller_identity.current.account_id}:parameter/anihpj/${var.environment}/*"
      },
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = [
          "kms:Decrypt"
        ]
        Resource = aws_kms_key.ssm.arn
      }
    ]
  })
}

# Attach to EC2 Role
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "ec2_ssm" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.ec2.name
  policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.ssm_access.arn
}

# Outputs
output "ssm_parameter_prefix" {
  description = "SSM parameter prefix"
  value       = "/anihpj/${var.environment}"
}
🐍
Django Configuration with Secrets Manager & SSM
# settings.py
import boto3
import json

# Initialize AWS clients
secrets_client = boto3.client('secretsmanager', region_name='us-east-1')
ssm_client = boto3.client('ssm', region_name='us-east-1')

def get_secret(secret_name):
    """Retrieve secret from Secrets Manager"""
    try:
        response = secrets_client.get_secret_value(SecretId=secret_name)
        return json.loads(response['SecretString'])
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error retrieving secret: {e}")
        return None

def get_parameter(parameter_name):
    """Retrieve parameter from SSM Parameter Store"""
    try:
        response = ssm_client.get_parameter(
            Name=parameter_name,
            WithDecryption=True
        )
        return response['Parameter']['Value']
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error retrieving parameter: {e}")
        return None

def get_parameters_by_path(path):
    """Retrieve multiple parameters by path"""
    try:
        response = ssm_client.get_parameters_by_path(
            Path=path,
            Recursive=True,
            WithDecryption=True
        )
        return {p['Name'].split('/')[-1]: p['Value'] for p in response['Parameters']}
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error retrieving parameters: {e}")
        return {}

# Environment
ENVIRONMENT = os.environ.get('ENVIRONMENT', 'development')

# Get Django secret key from Secrets Manager
secret_key_data = get_secret(f'anihpj/{ENVIRONMENT}/django/secret-key')
SECRET_KEY = secret_key_data if secret_key_data else 'fallback-insecure-key'

# Get database credentials
db_credentials = get_secret(f'anihpj/{ENVIRONMENT}/rds/credentials')
if db_credentials:
    DATABASES = {
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',
            'NAME': db_credentials['dbname'],
            'USER': db_credentials['username'],
            'PASSWORD': db_credentials['password'],
            'HOST': db_credentials['host'].split(':')[0],
            'PORT': db_credentials['port'],
        }
    }

# Get configuration from SSM
DEBUG = get_parameter(f'/anihpj/{ENVIRONMENT}/config/DEBUG') == 'True'
ALLOWED_HOSTS = get_parameter(f'/anihpj/{ENVIRONMENT}/config/ALLOWED_HOSTS').split(',')

# Get API keys
api_keys = json.loads(get_parameter(f'/anihpj/{ENVIRONMENT}/integrations/api-keys'))
STRIPE_API_KEY = api_keys.get('stripe_key')
SENDGRID_API_KEY = api_keys.get('sendgrid_key')

# Get all Celery configurations
celery_config = get_parameters_by_path(f'/anihpj/{ENVIRONMENT}/celery')
CELERY_BROKER_URL = celery_config.get('broker-url')
CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND = celery_config.get('result-backend')

# Get storage configuration
AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = get_parameter(f'/anihpj/{ENVIRONMENT}/storage/s3-bucket')
💡
Secrets Manager vs SSM Parameter Store
Feature Secrets Manager SSM Parameter Store
Automatic Rotation ✅ Built-in ❌ Manual
Cost $0.40/secret/month Free (standard), $0.05/advanced
Use Case Database credentials, API keys Configuration values, feature flags
Size Limit 64 KB 8 KB (standard), 8 KB (advanced)
⚠️
Security Best Practices
  • Never hardcode secrets in Terraform files - use sensitive = true for outputs
  • Enable automatic rotation for database credentials (30-90 days)
  • Use IAM policies with least privilege (specific parameter paths only)
  • Enable KMS encryption for SSM SecureString parameters
  • Implement secret versioning and rollback strategies
  • Monitor secret access with CloudTrail
  • Use VPC endpoints for private secret access (no internet required)

3.18 Additional AWS Services

Brief overview of additional AWS services useful for Django applications - DynamoDB, EventBridge, Systems Manager, WAF, Cognito, API Gateway, Step Functions, Kinesis, Athena, and Glue.

DynamoDB (NoSQL Database)

dynamodb.tf - NoSQL Database 
# DynamoDB Table for Session Storage
resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "sessions" {
  name           = "anihpj-sessions-${var.environment}"
  billing_mode   = "PAY_PER_REQUEST"
  hash_key       = "session_id"
  
  attribute {
    name = "session_id"
    type = "S"
  }

  ttl {
    attribute_name = "expiration_time"
    enabled        = true
  }

  point_in_time_recovery {
    enabled = true
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-sessions-${var.environment}"
  }
}

EventBridge (Event Bus)

eventbridge.tf - Event-Driven Architecture 
# Custom Event Bus
resource "aws_cloudwatch_event_bus" "anihpj" {
  name = "anihpj-events-${var.environment}"
}

# Rule for Job Applications
resource "aws_cloudwatch_event_rule" "job_application" {
  name           = "anihpj-job-application-${var.environment}"
  event_bus_name = aws_cloudwatch_event_bus.anihpj.name

  event_pattern = jsonencode({
    source      = ["anihpj.jobpost"]
    detail-type = ["Application Received"]
  })
}

resource "aws_cloudwatch_event_target" "notify_lambda" {
  rule           = aws_cloudwatch_event_rule.job_application.name
  event_bus_name = aws_cloudwatch_event_bus.anihpj.name
  arn            = aws_lambda_function.notify_recruiter.arn
}

Systems Manager (Parameter Store)

ssm.tf - Configuration Management 
# SSM Parameters for Django Config
resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "django_settings" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/django/settings"
  description = "Django settings module"
  type        = "String"
  value       = "anihpj.settings.${var.environment}"

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-settings-${var.environment}"
  }
}

resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "api_key" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/api/third-party-key"
  description = "Third-party API key"
  type        = "SecureString"
  value       = var.third_party_api_key

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-api-key-${var.environment}"
  }
}

WAF (Web Application Firewall)

waf.tf - Application Security 
# WAF Web ACL
resource "aws_wafv2_web_acl" "main" {
  name  = "anihpj-waf-${var.environment}"
  scope = "REGIONAL"

  default_action {
    allow {}
  }

  # Rate Limiting Rule
  rule {
    name     = "RateLimitRule"
    priority = 1

    action {
      block {}
    }

    statement {
      rate_based_statement {
        limit              = 2000
        aggregate_key_type = "IP"
      }
    }

    visibility_config {
      cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
      metric_name                = "RateLimitRule"
      sampled_requests_enabled   = true
    }
  }

  # AWS Managed Rules - Core Rule Set
  rule {
    name     = "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet"
    priority = 2

    override_action {
      none {}
    }

    statement {
      managed_rule_group_statement {
        name        = "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet"
        vendor_name = "AWS"
      }
    }

    visibility_config {
      cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
      metric_name                = "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet"
      sampled_requests_enabled   = true
    }
  }

  visibility_config {
    cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
    metric_name                = "anihpj-waf-${var.environment}"
    sampled_requests_enabled   = true
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-waf-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Associate WAF with ALB
resource "aws_wafv2_web_acl_association" "alb" {
  resource_arn = aws_lb.main.arn
  web_acl_arn  = aws_wafv2_web_acl.main.arn
}

Cognito (User Authentication)

cognito.tf - User Management 
# Cognito User Pool
resource "aws_cognito_user_pool" "main" {
  name = "anihpj-users-${var.environment}"

  username_attributes      = ["email"]
  auto_verified_attributes = ["email"]

  password_policy {
    minimum_length    = 8
    require_lowercase = true
    require_numbers   = true
    require_symbols   = true
    require_uppercase = true
  }

  schema {
    name                = "email"
    attribute_data_type = "String"
    required            = true
    mutable             = true
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-cognito-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Cognito User Pool Client
resource "aws_cognito_user_pool_client" "web" {
  name         = "anihpj-web-client-${var.environment}"
  user_pool_id = aws_cognito_user_pool.main.id

  explicit_auth_flows = [
    "ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH",
    "ALLOW_REFRESH_TOKEN_AUTH"
  ]

  prevent_user_existence_errors = "ENABLED"
}

API Gateway (REST API)

api-gateway.tf - API Management 
# API Gateway REST API
resource "aws_api_gateway_rest_api" "main" {
  name        = "anihpj-api-${var.environment}"
  description = "Anihpj API Gateway"

  endpoint_configuration {
    types = ["REGIONAL"]
  }
}

# API Resource
resource "aws_api_gateway_resource" "jobs" {
  rest_api_id = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.main.id
  parent_id   = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.main.root_resource_id
  path_part   = "jobs"
}

# API Method
resource "aws_api_gateway_method" "get_jobs" {
  rest_api_id   = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.main.id
  resource_id   = aws_api_gateway_resource.jobs.id
  http_method   = "GET"
  authorization = "NONE"
}

# Lambda Integration
resource "aws_api_gateway_integration" "lambda" {
  rest_api_id = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.main.id
  resource_id = aws_api_gateway_resource.jobs.id
  http_method = aws_api_gateway_method.get_jobs.http_method

  integration_http_method = "POST"
  type                    = "AWS_PROXY"
  uri                     = aws_lambda_function.api_handler.invoke_arn
}

Step Functions (Workflow Orchestration)

step-functions.tf - State Machine 
# Step Functions State Machine
resource "aws_sfn_state_machine" "job_processing" {
  name     = "anihpj-job-processing-${var.environment}"
  role_arn = aws_iam_role.step_functions.arn

  definition = jsonencode({
    Comment = "Job application processing workflow"
    StartAt = "ValidateApplication"
    States = {
      ValidateApplication = {
        Type     = "Task"
        Resource = aws_lambda_function.validate_app.arn
        Next     = "CheckDuplicates"
      }
      CheckDuplicates = {
        Type     = "Task"
        Resource = aws_lambda_function.check_duplicates.arn
        Next     = "SendConfirmation"
      }
      SendConfirmation = {
        Type     = "Task"
        Resource = aws_lambda_function.send_email.arn
        End      = true
      }
    }
  })
}

Kinesis (Real-Time Streaming)

kinesis.tf - Data Streaming 
# Kinesis Data Stream
resource "aws_kinesis_stream" "events" {
  name             = "anihpj-events-${var.environment}"
  shard_count      = 1
  retention_period = 24

  stream_mode_details {
    stream_mode = "PROVISIONED"
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-kinesis-${var.environment}"
  }
}

# Kinesis Firehose for S3 Delivery
resource "aws_kinesis_firehose_delivery_stream" "s3_delivery" {
  name        = "anihpj-s3-delivery-${var.environment}"
  destination = "extended_s3"

  extended_s3_configuration {
    role_arn   = aws_iam_role.firehose.arn
    bucket_arn = aws_s3_bucket.analytics.arn
    prefix     = "events/"

    cloudwatch_logging_options {
      enabled         = true
      log_group_name  = "/aws/kinesisfirehose/anihpj"
      log_stream_name = "S3Delivery"
    }
  }
}

Athena & Glue (Data Analytics)

athena-glue.tf - Analytics 
# Glue Database
resource "aws_glue_catalog_database" "analytics" {
  name = "anihpj_analytics_${var.environment}"

  description = "Analytics database for anihpj"
}

# Glue Crawler
resource "aws_glue_crawler" "logs" {
  name          = "anihpj-logs-crawler-${var.environment}"
  database_name = aws_glue_catalog_database.analytics.name
  role          = aws_iam_role.glue_crawler.arn

  s3_target {
    path = "s3://${aws_s3_bucket.logs.id}/alb-logs/"
  }

  schedule = "cron(0 1 * * ? *)"  # Daily at 1 AM
}

# Athena Workgroup
resource "aws_athena_workgroup" "main" {
  name = "anihpj-${var.environment}"

  configuration {
    enforce_workgroup_configuration    = true
    publish_cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true

    result_configuration {
      output_location = "s3://${aws_s3_bucket.athena_results.id}/results/"

      encryption_configuration {
        encryption_option = "SSE_S3"
      }
    }
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-athena-${var.environment}"
  }
}
💡
Service Selection Guide
  • DynamoDB: Use for sessions, caching, or high-speed NoSQL needs
  • EventBridge: Decouple microservices with event-driven patterns
  • WAF: Essential for production apps to block common attacks
  • Cognito: Quick user auth without managing your own user database
  • Step Functions: Complex multi-step workflows (better than Celery chains)
  • Athena + Glue: Query S3 logs with SQL without setting up a database

📦 4. Modules Deep Dive

Master modular infrastructure design — Build reusable components, enforce standards, and scale your Terraform codebase efficiently

🎯 Why Modules Matter for Django on AWS

Modules are containers for multiple resources used together. They're the building blocks of scalable, maintainable infrastructure. For the anihpj Django job portal, modules help you package VPC networking, RDS databases, and S3 storage into reusable components that can be shared across dev, staging, and production environments.

✨ The Power of Modules

♻️
Reusability

Write once, use everywhere. Deploy the same VPC across dev, staging, prod with different parameters

🎨
Organization

Logical separation of concerns. VPC modules, RDS modules, S3 modules — each with clear boundaries

📏
Standardization

Enforce best practices. Every RDS instance gets backups, every VPC gets proper subnets

🧪
Testability

Test modules in isolation. Validate VPC module works before using it in production

🤝
Collaboration

Teams can work on different modules. Network team owns VPC, database team owns RDS

⚡
Speed

Spin up complete environments in minutes. One module call creates entire VPC infrastructure

🏗️ Module Architecture for anihpj

🏠
Root Module
main.tf (Your project root)
↓ calls ↓
🌐
VPC Module
Network infrastructure
🗄️
RDS Module
PostgreSQL database
📦
S3 Module
Static & media files
⚖️
ALB Module
Load balancing
⚡
Redis Module
Caching layer
🔐
IAM Module
Security & roles
↓ creates ↓
☁️ AWS Resources
VPCs • Subnets • EC2 Instances • RDS Databases • S3 Buckets
Load Balancers • ElastiCache Clusters • IAM Roles • Security Groups

📊 Module Types Comparison

Module Type Source Use Case Example
Root Module Current directory Main entry point ./main.tf
Local Module Local file path Custom reusable components ./modules/vpc
Registry Module Terraform Registry Community-vetted modules terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws
Git Module Git repository Private company modules git::https://github.com/...
S3 Module S3 bucket Version-controlled releases s3::https://s3.../module.zip
HTTP Module HTTP URL Direct archive downloads https://example.com/module.zip
💡
Module Best Practice

Start with local modules to organize your code, then publish to a private registry for team sharing, and finally contribute public modules to the Terraform Registry to give back to the community.

4.1 Module Basics

📦

What is a Module?

A module is a container for multiple resources used together. Every Terraform configuration has at least one module (root module). Child modules can be called from other modules to create reusable components.

🏠

Root Module

Your main working directory with .tf files

👶

Child Module

Reusable module called from other modules

📂

Local Module

Module in a local directory path

🌐

Remote Module

Module from registry, Git, or S3

Module Structure

Standard Module Layout

modules/ └── vpc/ ├── main.tf # Resource definitions ├── variables.tf # Input variables ├── outputs.tf # Output values ├── versions.tf # Provider requirements ├── README.md # Documentation └── examples/ # Usage examples └── basic/ └── main.tf
Calling a Module 
# Local module
module "vpc" {
  source = "./modules/vpc"

  vpc_cidr           = "10.0.0.0/16"
  environment        = var.environment
  availability_zones = var.availability_zones
}

# Terraform Registry module
module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "5.4.0"

  name = "anihpj-vpc"
  cidr = "10.0.0.0/16"
  
  azs             = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"]
  private_subnets = ["10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.2.0/24"]
  public_subnets  = ["10.0.101.0/24", "10.0.102.0/24"]
}

# Git module
module "rds" {
  source = "git::https://github.com/company/terraform-modules.git//rds?ref=v1.2.0"
  
  db_name     = "anihpj"
  environment = var.environment
}

# S3 module
module "lambda" {
  source = "s3::https://s3-us-east-1.amazonaws.com/my-modules/lambda.zip"
}

🔄 Module Lifecycle

1
📝 Design

Define module purpose, inputs, outputs, and resources. Plan for reusability.

2
💻 Develop

Create main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf. Add validation and documentation.

3
🧪 Test

Write Terratest unit tests. Deploy to test environment and validate behavior.

4
📦 Publish

Tag version, push to Git, or publish to private/public Terraform Registry.

5
🔄 Maintain

Update for new features, fix bugs, manage versions. Monitor usage across teams.

📍 Module Source Types

FASTEST
📂
Local Path
./modules/vpc

✅ Fastest development
✅ Easy debugging
⚠️ Not shareable outside repo

PRIVATE
🔗
Git Repository
git::https://github.com/...

✅ Version control
✅ Private modules
✅ Team sharing

VERIFIED
🌐
Terraform Registry
terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws

✅ Production-ready
✅ Community support
✅ Best practices

ENTERPRISE
☁️
S3 Bucket
s3::https://s3.../module.zip

✅ Enterprise control
✅ Access policies
✅ Versioned releases

🔖 Module Versioning Strategy

Semantic Versioning (SemVer)
1
MAJOR
Breaking changes
.
2
MINOR
New features
.
3
PATCH
Bug fixes
✅ Do This
  • Use ~> for safe updates
  • Tag Git releases (v1.2.3)
  • Document CHANGELOG.md
  • Test before releasing
❌ Avoid This
  • No version constraints
  • Breaking changes in patches
  • Reusing version tags
  • Skipping testing

4.2 Creating Custom Modules

🎨 Module Design Principles

🎯
Single Responsibility

One module = one purpose. VPC module only handles networking.

🔌
Composability

Modules should work together like LEGO blocks.

🔒
Encapsulation

Hide complexity. Only expose what's needed via outputs.

⚙️
Configurability

Provide variables for customization with sensible defaults.

📖
Documentation

Clear README with examples and input/output descriptions.

✅
Validation

Validate inputs to fail fast with clear error messages.

Build a reusable VPC module for the anihpj Job Portal:

modules/vpc/variables.tf 
# =========================================
# VPC Module - Input Variables
# =========================================

variable "project_name" {
  description = "Project name for resource naming"
  type        = string
}

variable "environment" {
  description = "Environment (dev, staging, production)"
  type        = string

  validation {
    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "production"], var.environment)
    error_message = "Environment must be dev, staging, or production."
  }
}

variable "vpc_cidr" {
  description = "CIDR block for VPC"
  type        = string
  default     = "10.0.0.0/16"

  validation {
    condition     = can(cidrhost(var.vpc_cidr, 0))
    error_message = "Must be a valid CIDR block."
  }
}

variable "availability_zones" {
  description = "List of availability zones"
  type        = list(string)
}

variable "enable_nat_gateway" {
  description = "Enable NAT Gateway for private subnets"
  type        = bool
  default     = true
}

variable "single_nat_gateway" {
  description = "Use single NAT Gateway (cost savings for non-prod)"
  type        = bool
  default     = false
}

variable "tags" {
  description = "Additional tags for all resources"
  type        = map(string)
  default     = {}
}
modules/vpc/main.tf 
# =========================================
# VPC Module - Main Resources
# =========================================

locals {
  name_prefix = "${var.project_name}-${var.environment}"
  
  common_tags = merge(var.tags, {
    Module      = "vpc"
    Project     = var.project_name
    Environment = var.environment
    ManagedBy   = "Terraform"
  })
  
  nat_gateway_count = var.enable_nat_gateway ? (
    var.single_nat_gateway ? 1 : length(var.availability_zones)
  ) : 0
}

# VPC
resource "aws_vpc" "main" {
  cidr_block           = var.vpc_cidr
  enable_dns_hostnames = true
  enable_dns_support   = true

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-vpc"
  })
}

# Internet Gateway
resource "aws_internet_gateway" "main" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-igw"
  })
}

# Public Subnets
resource "aws_subnet" "public" {
  count = length(var.availability_zones)

  vpc_id                  = aws_vpc.main.id
  cidr_block              = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, count.index)
  availability_zone       = var.availability_zones[count.index]
  map_public_ip_on_launch = true

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-public-${count.index + 1}"
    Type = "Public"
  })
}

# Private Subnets
resource "aws_subnet" "private" {
  count = length(var.availability_zones)

  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.main.id
  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, count.index + 10)
  availability_zone = var.availability_zones[count.index]

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-private-${count.index + 1}"
    Type = "Private"
  })
}

# Database Subnets
resource "aws_subnet" "database" {
  count = length(var.availability_zones)

  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.main.id
  cidr_block        = cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, count.index + 20)
  availability_zone = var.availability_zones[count.index]

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-database-${count.index + 1}"
    Type = "Database"
  })
}

# Elastic IPs for NAT
resource "aws_eip" "nat" {
  count  = local.nat_gateway_count
  domain = "vpc"

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-nat-eip-${count.index + 1}"
  })

  depends_on = [aws_internet_gateway.main]
}

# NAT Gateways
resource "aws_nat_gateway" "main" {
  count = local.nat_gateway_count

  allocation_id = aws_eip.nat[count.index].id
  subnet_id     = aws_subnet.public[count.index].id

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-nat-${count.index + 1}"
  })
}

# Route Tables
resource "aws_route_table" "public" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id

  route {
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    gateway_id = aws_internet_gateway.main.id
  }

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-public-rt"
  })
}

resource "aws_route_table" "private" {
  count  = local.nat_gateway_count > 0 ? length(var.availability_zones) : 0
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id

  route {
    cidr_block     = "0.0.0.0/0"
    nat_gateway_id = aws_nat_gateway.main[var.single_nat_gateway ? 0 : count.index].id
  }

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-private-rt-${count.index + 1}"
  })
}

# Route Table Associations
resource "aws_route_table_association" "public" {
  count          = length(var.availability_zones)
  subnet_id      = aws_subnet.public[count.index].id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.public.id
}

resource "aws_route_table_association" "private" {
  count          = local.nat_gateway_count > 0 ? length(var.availability_zones) : 0
  subnet_id      = aws_subnet.private[count.index].id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.private[count.index].id
}

# DB Subnet Group
resource "aws_db_subnet_group" "main" {
  name       = "${local.name_prefix}-db-subnet-group"
  subnet_ids = aws_subnet.database[*].id

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-db-subnet-group"
  })
}
modules/vpc/outputs.tf 
# =========================================
# VPC Module - Outputs
# =========================================

output "vpc_id" {
  description = "ID of the VPC"
  value       = aws_vpc.main.id
}

output "vpc_cidr" {
  description = "CIDR block of the VPC"
  value       = aws_vpc.main.cidr_block
}

output "public_subnet_ids" {
  description = "IDs of public subnets"
  value       = aws_subnet.public[*].id
}

output "private_subnet_ids" {
  description = "IDs of private subnets"
  value       = aws_subnet.private[*].id
}

output "database_subnet_ids" {
  description = "IDs of database subnets"
  value       = aws_subnet.database[*].id
}

output "db_subnet_group_name" {
  description = "Name of the DB subnet group"
  value       = aws_db_subnet_group.main.name
}

output "nat_gateway_ips" {
  description = "Public IPs of NAT Gateways"
  value       = aws_eip.nat[*].public_ip
}

output "availability_zones" {
  description = "Availability zones used"
  value       = var.availability_zones
}

4.3 Using Terraform Registry Modules

The Terraform Registry provides verified, production-ready modules maintained by HashiCorp and the community.

🌟 Why Use Registry Modules?

⚡
Instant Setup

Production infrastructure in minutes

🛡️
Battle-Tested

Used by thousands of teams

🔄
Regular Updates

Security patches and features

📚
Great Docs

Examples and best practices

🤝
Community

Issues and pull requests

🏆 Top AWS Registry Modules for Django

🌐
VPC Module
terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws
⭐ 9.3k stars ✓ Verified

Complete VPC with public/private subnets, NAT gateways, and route tables.

  • Multi-AZ support
  • VPN & VPC endpoints
  • Flow logs integration
🗄️
RDS Module
terraform-aws-modules/rds/aws
⭐ 2.1k stars ✓ Verified

PostgreSQL/MySQL RDS with backups, encryption, and monitoring.

  • Read replicas
  • CloudWatch alarms
  • Enhanced monitoring
⚖️
ALB Module
terraform-aws-modules/alb/aws
⭐ 1.8k stars ✓ Verified

Application Load Balancer with SSL, target groups, and rules.

  • HTTP/HTTPS listeners
  • ACM SSL certificates
  • Access logs to S3
🔒
Security Group
terraform-aws-modules/security-group/aws
⭐ 1.5k stars ✓ Verified

Security groups with predefined rules for common services.

  • 80+ predefined rules
  • Computed rules
  • CIDR & SG references
🐳
ECS Module
terraform-aws-modules/ecs/aws
⭐ 2.4k stars ✓ Verified

ECS cluster with Fargate/EC2, services, and task definitions.

  • Fargate & EC2 launch
  • Auto scaling
  • Service discovery
📦
S3 Bucket
terraform-aws-modules/s3-bucket/aws
⭐ 1.9k stars ✓ Verified

S3 bucket with versioning, encryption, and lifecycle policies.

  • CORS & ACL rules
  • CloudFront integration
  • Intelligent tiering
main.tf - Using AWS VPC Module 
# =========================================
# anihpj using Terraform Registry Modules
# =========================================

module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "5.4.0"

  name = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  cidr = "10.0.0.0/16"

  azs              = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]
  private_subnets  = ["10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.2.0/24", "10.0.3.0/24"]
