~2 min
→
🔬
Integration
~15 min
→
🚀
E2E (Nightly)
~30 min
On Every Commit
On PR Merge
Scheduled

📍 In This Section

12.1 Built-in Validation 12.2 Unit Testing 12.3 Terratest 12.4 Contract Testing 12.5 E2E Testing 12.6 Patterns

12.1 Built-in Validation

🔄 Terraform Validation Flow

📝
Write Code
→
✓
validate
→
📏
Variable Rules
→
⚡
Precondition
→
🚀
Apply
→
🏁
Postcondition
✓ All checks pass → Infrastructure deployed successfully
✓

terraform validate

Syntax & configuration check

📏

Variable Validation

Custom validation rules

⚡

Preconditions

Check before resource creation

🏁

Postconditions

Verify after creation

variables.tf - Variable Validation Rules 
# =========================================
# Variable Validation for anihpj
# =========================================

variable "environment" {
  description = "Deployment environment"
  type        = string

  validation {
    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "production"], var.environment)
    error_message = "Environment must be dev, staging, or production."
  }
}

variable "instance_type" {
  description = "EC2 instance type for Django app"
  type        = string
  default     = "t3.medium"

  validation {
    condition     = can(regex("^t3\\.(micro|small|medium|large)$", var.instance_type))
    error_message = "Instance type must be t3.micro, t3.small, t3.medium, or t3.large."
  }
}

variable "vpc_cidr" {
  description = "CIDR block for VPC"
  type        = string

  validation {
    condition     = can(cidrhost(var.vpc_cidr, 0))
    error_message = "VPC CIDR must be a valid IPv4 CIDR block."
  }

  validation {
    condition     = tonumber(split("/", var.vpc_cidr)[1]) >= 16 && tonumber(split("/", var.vpc_cidr)[1]) <= 24
    error_message = "VPC CIDR prefix must be between /16 and /24."
  }
}

variable "db_password" {
  description = "Database password"
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true

  validation {
    condition     = length(var.db_password) >= 16
    error_message = "Database password must be at least 16 characters."
  }

  validation {
    condition     = can(regex("[A-Z]", var.db_password)) && can(regex("[a-z]", var.db_password)) && can(regex("[0-9]", var.db_password))
    error_message = "Password must contain uppercase, lowercase, and numbers."
  }
}
main.tf - Preconditions & Postconditions 
# =========================================
# Preconditions & Postconditions
# =========================================

resource "aws_instance" "django_app" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id
  instance_type = var.instance_type
  subnet_id     = aws_subnet.private[0].id

  # Precondition: Check BEFORE creation
  lifecycle {
    precondition {
      condition     = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.architecture == "x86_64"
      error_message = "AMI must be x86_64 architecture for Django app."
    }

    precondition {
      condition     = var.environment != "production" || var.instance_type != "t3.micro"
      error_message = "Production environment cannot use t3.micro instances."
    }
  }

  tags = local.common_tags
}

# Data source with postcondition
data "aws_ami" "ubuntu" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["099720109477"]  # Canonical

  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["ubuntu/images/hvm-ssd/ubuntu-jammy-22.04-amd64-server-*"]
  }

  # Postcondition: Verify AFTER data is fetched
  lifecycle {
    postcondition {
      condition     = self.root_device_type == "ebs"
      error_message = "AMI must use EBS root device."
    }
  }
}

# Output with precondition
output "db_endpoint" {
  value = aws_db_instance.postgres.endpoint

  precondition {
    condition     = aws_db_instance.postgres.status == "available"
    error_message = "Database must be available before exposing endpoint."
  }
}

📚 Common Validation Patterns Reference

String Patterns
can(regex("^[a-z]+$", var.name))
Lowercase letters only
List Contains
contains(["a","b"], var.x)
Value in allowed list
Number Range
var.port >= 1 && var.port <= 65535
Valid port range
CIDR Validation
can(cidrhost(var.cidr, 0))
Valid CIDR block
Length Check
length(var.list) > 0
Non-empty list
ARN Format
can(regex("^arn:aws:", var.arn))
Valid AWS ARN

💡 Pro Tips for Validation

✅ Use Multiple Validations
Combine validations for comprehensive checks. Each gets its own error message.
🔒 Validate Sensitive Vars
Check password complexity without exposing values in error messages.
⚡ Fail Fast
Preconditions catch issues before expensive operations run.
🎯 Clear Error Messages
Include valid options in error messages to guide users.

12.2 Unit Testing

🧪

Terraform Test Framework (1.6+)

Native testing with .tftest.hcl files

📄 .tftest.hcl
🎭 Mock Providers
✅ Assertions
📊 Plan/Apply
📁 Test File Structure
📂 terraform-project/
├── 📄 main.tf
├── 📄 variables.tf
├── 📄 outputs.tf
└── 📂 tests/
├── 📄 vpc.tftest.hcl
├── 📄 rds.tftest.hcl
├── 📄 ecs.tftest.hcl
└── 📂 fixtures/
└── 📄 common.tfvars
⚙️ Test Block Anatomy
run "test_name" {
command = plan | apply
# plan = no real resources
# apply = real deployment
assert {
condition = expression
error_message = "..."
}
}
tests/vpc.tftest.hcl - Unit Tests for VPC Module 
# =========================================
# Unit Tests for anihpj VPC Module
# =========================================

# Test variables
variables {
  project_name       = "test-anihpj"
  environment        = "test"
  vpc_cidr           = "10.0.0.0/16"
  availability_zones = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"]
}

# Test 1: VPC Creation
run "vpc_is_created_correctly" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = aws_vpc.main.cidr_block == "10.0.0.0/16"
    error_message = "VPC CIDR block should be 10.0.0.0/16"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_vpc.main.enable_dns_hostnames == true
    error_message = "DNS hostnames should be enabled"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_vpc.main.enable_dns_support == true
    error_message = "DNS support should be enabled"
  }
}

# Test 2: Subnet Creation
run "subnets_are_created" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = length(aws_subnet.public) == 2
    error_message = "Should create 2 public subnets"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = length(aws_subnet.private) == 2
    error_message = "Should create 2 private subnets"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = alltrue([for s in aws_subnet.public : s.map_public_ip_on_launch == true])
    error_message = "Public subnets should auto-assign public IPs"
  }
}

# Test 3: Tags are applied
run "tags_are_applied" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = aws_vpc.main.tags["Project"] == "test-anihpj"
    error_message = "Project tag should be set"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_vpc.main.tags["Environment"] == "test"
    error_message = "Environment tag should be set"
  }
}
tests/rds.tftest.hcl - Mock Provider Test 
# =========================================
# Mock Provider Tests for RDS
# =========================================

# Mock the AWS provider to avoid real API calls
mock_provider "aws" {
  mock_data "aws_availability_zones" {
    defaults = {
      names = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]
    }
  }

  mock_resource "aws_db_instance" {
    defaults = {
      endpoint = "anihpj-db.123456789.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com"
      port     = 5432
    }
  }
}

variables {
  db_name     = "anihpj_jobs"
  db_username = "anihpj_admin"
  db_password = "TestPassword123!"
}

run "rds_configuration_is_correct" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = aws_db_instance.postgres.engine == "postgres"
    error_message = "Database engine should be PostgreSQL"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_db_instance.postgres.engine_version == "15.4"
    error_message = "PostgreSQL version should be 15.4"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_db_instance.postgres.multi_az == true
    error_message = "Multi-AZ should be enabled for production"
  }
}
Terminal - Running Terraform Tests 
# Run all tests in /tests directory
terraform test

# Run specific test file
terraform test -filter=tests/vpc.tftest.hcl

# Verbose output
terraform test -verbose

# Run with JSON output (for CI/CD)
terraform test -json > test-results.json

# Run tests with specific variables
terraform test -var="environment=staging"
$ terraform test ✓ PASSED
tests/vpc.tftest.hcl...
✓ run "vpc_is_created_correctly" ... pass
✓ run "subnets_are_created" ... pass
✓ run "tags_are_applied" ... pass
tests/rds.tftest.hcl...
✓ run "rds_configuration_is_correct" ... pass
Success! 4 passed, 0 failed.
📋 command = plan
  • No real resources created
  • Fast execution (~seconds)
  • Zero cloud costs
  • Tests planned values
  • Safe for frequent runs
🚀 command = apply
  • Creates real resources
  • Slower (~minutes)
  • Incurs cloud costs
  • Tests actual values
  • Use for integration tests

12.3 Integration Testing with Terratest

🔬

Terratest (Go)

Real infrastructure deployment testing

🚀 Real Deploy
⚡ Parallel
🧹 Cleanup
✅ Assertions

🔄 Terratest Execution Lifecycle

📦
Setup
Options
→
🔧
Init
terraform init
→
🚀
Apply
Create Real
→
✅
Assert
Validate
→
🧹
Destroy
defer cleanup
⚠️ Important: Always use defer terraform.Destroy() to avoid orphaned resources!
test/vpc_test.go - Terratest for anihpj VPC 
// =========================================
// Terratest for anihpj VPC Module
// =========================================

package test

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/gruntwork-io/terratest/modules/terraform"
    "github.com/gruntwork-io/terratest/modules/aws"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
)

func TestVPCModule(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    // Terraform options
    terraformOptions := terraform.WithDefaultRetryableErrors(t, &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../modules/networking",
        
        Vars: map[string]interface{}{
            "project_name":       "test-anihpj",
            "environment":        "test",
            "vpc_cidr":           "10.99.0.0/16",
            "availability_zones": []string{"us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"},
        },

        EnvVars: map[string]string{
            "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION": "us-east-1",
        },
    })

    // Cleanup after test
    defer terraform.Destroy(t, terraformOptions)

    // Deploy infrastructure
    terraform.InitAndApply(t, terraformOptions)

    // Get outputs
    vpcId := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "vpc_id")
    publicSubnetIds := terraform.OutputList(t, terraformOptions, "public_subnet_ids")
    privateSubnetIds := terraform.OutputList(t, terraformOptions, "private_subnet_ids")

    // Assertions
    assert.NotEmpty(t, vpcId, "VPC ID should not be empty")
    assert.Len(t, publicSubnetIds, 2, "Should have 2 public subnets")
    assert.Len(t, privateSubnetIds, 2, "Should have 2 private subnets")

    // Verify VPC exists in AWS
    vpc := aws.GetVpcById(t, vpcId, "us-east-1")
    assert.Equal(t, "10.99.0.0/16", *vpc.CidrBlock)

    // Verify subnets are in correct AZs
    for _, subnetId := range publicSubnetIds {
        subnet := aws.GetSubnetById(t, subnetId, "us-east-1")
        assert.True(t, *subnet.MapPublicIpOnLaunch, "Public subnets should auto-assign IPs")
    }
}
test/rds_test.go - Testing AWS RDS 
// =========================================
// Terratest for anihpj RDS Module
// =========================================

func TestRDSModule(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    terraformOptions := terraform.WithDefaultRetryableErrors(t, &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../modules/database",
        
        Vars: map[string]interface{}{
            "db_name":        "anihpj_test",
            "db_username":    "test_admin",
            "db_password":    "TestPassword123!",
            "instance_class": "db.t3.micro",
            "multi_az":       false,  // Single AZ for tests
        },
    })

    defer terraform.Destroy(t, terraformOptions)
    terraform.InitAndApply(t, terraformOptions)

    // Get the RDS endpoint
    dbEndpoint := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "db_endpoint")
    dbPort := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "db_port")

    // Verify connectivity
    assert.NotEmpty(t, dbEndpoint)
    assert.Equal(t, "5432", dbPort)

    // Test database connection
    host := strings.Split(dbEndpoint, ":")[0]
    connectionString := fmt.Sprintf(
        "host=%s port=%s user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=require",
        host, dbPort, "test_admin", "TestPassword123!", "anihpj_test",
    )
    
    db, err := sql.Open("postgres", connectionString)
    assert.NoError(t, err)
    defer db.Close()
    
    err = db.Ping()
    assert.NoError(t, err, "Should be able to connect to database")
}
Terminal - Running Terratest 
# Initialize Go module
cd test/
go mod init github.com/anihpj/terraform-tests
go mod tidy

# Run all tests
go test -v -timeout 30m

# Run specific test
go test -v -run TestVPCModule -timeout 30m

# Run with parallel limit
go test -v -parallel 2 -timeout 30m

# Generate coverage report
go test -v -coverprofile=coverage.out
go tool cover -html=coverage.out

📚 Terratest Common Helper Functions

Function Description Module
terraform.InitAndApply() Run init + apply terraform
terraform.Output() Get output value terraform
terraform.Destroy() Clean up resources terraform
aws.GetVpcById() Fetch VPC details aws
http_helper.HttpGetWithRetry() HTTP request with retries http_helper
retry.DoWithRetry() Retry any operation retry

12.4 Contract Testing

📜 Module Contract Testing

📥 Inputs
Variables defined
→
⚙️ Module
Black box
→
📤 Outputs
Validated values

🔗 Cross-Module Contract Dependencies

📦 VPC Module
OUTPUTS
• vpc_id
• subnet_ids
• security_group_id
→
Contract
📦 Database Module
INPUTS
• vpc_id ←
• subnet_ids ←
OUTPUTS
• db_endpoint
→
Contract
📦 ECS Module
INPUTS
• vpc_id ←
• db_endpoint ←
Contract tests verify that module interfaces remain stable across changes
tests/contract.tftest.hcl - Module Interface Testing 
# =========================================
# Contract Tests for anihpj Modules
# =========================================

# Test: VPC module outputs correct interface
run "vpc_module_contract" {
  command = plan
  
  module {
    source = "./modules/networking"
  }

  variables {
    project_name = "contract-test"
    environment  = "test"
    vpc_cidr     = "10.0.0.0/16"
  }

  # Output contract assertions
  assert {
    condition     = output.vpc_id != null
    error_message = "Module must output vpc_id"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = output.public_subnet_ids != null
    error_message = "Module must output public_subnet_ids"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = output.private_subnet_ids != null
    error_message = "Module must output private_subnet_ids"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = length(output.public_subnet_ids) > 0
    error_message = "Must have at least one public subnet"
  }
}

# Test: Database module integrates with VPC module
run "cross_module_contract" {
  command = plan

  # Use VPC outputs as database inputs
  assert {
    condition     = can(module.database.db_subnet_group_name)
    error_message = "Database module must create subnet group"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = module.database.db_security_group_id != null
    error_message = "Database module must output security group ID"
  }
}
modules/networking/outputs.tf - Output Validation 
# =========================================
# Output Validation with Preconditions
# =========================================

output "vpc_id" {
  description = "The ID of the VPC"
  value       = aws_vpc.main.id

  precondition {
    condition     = aws_vpc.main.state == "available"
    error_message = "VPC must be available before exposing ID"
  }
}

output "public_subnet_ids" {
  description = "List of public subnet IDs"
  value       = aws_subnet.public[*].id

  precondition {
    condition     = length(aws_subnet.public) >= 2
    error_message = "Must have at least 2 public subnets for HA"
  }
}

output "private_subnet_ids" {
  description = "List of private subnet IDs"
  value       = aws_subnet.private[*].id

  precondition {
    condition     = length(aws_subnet.private) >= 2
    error_message = "Must have at least 2 private subnets for HA"
  }
}

output "nat_gateway_ips" {
  description = "Elastic IPs of NAT Gateways"
  value       = aws_eip.nat[*].public_ip

  precondition {
    condition     = alltrue([for eip in aws_eip.nat : eip.public_ip != ""])
    error_message = "All NAT gateways must have public IPs"
  }
}

12.5 End-to-End Testing

🚀

Full Stack Deploy

Complete infrastructure test

💨

Smoke Tests

Quick sanity checks

📊

Performance

Load & stress testing

🔥

Chaos Engineering

Failure injection

📊 E2E Test Metrics Dashboard

98%
Pass Rate
~25m
Avg Duration
$2.50
Cost/Run
Daily
Run Frequency
✅ Smoke Test Checklist
  • ☑ ALB health check responds 200
  • ☑ Django admin page loads
  • ☑ API endpoints accessible
  • ☑ Database connectivity works
  • ☑ Static files served from S3
🔥 Chaos Test Scenarios
  • ⚡ Instance termination recovery
  • ⚡ RDS Multi-AZ failover
  • ⚡ Network partition simulation
  • ⚡ Memory/CPU stress tests
  • ⚡ DNS failover validation
test/e2e_test.go - Full Stack Deployment Test 
// =========================================
// E2E Test for anihpj Full Stack
// =========================================

func TestFullStackDeployment(t *testing.T) {
    // Don't run in parallel - full stack test
    
    terraformOptions := terraform.WithDefaultRetryableErrors(t, &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../environments/test",
        
        Vars: map[string]interface{}{
            "project_name": "e2e-anihpj",
            "environment":  "test",
        },
    })

    defer terraform.Destroy(t, terraformOptions)
    terraform.InitAndApply(t, terraformOptions)

    // Get ALB URL
    albUrl := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "alb_dns_name")
    
    // =========================================
    // Smoke Tests
    // =========================================
    
    // Test 1: ALB responds
    http_helper.HttpGetWithRetry(t, 
        fmt.Sprintf("http://%s/health", albUrl),
        nil, 200, "OK", 30, 10*time.Second,
    )

    // Test 2: Django admin loads
    http_helper.HttpGetWithRetry(t,
        fmt.Sprintf("http://%s/admin/", albUrl),
        nil, 200, "Django", 10, 5*time.Second,
    )

    // Test 3: API endpoint works
    http_helper.HttpGetWithRetry(t,
        fmt.Sprintf("http://%s/api/jobs/", albUrl),
        nil, 200, "results", 10, 5*time.Second,
    )

    // =========================================
    // Performance Test
    // =========================================
    
    // Simple load test - 100 concurrent requests
    var wg sync.WaitGroup
    errors := make(chan error, 100)
    
    for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
        wg.Add(1)
        go func() {
            defer wg.Done()
            _, err := http.Get(fmt.Sprintf("http://%s/api/jobs/", albUrl))
            if err != nil {
                errors <- err
            }
        }()
    }
    
    wg.Wait()
    close(errors)
    
    errorCount := len(errors)
    assert.LessOrEqual(t, errorCount, 5, "Should have < 5% error rate")
}
test/chaos_test.go - Chaos Engineering 
// =========================================
// Chaos Engineering Tests for anihpj
// =========================================

func TestChaosInstanceTermination(t *testing.T) {
    terraformOptions := terraform.WithDefaultRetryableErrors(t, &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../environments/test",
    })

    // Get instance IDs
    instanceIds := terraform.OutputList(t, terraformOptions, "ecs_instance_ids")
    albUrl := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "alb_dns_name")

    // Verify app is healthy before chaos
    http_helper.HttpGetWithRetry(t, 
        fmt.Sprintf("http://%s/health", albUrl),
        nil, 200, "OK", 5, 5*time.Second,
    )

    // CHAOS: Terminate one instance
    if len(instanceIds) > 1 {
        aws.TerminateInstance(t, "us-east-1", instanceIds[0])
        
        // Wait for ASG to replace
        time.Sleep(60 * time.Second)
        
        // Verify app still works (HA validation)
        http_helper.HttpGetWithRetry(t,
            fmt.Sprintf("http://%s/health", albUrl),
            nil, 200, "OK", 30, 10*time.Second,
        )
    }
}

func TestChaosDatabaseFailover(t *testing.T) {
    // Test RDS Multi-AZ failover
    terraformOptions := terraform.WithDefaultRetryableErrors(t, &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../environments/test",
    })

    dbIdentifier := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "db_identifier")
    
    // Trigger failover
    aws.RebootRDSInstance(t, "us-east-1", dbIdentifier, true) // forceFailover=true
    
    // Wait and verify recovery
    time.Sleep(120 * time.Second)
    
    albUrl := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "alb_dns_name")
    http_helper.HttpGetWithRetry(t,
        fmt.Sprintf("http://%s/api/jobs/", albUrl),
        nil, 200, "results", 30, 10*time.Second,
    )
}

12.6 Testing Patterns

📁

Test Fixtures

Reusable test data and configurations

🏆

Golden Files

Expected output comparison

📸

Snapshot Testing

Plan output snapshots

tests/fixtures/common.tfvars - Test Fixtures 
# =========================================
# Test Fixtures for anihpj
# =========================================

# Common test variables
project_name = "test-anihpj"
environment  = "test"

# VPC fixtures
vpc_cidr           = "10.99.0.0/16"
availability_zones = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"]

# Database fixtures
db_name          = "anihpj_test"
db_username      = "test_admin"
db_instance_class = "db.t3.micro"
db_multi_az       = false

# ECS fixtures
ecs_desired_count = 1
ecs_cpu           = 256
ecs_memory        = 512

# Tags fixture
common_tags = {
  Project     = "anihpj"
  Environment = "test"
  ManagedBy   = "terraform"
  Purpose     = "integration-testing"
}
tests/golden/vpc_plan.golden - Golden File 
# =========================================
# Golden File - Expected VPC Plan Output
# =========================================

Terraform will perform the following actions:

  # aws_vpc.main will be created
  + resource "aws_vpc" "main" {
      + cidr_block           = "10.99.0.0/16"
      + enable_dns_hostnames = true
      + enable_dns_support   = true
      + tags                 = {
          + "Environment" = "test"
          + "Name"        = "test-anihpj-vpc"
          + "Project"     = "test-anihpj"
        }
    }

Plan: 1 to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy.
test/snapshot_test.go - Snapshot Testing 
// =========================================
// Snapshot Testing for anihpj
// =========================================

func TestPlanSnapshot(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    terraformOptions := &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../modules/networking",
        VarFiles:     []string{"../tests/fixtures/common.tfvars"},
        PlanFilePath: "./vpc.tfplan",
    }

    // Generate plan
    terraform.Init(t, terraformOptions)
    planJson := terraform.Plan(t, terraformOptions)

    // Compare with golden file
    goldenFile := "../tests/golden/vpc_plan.golden"
    
    if os.Getenv("UPDATE_GOLDEN") == "true" {
        // Update golden file
        os.WriteFile(goldenFile, []byte(planJson), 0644)
    } else {
        // Compare with existing golden file
        expected, _ := os.ReadFile(goldenFile)
        assert.Equal(t, string(expected), planJson, 
            "Plan output doesn't match golden file. Run with UPDATE_GOLDEN=true to update.")
    }
}

🐍 Django Example: Testing Django Infrastructure Module

tests/django_infra.tftest.hcl - anihpj Django Tests 
# =========================================
# Django Infrastructure Tests for anihpj
# =========================================

variables {
  project_name    = "anihpj"
  environment     = "test"
  django_version  = "4.2"
  python_version  = "3.11"
}

# Test Django container configuration
run "django_ecs_task_config" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = aws_ecs_task_definition.django.container_definitions != null
    error_message = "Django task must have container definitions"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = jsondecode(aws_ecs_task_definition.django.container_definitions)[0].image == "anihpj/django:latest"
    error_message = "Container image should be anihpj/django:latest"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = jsondecode(aws_ecs_task_definition.django.container_definitions)[0].portMappings[0].containerPort == 8000
    error_message = "Django should expose port 8000"
  }
}

# Test Django environment variables
run "django_environment_config" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = contains(keys(jsondecode(aws_ecs_task_definition.django.container_definitions)[0].environment), "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE")
    error_message = "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE must be set"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = contains(keys(jsondecode(aws_ecs_task_definition.django.container_definitions)[0].secrets), "DATABASE_URL")
    error_message = "DATABASE_URL must be in secrets"
  }
}

# Test Django static files S3 bucket
run "django_static_files" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = aws_s3_bucket.static.bucket == "anihpj-test-static"
    error_message = "Static bucket name should be anihpj-test-static"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block.static.block_public_acls == true
    error_message = "Static bucket should block public ACLs"
  }
}

📋 Testing & Validation Cheat Sheet

✅ Validation Commands
terraform validate
terraform fmt -check
tflint
tfsec .
checkov -d .
🧪 Test Commands
terraform test
terraform test -verbose
terraform test -filter=file
go test -v ./...
go test -timeout 30m
📁 File Structure
tests/
  ├── *.tftest.hcl
  ├── fixtures/
  │   └── *.tfvars
  └── golden/
      └── *.golden
🔺 Testing Pyramid
E2E (few, slow, costly)
Integration (terratest)
Contract (interfaces)
Unit (many, fast, free)
⚡ Validation Functions
can(expression)
contains(list, val)
regex("pattern", str)
length(collection)
alltrue(list)
🏷️ Test Block Keywords
run "name" { }
variables { }
assert { }
command = plan|apply
mock_provider { }
✅ DO
  • Test modules in isolation
  • Use command = plan for fast tests
  • Always defer Destroy() in Terratest
  • Use meaningful assertion messages
  • Run tests in CI/CD pipeline
  • Use test fixtures for consistency
❌ DON'T
  • Test with production credentials
  • Skip cleanup (orphaned resources)
  • Hardcode region-specific values
  • Run E2E tests on every commit
  • Ignore test failures in CI
  • Share state between tests

📚 Appendix: Quick Reference

Command Quick Reference

Command Description Common Flags
terraform init Initialize working directory -upgrade, -reconfigure
terraform plan Preview changes -out, -var-file, -target
terraform apply Apply changes -auto-approve, -var-file
terraform destroy Destroy resources -target, -auto-approve
terraform fmt Format code -check, -recursive
terraform validate Validate syntax -json
terraform output Show outputs -json, -raw
terraform state list List resources Pattern filter
terraform state show Show resource Resource address
terraform import Import resource Address + ID

Django + Terraform Cheat Sheet

🦄
Essential AWS Resources for Django
Compute
  • EC2 / ECS / Lambda
  • Auto Scaling Group
  • Application Load Balancer
Database
  • RDS PostgreSQL
  • ElastiCache Redis
  • DynamoDB (sessions)
Storage
  • S3 (static/media)
  • CloudFront (CDN)
  • EFS (shared files)
Security
  • Secrets Manager
  • IAM Roles/Policies
  • Security Groups

Quick Start: Deploy Django on AWS

Terminal - Complete Deployment 
# 1. Clone your Terraform repo
git clone https://github.com/yourorg/terraform-anihpj.git
cd terraform-anihpj

# 2. Configure AWS credentials
aws configure --profile anihpj
export AWS_PROFILE=anihpj

# 3. Initialize Terraform
cd environments/dev
terraform init

# 4. Review the plan
terraform plan -var-file="terraform.tfvars"

# 5. Apply infrastructure
terraform apply -var-file="terraform.tfvars"

# 6. Get outputs for Django settings
terraform output -json > outputs.json

# 7. Deploy Django application
# (Use outputs to configure settings.py)

Common Issues & Solutions

Issue Cause Solution
State locked Previous operation crashed terraform force-unlock LOCK_ID
Provider version mismatch Lock file outdated terraform init -upgrade
Resource already exists Created outside Terraform terraform import
Dependency cycle Circular references Use depends_on or restructure
Plan shows unwanted changes State drift terraform refresh or ignore_changes

13. Advanced Topics

🔧
⚡

Advanced Topics

Master advanced Terraform techniques including import strategies, CDK, custom providers, multi-cloud deployments, and Terraform internals.

9
Topics
Expert
Level
1.5+
TF Version

📍 In This Section

13.1 Import 13.2 Moved Blocks 13.3 CDKTF 13.4 Custom Providers 13.5 State Migration 13.6 Stacks 13.7 OIDC 13.8 Multi-Cloud 13.9 Internals

13.1 Terraform Import

Import existing AWS resources into Terraform management without recreating them.

🔄 Import Workflow (Terraform 1.5+)

🔍
Identify
Resources
→
📝
Add Import
Blocks
→
⚙️
Generate
Config
→
✏️
Review &
Refine
→
🚀
Apply
Import
✨ Import Block (1.5+) - Recommended
  • Declarative in code
  • Auto-generate config
  • Version controlled
  • Reproducible
  • Part of plan/apply workflow
📜 CLI Import (Legacy)
  • Imperative command
  • Write config manually
  • Not in version control
  • One-time operation
  • Still useful for quick imports
import.tf - Import Blocks (Terraform 1.5+) 
# =========================================
# Import Existing anihpj Resources
# =========================================

# Import existing S3 bucket
import {
  to = aws_s3_bucket.existing_static
  id = "anihpj-static-prod-123456789"
}

# Import existing RDS instance
import {
  to = aws_db_instance.existing_postgres
  id = "anihpj-postgres-prod"
}

# Import existing VPC
import {
  to = aws_vpc.existing
  id = "vpc-0abc123def456789"
}

# Import security group
import {
  to = aws_security_group.existing_web
  id = "sg-0abc123def456789"
}

# After adding import blocks, generate configuration:
# terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf
Terminal - Import Workflow 
# Step 1: Add import blocks to your configuration

# Step 2: Run plan to see what will be imported
terraform plan

# Step 3: Generate configuration (Terraform 1.5+)
terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated_resources.tf

# Step 4: Review generated configuration
cat generated_resources.tf

# Step 5: Apply to import resources
terraform apply

# Step 6: Verify state
terraform state list
terraform state show aws_s3_bucket.existing_static

# Legacy import command (still works)
terraform import aws_s3_bucket.bucket my-bucket-name
terraform import aws_db_instance.db mydb-instance-id

📚 Common AWS Resource Import IDs

Resource Import ID Format Example
aws_vpc VPC ID vpc-0abc123def
aws_subnet Subnet ID subnet-0abc123def
aws_s3_bucket Bucket Name my-bucket-name
aws_db_instance DB Identifier my-postgres-db
aws_iam_role Role Name my-iam-role
aws_security_group Security Group ID sg-0abc123def

💡 Import Best Practices

🔍 Audit First
List existing resources with AWS CLI before importing.
📋 Review Generated Code
Always clean up auto-generated config before committing.
🧪 Test in Dev
Practice import workflow on non-production resources first.
📦 Batch Imports
Group related resources in same import file for clarity.

13.2 State Refactoring with moved Blocks

✏️
Rename Resource
Change resource names
📦
Move to Module
Reorganize into modules
🔄
count → for_each
Migration pattern
📂
Rename Module
Module refactoring

📍 moved Block State Transition

FROM (Old Address)
aws_s3_bucket.static
→
moved
No destroy!
TO (New Address)
aws_s3_bucket.django_static
refactor.tf - moved Blocks 
# =========================================
# Refactoring Resources Without Recreation
# =========================================

# Rename a resource
moved {
  from = aws_s3_bucket.static
  to   = aws_s3_bucket.django_static
}

# Move resource into module
moved {
  from = aws_vpc.main
  to   = module.networking.aws_vpc.main
}

# Move resource out of module
moved {
  from = module.old_networking.aws_vpc.main
  to   = aws_vpc.main
}

# Rename module
moved {
  from = module.webservers
  to   = module.django_servers
}

# Move indexed resource to for_each
moved {
  from = aws_subnet.public[0]
  to   = aws_subnet.public["us-east-1a"]
}

# After refactoring, apply to update state
# terraform apply

💡 Best Practices for moved Blocks

✅ Keep in Code
Leave moved blocks as documentation of refactoring history.
🧪 Test First
Always run terraform plan before apply to verify moves.
🔗 Chain Moves
Multiple moved blocks can chain together for complex refactors.

13.3 Cloud Development Kit for Terraform (CDKTF)

Write Terraform configurations using your favorite programming language instead of HCL.

🚀 What is CDKTF?

💻 Use Real Languages
TypeScript, Python, Java, C#, Go - write IaC in languages you already know.
🔧 Full IDE Support
Autocomplete, refactoring, type checking, and debugging in your IDE.
📦 Use Any Provider
Works with all 3000+ Terraform providers from the Registry.
🔄 Synth to HCL JSON
Generates standard Terraform JSON that Terraform CLI executes.

⚖️ When to Use HCL vs CDKTF

Aspect ✅ Choose HCL ✅ Choose CDKTF
Team Background Ops/SRE teams familiar with HCL Developer teams stronger in TypeScript/Python
Project Complexity Simple to moderate infrastructure Complex logic, conditionals, loops
Abstraction Needs Standard module patterns Custom constructs, inheritance, composition
Testing terraform test, Terratest Native unit testing (Jest, pytest, etc.)
Hiring/Learning HCL is industry standard Leverage existing programming skills
📘
TypeScript
Most Popular
🐍
Python
Data/ML Teams
☕
Java
Enterprise
🔷
C#
.NET Teams
🔵
Go
Cloud Native
Terminal - CDKTF Setup 
# Install CDKTF CLI
npm install -g cdktf-cli

# Initialize new TypeScript project
mkdir anihpj-cdktf && cd anihpj-cdktf
cdktf init --template=typescript --local

# Or Python project
cdktf init --template=python --local

# Add AWS provider
cdktf provider add aws

# Synthesize Terraform JSON
cdktf synth

# Deploy infrastructure
cdktf deploy

# Destroy infrastructure
cdktf destroy
main.ts - CDKTF TypeScript Example 
import { Construct } from "constructs";
import { App, TerraformStack, TerraformOutput } from "cdktf";
import { AwsProvider } from "@cdktf/provider-aws/lib/provider";
import { S3Bucket } from "@cdktf/provider-aws/lib/s3-bucket";
import { DbInstance } from "@cdktf/provider-aws/lib/db-instance";
import { Instance } from "@cdktf/provider-aws/lib/instance";

// =========================================
// Django Application Stack with CDKTF
// =========================================

class DjangoInfraStack extends TerraformStack {
  constructor(scope: Construct, id: string) {
    super(scope, id);

    // Configure AWS Provider
    new AwsProvider(this, "aws", {
      region: "us-east-1",
    });

    // S3 Bucket for Django Static Files
    const staticBucket = new S3Bucket(this, "static-files", {
      bucket: "anihpj-static-files-${Date.now()}",
      tags: {
        Name: "anihpj-static",
        Project: "anihpj",
        Environment: "production",
        ManagedBy: "cdktf",
      },
    });

    // RDS PostgreSQL for Django
    const database = new DbInstance(this, "postgres", {
      identifier: "anihpj-postgres",
      engine: "postgres",
      engineVersion: "15.4",
      instanceClass: "db.t3.micro",
      allocatedStorage: 20,
      dbName: "anihpj",
      username: "django_admin",
      password: "changeme123!", // Use secrets manager!
      skipFinalSnapshot: true,
      tags: {
        Name: "anihpj-database",
        Project: "anihpj",
      },
    });

    // EC2 Instance for Django App
    const webServer = new Instance(this, "django-server", {
      ami: "ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0",
      instanceType: "t3.small",
      tags: {
        Name: "anihpj-django-server",
        Project: "anihpj",
      },
    });

    // Outputs
    new TerraformOutput(this, "bucket_name", {
      value: staticBucket.bucket,
    });

    new TerraformOutput(this, "db_endpoint", {
      value: database.endpoint,
    });

    new TerraformOutput(this, "server_ip", {
      value: webServer.publicIp,
    });
  }
}

// Create app and stack
const app = new App();
new DjangoInfraStack(app, "anihpj-production");
app.synth();
main.py - CDKTF Python Example 
#!/usr/bin/env python
from constructs import Construct
from cdktf import App, TerraformStack, TerraformOutput
from cdktf_cdktf_provider_aws.provider import AwsProvider
from cdktf_cdktf_provider_aws.s3_bucket import S3Bucket
from cdktf_cdktf_provider_aws.db_instance import DbInstance
from cdktf_cdktf_provider_aws.instance import Instance

# =========================================
# Django Application Stack with CDKTF
# =========================================

class DjangoInfraStack(TerraformStack):
    def __init__(self, scope: Construct, id: str):
        super().__init__(scope, id)

        # Configure AWS Provider
        AwsProvider(self, "aws", region="us-east-1")

        # S3 Bucket for Django Static Files
        static_bucket = S3Bucket(
            self, "static-files",
            bucket="anihpj-static-files-python",
            tags={
                "Name": "anihpj-static",
                "Project": "anihpj",
                "Environment": "production",
                "ManagedBy": "cdktf-python",
            }
        )

        # RDS PostgreSQL for Django
        database = DbInstance(
            self, "postgres",
            identifier="anihpj-postgres",
            engine="postgres",
            engine_version="15.4",
            instance_class="db.t3.micro",
            allocated_storage=20,
            db_name="anihpj",
            username="django_admin",
            password="changeme123!",  # Use secrets manager!
            skip_final_snapshot=True,
            tags={
                "Name": "anihpj-database",
                "Project": "anihpj",
            }
        )

        # EC2 Instance for Django App
        web_server = Instance(
            self, "django-server",
            ami="ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0",
            instance_type="t3.small",
            tags={
                "Name": "anihpj-django-server",
                "Project": "anihpj",
            }
        )

        # Outputs
        TerraformOutput(self, "bucket_name", value=static_bucket.bucket)
        TerraformOutput(self, "db_endpoint", value=database.endpoint)
        TerraformOutput(self, "server_ip", value=web_server.public_ip)


# Create app and stack
app = App()
DjangoInfraStack(app, "anihpj-production")
app.synth()

🔄 CDKTF Workflow

📝
Write
TS/Python
→
⚙️
cdktf synth
Generate
→
📄
TF JSON
cdktf.out/
→
📋
cdktf diff
Plan
→
🚀
cdktf deploy
Apply

🤔 Should You Use CDKTF?

✅ Yes, if...
  • Team prefers TypeScript/Python
  • Complex logic & conditionals
  • Want native unit testing
  • Building reusable constructs
❌ No, if...
  • Team already knows HCL
  • Simple infrastructure
  • Using Terraform Cloud/Enterprise
  • Need maximum community support

13.4 Custom Providers

Build your own Terraform providers for internal APIs, custom services, or unsupported platforms.

🏗️ Provider Architecture

📄
HCL Config
Your Terraform code
→
⚙️
Provider Plugin
gRPC Protocol
→
🌐
Target API
REST/gRPC/SDK
✨ Plugin Framework (Recommended)
  • Modern Go framework
  • Type-safe schemas
  • Built-in validation
  • Better debugging
  • Active development
📜 SDKv2 (Legacy)
  • Original framework
  • Most existing providers
  • Extensive documentation
  • Maintenance mode
  • Migrate to Framework
Terminal - Provider Scaffolding 
# Install terraform-plugin-docs
go install github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-docs/cmd/tfplugindocs@latest

# Clone template repository
git clone https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-provider-scaffolding-framework
mv terraform-provider-scaffolding-framework terraform-provider-anihpj
cd terraform-provider-anihpj

# Update go.mod
go mod edit -module=terraform-provider-anihpj
go mod tidy

# Build provider
go build -o terraform-provider-anihpj

# Run acceptance tests
TF_ACC=1 go test ./... -v
provider.go - Provider Definition 
package provider

import (
    "context"

    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/datasource"
    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/provider"
    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/provider/schema"
    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/resource"
    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/types"
)

// Ensure provider satisfies interfaces
var _ provider.Provider = &AnihpjProvider{}

// AnihpjProvider defines the provider implementation
type AnihpjProvider struct {
    version string
}

// AnihpjProviderModel describes the provider data model
type AnihpjProviderModel struct {
    Endpoint types.String `tfsdk:"endpoint"`
    ApiKey   types.String `tfsdk:"api_key"`
}

func (p *AnihpjProvider) Metadata(ctx context.Context, req provider.MetadataRequest, resp *provider.MetadataResponse) {
    resp.TypeName = "anihpj"
    resp.Version = p.version
}

func (p *AnihpjProvider) Schema(ctx context.Context, req provider.SchemaRequest, resp *provider.SchemaResponse) {
    resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
        Attributes: map[string]schema.Attribute{
            "endpoint": schema.StringAttribute{
                MarkdownDescription: "API endpoint URL",
                Optional:            true,
            },
            "api_key": schema.StringAttribute{
                MarkdownDescription: "API key for authentication",
                Optional:            true,
                Sensitive:           true,
            },
        },
    }
}

func (p *AnihpjProvider) Configure(ctx context.Context, req provider.ConfigureRequest, resp *provider.ConfigureResponse) {
    var data AnihpjProviderModel

    resp.Diagnostics.Append(req.Config.Get(ctx, &data)...)
    if resp.Diagnostics.HasError() {
        return
    }

    // Create API client with configuration
    client := NewAPIClient(data.Endpoint.ValueString(), data.ApiKey.ValueString())

    resp.DataSourceData = client
    resp.ResourceData = client
}

func (p *AnihpjProvider) Resources(ctx context.Context) []func() resource.Resource {
    return []func() resource.Resource{
        NewJobPostResource,
    }
}

func (p *AnihpjProvider) DataSources(ctx context.Context) []func() datasource.DataSource {
    return []func() datasource.DataSource{
        NewJobPostDataSource,
    }
}

func New(version string) func() provider.Provider {
    return func() provider.Provider {
        return &AnihpjProvider{
            version: version,
        }
    }
}
resource_jobpost.go - Resource Implementation 
package provider

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"

    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/resource"
    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/resource/schema"
    "github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-framework/types"
)

var _ resource.Resource = &JobPostResource{}

type JobPostResource struct {
    client *APIClient
}

type JobPostResourceModel struct {
    Id          types.String `tfsdk:"id"`
    Title       types.String `tfsdk:"title"`
    Description types.String `tfsdk:"description"`
    Company     types.String `tfsdk:"company"`
    Location    types.String `tfsdk:"location"`
    Salary      types.Int64  `tfsdk:"salary"`
}

func NewJobPostResource() resource.Resource {
    return &JobPostResource{}
}

func (r *JobPostResource) Metadata(ctx context.Context, req resource.MetadataRequest, resp *resource.MetadataResponse) {
    resp.TypeName = req.ProviderTypeName + "_jobpost"
}

func (r *JobPostResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, req resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
    resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
        MarkdownDescription: "Manages a job posting in anihpj",
        Attributes: map[string]schema.Attribute{
            "id": schema.StringAttribute{
                Computed:            true,
                MarkdownDescription: "Job post identifier",
            },
            "title": schema.StringAttribute{
                Required:            true,
                MarkdownDescription: "Job title",
            },
            "description": schema.StringAttribute{
                Required:            true,
                MarkdownDescription: "Job description",
            },
            "company": schema.StringAttribute{
                Required:            true,
                MarkdownDescription: "Company name",
            },
            "location": schema.StringAttribute{
                Optional:            true,
                MarkdownDescription: "Job location",
            },
            "salary": schema.Int64Attribute{
                Optional:            true,
                MarkdownDescription: "Annual salary",
            },
        },
    }
}

func (r *JobPostResource) Create(ctx context.Context, req resource.CreateRequest, resp *resource.CreateResponse) {
    var data JobPostResourceModel

    resp.Diagnostics.Append(req.Plan.Get(ctx, &data)...)
    if resp.Diagnostics.HasError() {
        return
    }

    // Call API to create job post
    jobPost, err := r.client.CreateJobPost(JobPost{
        Title:       data.Title.ValueString(),
        Description: data.Description.ValueString(),
        Company:     data.Company.ValueString(),
        Location:    data.Location.ValueString(),
        Salary:      data.Salary.ValueInt64(),
    })
    if err != nil {
        resp.Diagnostics.AddError("Client Error", fmt.Sprintf("Unable to create job post: %s", err))
        return
    }

    data.Id = types.StringValue(jobPost.ID)
    resp.Diagnostics.Append(resp.State.Set(ctx, &data)...)
}

// Read, Update, Delete methods would follow similar patterns...

1
Create GitHub Repo
terraform-provider-name
2
Add GPG Key
Sign releases

Create Release

Publish
registry.terraform.io

13.5 State Migration

Safely migrate Terraform state between backends and refactor state structure.

🔄
Backend Migration
Local → S3/Cloud
📂
State Split
Monolith → Multiple
🔗
State Merge
Combine states
🏷️
Workspace Migration
TF Cloud/Enterprise

🔄 Backend Migration Flow

💾
Local
.tfstate
→
📋
Backup
Copy state
→
⚙️
Configure
New backend
→
🔄
tf init
-migrate-state
→
☁️
S3/Cloud
Remote state
Terminal - Backend Migration Commands 
# =========================================
# Backend Migration: Local to S3
# =========================================

# Step 1: Backup current state (CRITICAL!)
cp terraform.tfstate terraform.tfstate.backup
cp terraform.tfstate.backup ~/safe-location/

# Step 2: Update backend configuration in backend.tf
# (see code block below)

# Step 3: Initialize with migration flag
terraform init -migrate-state

# When prompted, confirm the migration
# Terraform will copy state to new backend

# Step 4: Verify state was migrated
terraform state list

# Step 5: Run plan to confirm no changes
terraform plan
# Should show: "No changes"

# =========================================
# State Commands for Manual Operations
# =========================================

# Pull remote state to local file
terraform state pull > state_backup.json

# Push local state to remote backend
terraform state push state_backup.json

# List all resources in state
terraform state list

# Show specific resource
terraform state show aws_s3_bucket.django_static

# Remove resource from state (doesn't destroy!)
terraform state rm aws_s3_bucket.old_bucket

# Move resource to different address
terraform state mv aws_s3_bucket.old aws_s3_bucket.new

# Move resource between state files
terraform state mv -state=source.tfstate -state-out=dest.tfstate \
  aws_s3_bucket.bucket aws_s3_bucket.bucket
backend.tf - S3 Backend Configuration 
# =========================================
# Migrate from Local to S3 Backend
# =========================================

# BEFORE: Local backend (default, no config needed)
# terraform {
#   # Uses local terraform.tfstate by default
# }

# AFTER: S3 backend configuration
terraform {
  backend "s3" {
    bucket         = "anihpj-terraform-state"
    key            = "production/terraform.tfstate"
    region         = "us-east-1"
    encrypt        = true
    dynamodb_table = "anihpj-terraform-locks"
    
    # Optional: Use IAM role
    # role_arn = "arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/terraform-state-role"
  }
}

# =========================================
# State Locking with DynamoDB
# =========================================

resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "terraform_locks" {
  name         = "anihpj-terraform-locks"
  billing_mode = "PAY_PER_REQUEST"
  hash_key     = "LockID"

  attribute {
    name = "LockID"
    type = "S"
  }

  tags = {
    Name    = "Terraform State Lock Table"
    Project = "anihpj"
  }
}

⚠️ Critical Migration Rules

💾 Always Backup First
Copy state file before ANY migration. Store in multiple safe locations.
🔒 Lock State During Migration
Ensure no other operations are running. Communicate with team.
✅ Verify with Plan
After migration, terraform plan should show NO changes.
🧪 Test in Non-Prod
Practice migration on dev/staging before production.

13.6 Terraform Stacks (Preview)

Orchestrate multiple Terraform configurations with dependencies using Terraform Stacks (HCP Terraform feature).

📚 What are Terraform Stacks?

📦 Components
Logical groupings of Terraform configs that deploy together.
🔗 Orchestration
Define dependencies between components for ordered deployments.
🌍 Multi-Region
Deploy same components across multiple regions/accounts.
🔄 Deployments
Coordinate changes across all components with rollback capability.

🏗️ Stack Architecture Example

📚 anihpj-stack
↓
🌐 Network
VPC, Subnets
→
💾 Database
RDS, ElastiCache
→
🖥️ Compute
ECS, EC2
Dependencies ensure proper deployment order
tstack.tf - Stack Configuration 
# =========================================
# Terraform Stack Configuration
# =========================================

# Define the stack
stack {
  name        = "anihpj-production"
  description = "Production infrastructure for anihpj Django app"
}

# Network Component
component "network" {
  source  = "./components/network"
  version = "~> 1.0"

  inputs = {
    environment    = "production"
    vpc_cidr       = "10.0.0.0/16"
    azs            = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]
    project_name   = "anihpj"
  }
}

# Database Component - depends on network
component "database" {
  source  = "./components/database"
  version = "~> 1.0"

  inputs = {
    vpc_id             = component.network.vpc_id
    private_subnet_ids = component.network.private_subnet_ids
    environment        = "production"
    db_name            = "anihpj"
    instance_class     = "db.t3.medium"
  }

  depends_on = [component.network]
}

# Compute Component - depends on network and database
component "compute" {
  source  = "./components/compute"
  version = "~> 1.0"

  inputs = {
    vpc_id             = component.network.vpc_id
    public_subnet_ids  = component.network.public_subnet_ids
    private_subnet_ids = component.network.private_subnet_ids
    db_endpoint        = component.database.endpoint
    db_password_secret = component.database.password_secret_arn
    environment        = "production"
    instance_type      = "t3.small"
    min_capacity       = 2
    max_capacity       = 10
  }

  depends_on = [component.network, component.database]
}

# Storage Component - can deploy in parallel with compute
component "storage" {
  source  = "./components/storage"
  version = "~> 1.0"

  inputs = {
    bucket_prefix = "anihpj-production"
    environment   = "production"
  }

  depends_on = [component.network]
}
deployments.tf - Multi-Region Deployment 
# =========================================
# Deploy Stack to Multiple Regions
# =========================================

# US East deployment
deployment "us-east" {
  stack = stack.anihpj-production

  inputs = {
    region      = "us-east-1"
    environment = "production"
  }

  providers = {
    aws = aws.us_east
  }
}

# EU West deployment
deployment "eu-west" {
  stack = stack.anihpj-production

  inputs = {
    region      = "eu-west-1"
    environment = "production"
  }

  providers = {
    aws = aws.eu_west
  }

  depends_on = [deployment.us-east]
}

# Provider configurations
provider "aws" {
  alias  = "us_east"
  region = "us-east-1"
}

provider "aws" {
  alias  = "eu_west"
  region = "eu-west-1"
}

✨ When to Use Terraform Stacks

✅ Large Deployments
Multiple related configurations
✅ Multi-Region
Deploy same infra globally
✅ Dependencies
Complex ordering needs
✅ HCP Terraform
Enterprise orchestration

13.7 Workload Identity (OIDC)

Use OIDC for keyless authentication from CI/CD pipelines—no long-lived credentials needed.

🔐 OIDC Authentication Flow

🔄
CI/CD
GitHub/GitLab
→
🎫
OIDC Token
JWT
→
🔑
AWS STS
AssumeRole
→
⏱️
Temp Creds
Short-lived
❌ Static Credentials (Avoid)
  • Long-lived access keys
  • Stored in CI secrets
  • Rotation burden
  • Risk if compromised
✅ OIDC (Recommended)
  • No stored secrets
  • Auto-rotating tokens
  • Federated trust
  • Audit-friendly
oidc.tf - GitHub Actions OIDC 
# =========================================
# OIDC Provider for GitHub Actions
# =========================================

# OIDC Provider
resource "aws_iam_openid_connect_provider" "github" {
  url = "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com"

  client_id_list = ["sts.amazonaws.com"]

  thumbprint_list = [
    "6938fd4d98bab03faadb97b34396831e3780aea1"
  ]

  tags = {
    Name = "GitHub Actions OIDC Provider"
  }
}

# IAM Role for GitHub Actions
resource "aws_iam_role" "github_actions" {
  name = "anihpj-github-actions-role"

  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Principal = {
          Federated = aws_iam_openid_connect_provider.github.arn
        }
        Action = "sts:AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity"
        Condition = {
          StringEquals = {
            "token.actions.githubusercontent.com:aud" = "sts.amazonaws.com"
          }
          StringLike = {
            "token.actions.githubusercontent.com:sub" = "repo:yourorg/anihpj:*"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  })
}

# Attach policies
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "terraform_access" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.github_actions.name
  policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.terraform_deploy.arn
}
.github/workflows/terraform.yml - Using OIDC 
jobs:
  terraform:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      id-token: write  # Required for OIDC
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/anihpj-github-actions-role
          aws-region: us-east-1

      - name: Terraform Plan
        run: terraform plan
🐙
GitHub Actions
✓ Native Support
🦊
GitLab CI
✓ Supported
🔵
Azure DevOps
✓ Supported
🟣
CircleCI
✓ Supported

13.8 Multi-Cloud Deployments

Manage infrastructure across multiple cloud providers with unified Terraform configurations.

☁️ Multi-Cloud Strategy

🟠
AWS
Primary
+
🔵
Azure
DR/Hybrid
+
🟢
GCP
ML/Data
🔀 Abstraction Pattern
  • Cloud-agnostic modules
  • Unified interface
  • Provider-specific implementations
  • Easy cloud switching
🎯 Best-of-Breed Pattern
  • Use each cloud's strengths
  • AWS for compute
  • GCP for ML/BigQuery
  • Azure for enterprise
🛡️ DR/Failover Pattern
  • Primary in one cloud
  • DR in another
  • Avoid vendor lock-in
  • Business continuity
providers.tf - Multi-Cloud Configuration 
# =========================================
# Multi-Cloud Provider Configuration
# =========================================

terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.5.0"

  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
    azurerm = {
      source  = "hashicorp/azurerm"
      version = "~> 3.0"
    }
    google = {
      source  = "hashicorp/google"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }
}

# AWS Provider - Primary Infrastructure
provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region

  default_tags {
    tags = {
      Project     = "anihpj"
      Environment = var.environment
      ManagedBy   = "terraform"
      Cloud       = "aws"
    }
  }
}

# Azure Provider - Disaster Recovery
provider "azurerm" {
  features {}

  subscription_id = var.azure_subscription_id
  tenant_id       = var.azure_tenant_id
}

# GCP Provider - ML/Analytics
provider "google" {
  project = var.gcp_project_id
  region  = var.gcp_region
}

# =========================================
# Variables
# =========================================

variable "aws_region" {
  default = "us-east-1"
}

variable "azure_subscription_id" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

variable "azure_tenant_id" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

variable "gcp_project_id" {
  type = string
}

variable "gcp_region" {
  default = "us-central1"
}

variable "environment" {
  default = "production"
}
modules/storage/main.tf - Cloud-Agnostic Module 
# =========================================
# Cloud-Agnostic Storage Module
# =========================================

variable "cloud_provider" {
  description = "Cloud provider: aws, azure, or gcp"
  type        = string
  validation {
    condition     = contains(["aws", "azure", "gcp"], var.cloud_provider)
    error_message = "Must be aws, azure, or gcp."
  }
}

variable "bucket_name" {
  description = "Name of the storage bucket"
  type        = string
}

variable "environment" {
  description = "Environment name"
  type        = string
}

# AWS S3
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "storage" {
  count  = var.cloud_provider == "aws" ? 1 : 0
  bucket = "${var.bucket_name}-${var.environment}"

  tags = {
    Name        = var.bucket_name
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Azure Blob Storage
resource "azurerm_storage_account" "storage" {
  count                    = var.cloud_provider == "azure" ? 1 : 0
  name                     = replace("${var.bucket_name}${var.environment}", "-", "")
  resource_group_name      = var.azure_resource_group
  location                 = var.azure_location
  account_tier             = "Standard"
  account_replication_type = "GRS"

  tags = {
    Name        = var.bucket_name
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "azurerm_storage_container" "storage" {
  count                 = var.cloud_provider == "azure" ? 1 : 0
  name                  = var.bucket_name
  storage_account_name  = azurerm_storage_account.storage[0].name
  container_access_type = "private"
}

# GCP Cloud Storage
resource "google_storage_bucket" "storage" {
  count         = var.cloud_provider == "gcp" ? 1 : 0
  name          = "${var.bucket_name}-${var.environment}"
  location      = var.gcp_region
  force_destroy = false

  uniform_bucket_level_access = true

  labels = {
    name        = var.bucket_name
    environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Outputs - Unified interface
output "bucket_id" {
  value = coalesce(
    try(aws_s3_bucket.storage[0].id, null),
    try(azurerm_storage_container.storage[0].id, null),
    try(google_storage_bucket.storage[0].id, null)
  )
}

output "bucket_url" {
  value = coalesce(
    try("s3://${aws_s3_bucket.storage[0].bucket}", null),
    try(azurerm_storage_account.storage[0].primary_blob_endpoint, null),
    try("gs://${google_storage_bucket.storage[0].name}", null)
  )
}
main.tf - Multi-Cloud Deployment 
# =========================================
# anihpj Multi-Cloud Deployment
# =========================================

# Primary: AWS
module "aws_storage" {
  source = "./modules/storage"

  cloud_provider = "aws"
  bucket_name    = "anihpj-static"
  environment    = var.environment
}

module "aws_compute" {
  source = "./modules/compute"

  cloud_provider = "aws"
  instance_type  = "t3.medium"
  environment    = var.environment
}

# DR Site: Azure
module "azure_storage" {
  source = "./modules/storage"

  cloud_provider       = "azure"
  bucket_name          = "anihpj-static-dr"
  environment          = var.environment
  azure_resource_group = azurerm_resource_group.dr.name
  azure_location       = "eastus2"
}

# ML/Analytics: GCP
module "gcp_bigquery" {
  source = "./modules/analytics"

  cloud_provider = "gcp"
  dataset_name   = "anihpj_analytics"
  environment    = var.environment
  gcp_region     = "us-central1"
}

# Cross-Cloud Outputs
output "storage_endpoints" {
  value = {
    primary    = module.aws_storage.bucket_url
    dr         = module.azure_storage.bucket_url
    analytics  = module.gcp_bigquery.dataset_url
  }
}

⚠️ Multi-Cloud Considerations

💰 Cost Complexity
Multiple billing, data transfer costs, management overhead.
🔐 Security
Different IAM models, network security, compliance requirements.
🎓 Skills
Team needs expertise in multiple cloud platforms.
🌐 Networking
Cross-cloud connectivity, latency, and bandwidth.

13.9 Terraform Internals

Understand how Terraform works under the hood—dependency graphs, parallelism, and the provider plugin protocol.

⚙️ Terraform Execution Flow

📄
Parse
HCL → AST
→
🔍
Validate
Check syntax
→
🕸️
Graph
Build DAG
→
📊
Diff
Plan changes
→
🚀
Apply
Execute

🕸️ Dependency Graph (DAG)

aws_vpc.main
↓
aws_subnet.public
↓
aws_subnet.private
↓
aws_instance.web
↓
aws_db_instance.postgres
Resources at the same level can be created in parallel
Terminal - Graph Visualization 
# =========================================
# Visualize Terraform Dependency Graph
# =========================================

# Generate graph in DOT format
terraform graph > graph.dot

# Generate graph for plan only
terraform graph -type=plan > plan_graph.dot

# Generate graph for apply
terraform graph -type=apply > apply_graph.dot

# Convert to PNG (requires Graphviz)
# Install: apt install graphviz OR brew install graphviz
dot -Tpng graph.dot -o graph.png

# Convert to SVG for web
dot -Tsvg graph.dot -o graph.svg

# View specific resource dependencies
terraform graph | grep -E "aws_vpc|aws_subnet|aws_instance"

# Visualize with online tool
# Copy output and paste to: https://dreampuf.github.io/GraphvizOnline/
⚡ Parallelism

Terraform creates/destroys resources in parallel when no dependencies exist.

# Default: 10 concurrent operations
terraform apply -parallelism=20

# Disable parallelism (debug)
terraform apply -parallelism=1
🔌 Provider Protocol

Providers are separate binaries that communicate via gRPC protocol.

  • Protocol v5/v6 (Framework)
  • Binary plugins in .terraform/
  • Schema negotiation
  • CRUD operations via RPC
Terminal - Debugging & Internals 
# =========================================
# Terraform Debug & Trace Logging
# =========================================

# Enable debug logging
export TF_LOG=DEBUG
terraform plan

# Enable trace logging (most verbose)
export TF_LOG=TRACE
terraform apply

# Log to file
export TF_LOG=DEBUG
export TF_LOG_PATH="./terraform.log"
terraform plan

# Provider-specific logging
export TF_LOG_PROVIDER=DEBUG

# Core Terraform logging only
export TF_LOG_CORE=DEBUG

# Disable logging
unset TF_LOG

# =========================================
# Provider Plugin Location
# =========================================

# View provider plugins
ls -la .terraform/providers/

# Provider binary paths
tree .terraform/providers/registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/

# Plugin cache (global)
export TF_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR="$HOME/.terraform.d/plugin-cache"

# =========================================
# State Internals
# =========================================

# View raw state (JSON)
terraform state pull | jq .

# View specific resource in state
terraform state show aws_s3_bucket.static

# State file format version
terraform state pull | jq '.version'

# Resource lineage
terraform state pull | jq '.lineage'

# Serial number (increments on changes)
terraform state pull | jq '.serial'

📁 State File Structure

📋 version
State format version (4)
🔗 lineage
Unique state identifier
#️⃣ serial
Version counter
📦 resources
Resource instances & attrs
📤 outputs
Output values
✅ check_results
Validation results

💡 Performance Tips

🚀 Increase Parallelism
Use -parallelism=20 for large deployments with independent resources.
📦 Plugin Cache
Set TF_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR to avoid re-downloading providers.
🎯 Target Resources
Use -target for faster applies on specific resources.
📊 Refresh Skipping
Use -refresh=false in CI when state is known to be current.

📋 Advanced Topics Quick Reference

🔄 Import Commands
terraform plan -generate-config-out=gen.tf
terraform import aws_s3_bucket.b bucket-name
📍 Moved Block Syntax
moved {
  from = aws_s3_bucket.old
  to = aws_s3_bucket.new
}
🚀 CDKTF Commands
cdktf init --template=typescript
cdktf synth && cdktf deploy
🔄 State Migration
terraform init -migrate-state
terraform state mv old.addr new.addr
🕸️ Graph & Debug
terraform graph | dot -Tpng > g.png
TF_LOG=DEBUG terraform plan
⚡ Performance
terraform apply -parallelism=20
terraform apply -refresh=false

14. Best Practices & Patterns

📋
✨

Best Practices & Patterns

Learn proven patterns and avoid common pitfalls when building production-grade Terraform infrastructure.

7
Topics
Production
Ready
Enterprise
Grade

📍 In This Section

14.1 Code Style 14.2 Resource Patterns 14.3 Module Patterns 14.4 State Patterns 14.5 Security 14.6 Cost 14.7 Anti-Patterns

14.1 Code Style Guide

Element Convention Example
Resource Names snake_case, descriptive aws_s3_bucket.django_static_files
Variable Names snake_case, noun-based var.instance_count
Output Names snake_case, descriptive output.vpc_id
Local Names snake_case local.common_tags
Module Names kebab-case module.vpc-network
AWS Resource Tags PascalCase Environment, Project, ManagedBy
AWS Resource Names kebab-case with context anihpj-web-prod-us-east-1
Naming Pattern Template 
# =========================================
# Consistent Naming with Locals
# =========================================

locals {
  # Naming components
  project     = "anihpj"
  environment = var.environment
  region      = var.aws_region

  # Name prefix for all resources
  name_prefix = "${local.project}-${local.environment}"

  # Full name with region
  full_name = "${local.name_prefix}-${local.region}"

  # Common tags for all resources
  common_tags = {
    Project     = local.project
    Environment = local.environment
    ManagedBy   = "Terraform"
    Repository  = "github.com/yourorg/terraform-anihpj"
  }
}

# Usage
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "static" {
  bucket = "${local.name_prefix}-static"
  tags   = local.common_tags
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "main" {
  identifier = "${local.name_prefix}-postgres"
  tags       = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${local.name_prefix}-postgres"
  })
}

📁 Recommended File Structure

main.tf
Primary resources
variables.tf
Input variables
outputs.tf
Output values
providers.tf
Provider config
locals.tf
Local values
data.tf
Data sources

💬 Comment Guidelines

✅ DO: Explain Why
# Enable versioning for disaster recovery compliance
❌ DON'T: State the Obvious
# Create S3 bucket 
# =========================================
# SECTION HEADERS: Use for major blocks
# =========================================

# Resource comments explain business logic
# WHY this exists, not WHAT it does
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "media" {
  # Separate bucket for user uploads to isolate 
  # from static files and enable different lifecycle rules
  bucket = "${var.project}-media-${var.environment}"
}

# TODO: comments for planned changes
# TODO: Add cross-region replication for DR

# FIXME: for known issues
# FIXME: Bucket policy too permissive, tighten in next sprint

📖 Documentation Standards

📝 README.md
Purpose, usage, requirements
📋 CHANGELOG.md
Version history, breaking changes
📊 variables.tf
Descriptions on all variables
📤 outputs.tf
Descriptions on all outputs

14.2 Resource Patterns

Proven patterns for organizing and managing AWS resources with Terraform.

🏷️ Enterprise Tagging Strategy

💰
Cost Allocation
Project, CostCenter, Owner
🔍
Operations
Environment, ManagedBy
📋
Compliance
DataClass, Compliance
🔄
Automation
BackupPolicy, Retention
tags.tf - Enterprise Tagging 
# =========================================
# Comprehensive Tagging Strategy
# =========================================

locals {
  # Required tags (enforce with Sentinel/OPA)
  required_tags = {
    Project     = var.project_name
    Environment = var.environment
    ManagedBy   = "Terraform"
    Owner       = var.owner_email
    CostCenter  = var.cost_center
  }

  # Compliance tags for production
  compliance_tags = var.environment == "production" ? {
    Compliance    = "PCI-DSS"
    DataClass     = "Confidential"
    BackupPolicy  = "Daily"
  } : {}

  # Merge all tags
  all_tags = merge(local.required_tags, local.compliance_tags)
}

# Provider default tags (applies to ALL resources)
provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region
  default_tags {
    tags = local.required_tags
  }
}

📝 Resource Naming Convention

{project}-{component}-{environment}-{region}
Example: anihpj-web-prod-us-east-1

🗂️ Resource Organization Strategy

📦 By Layer
network/ → compute/ → data/ → app/
🌍 By Environment
envs/dev/ → envs/staging/ → envs/prod/
🔧 By Service
api/ → web/ → worker/ → shared/
🏢 By Team
platform/ → backend/ → frontend/

🔍 Data Source Best Practices

Use data sources to reference existing resources without managing them.
# ✅ Good: Use data sources for shared resources
data "aws_vpc" "main" {
  tags = { Name = "anihpj-vpc" }
}

data "aws_ami" "amazon_linux" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["amazon"]
  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["amzn2-ami-hvm-*-x86_64-gp2"]
  }
}

# Reference in resources
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  subnet_id     = data.aws_vpc.main.id
}

14.3 Module Patterns

Design patterns for creating reusable, maintainable Terraform modules.

🎯 Single Responsibility

Each module should do one thing well. Don't combine VPC + ECS + RDS in one module.

🔌 Loose Coupling

Modules should accept IDs/ARNs as inputs, not create dependencies internally.

📤 Clear Interface

Well-documented inputs with validation, and comprehensive outputs.

🔢 Semantic Versioning

Use semver (v1.2.3) for module versions. Document breaking changes.

🏗️ Module Composition Pattern

📦 Root Module (Environment)
↓ composes
🌐 VPC Module
💾 RDS Module
🖥️ ECS Module
📦 S3 Module
↓ uses
Base Modules (Labels, Tags)
modules/ecs-service/variables.tf - Module Interface 
# =========================================
# Well-Designed Module Interface
# =========================================

# Required inputs with validation
variable "name" {
  description = "Name of the ECS service"
  type        = string
  validation {
    condition     = length(var.name) <= 32
    error_message = "Name must be 32 characters or less."
  }
}

variable "environment" {
  description = "Environment (dev, staging, production)"
  type        = string
  validation {
    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "production"], var.environment)
    error_message = "Must be dev, staging, or production."
  }
}

# Optional with sensible defaults
variable "cpu" {
  description = "CPU units for the task"
  type        = number
  default     = 256
}

variable "memory" {
  description = "Memory (MB) for the task"
  type        = number
  default     = 512
}

# Complex type with object
variable "autoscaling" {
  description = "Autoscaling configuration"
  type = object({
    min_capacity = number
    max_capacity = number
    cpu_target   = number
  })
  default = {
    min_capacity = 1
    max_capacity = 4
    cpu_target   = 70
  }
}

🔢 Version Management

MAJOR
Breaking changes
MINOR
New features
PATCH
Bug fixes
# Pin module versions for stability
module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "~> 5.0"  # Allows 5.x but not 6.0
}

module "internal" {
  source = "git::https://github.com/company/modules.git//vpc?ref=v2.1.0"
}

⚠️ Handling Breaking Changes

Renaming Variables
Keep old variable with deprecation warning, add new one
Removing Outputs
Document in CHANGELOG, bump major version
Changing Defaults
Announce in release notes, provide migration path
State Migration
Provide moved blocks or state mv scripts

14.4 State Patterns

Best practices for managing Terraform state in team environments.

🗄️ State Management Architecture

💻
Local Dev
terraform plan
→
🔒
DynamoDB
State Lock
→
📦
S3 Bucket
State Storage
→
🔐
KMS
Encryption
✅ By Environment
envs/
├── dev/terraform.tfstate
├── staging/terraform.tfstate
└── prod/terraform.tfstate
✅ By Component
prod/
├── network/terraform.tfstate
├── database/terraform.tfstate
└── compute/terraform.tfstate
backend.tf - Production Backend 
# =========================================
# Production-Ready Backend Configuration
# =========================================

terraform {
  backend "s3" {
    bucket         = "anihpj-terraform-state"
    key            = "prod/network/terraform.tfstate"
    region         = "us-east-1"
    encrypt        = true
    kms_key_id     = "alias/terraform-state"
    dynamodb_table = "terraform-locks"
    
    # Prevent accidental deletion
    # Enable versioning on the S3 bucket
  }
}

# Cross-state data access
data "terraform_remote_state" "network" {
  backend = "s3"
  config = {
    bucket = "anihpj-terraform-state"
    key    = "prod/network/terraform.tfstate"
    region = "us-east-1"
  }
}

# Use outputs from network state
resource "aws_db_subnet_group" "main" {
  subnet_ids = data.terraform_remote_state.network.outputs.private_subnet_ids
}

14.5 Security Patterns

Security best practices for Terraform configurations and AWS resources.

🔐 Security Checklist

✅ No Secrets in Code
Use Secrets Manager, SSM, or environment variables.
✅ Encrypt State
Enable S3 encryption with KMS for state files.
✅ Least Privilege
Minimal IAM permissions for Terraform roles.
✅ Private Resources
Databases and backends in private subnets.
security.tf - Secrets Management 
# =========================================
# Secure Secrets Management
# =========================================

# ❌ NEVER do this
# variable "db_password" {
#   default = "mysecretpassword"
# }

# ✅ Use Secrets Manager
data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
  secret_id = "anihpj/prod/db-password"
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "main" {
  identifier     = "anihpj-postgres"
  engine         = "postgres"
  instance_class = "db.t3.medium"
  
  username = "django_admin"
  password = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.db_password.secret_string
  
  # Security settings
  publicly_accessible    = false
  storage_encrypted      = true
  kms_key_id            = aws_kms_key.database.arn
  deletion_protection   = true
  
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.database.id]
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.private.name
}

# ✅ Use sensitive variable
variable "api_key" {
  description = "External API key"
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true  # Won't show in logs
}

🌐 Network Segmentation

🌍 Public Subnet (ALB, NAT Gateway)
↓ Only port 443
🖥️ Private Subnet (ECS, EC2)
↓ Only app port
💾 Data Subnet (RDS, ElastiCache)
✅ Databases should NEVER be in public subnets

📋 Audit Logging

📝
CloudTrail
API calls logging
🔍
VPC Flow Logs
Network traffic
⚙️
Config Rules
Compliance checks
🚨
GuardDuty
Threat detection
# Enable CloudTrail for all API auditing
resource "aws_cloudtrail" "main" {
  name           = "anihpj-audit-trail"
  s3_bucket_name = aws_s3_bucket.cloudtrail.id
  
  enable_logging                = true
  include_global_service_events = true
  is_multi_region_trail        = true
}

14.6 Cost Optimization

Patterns for managing and reducing AWS costs with Terraform.

💰
Right-Sizing
Match instance to workload
📅
Reserved
1-3 year commitments
⚡
Spot Instances
Up to 90% savings
🌙
Scheduling
Stop non-prod at night
cost-optimization.tf - Cost Patterns 
# =========================================
# Cost Optimization Patterns
# =========================================

# Environment-based sizing
locals {
  instance_sizes = {
    dev        = "t3.micro"
    staging    = "t3.small"
    production = "t3.medium"
  }
  
  db_sizes = {
    dev        = "db.t3.micro"
    staging    = "db.t3.small"
    production = "db.t3.medium"
  }
}

resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  instance_type = local.instance_sizes[var.environment]
  
  # Use spot for non-production
  dynamic "instance_market_options" {
    for_each = var.environment != "production" ? [1] : []
    content {
      market_type = "spot"
      spot_options {
        max_price = "0.05"
      }
    }
  }
}

# S3 lifecycle for cost savings
resource "aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration" "logs" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.logs.id
  
  rule {
    id     = "archive-old-logs"
    status = "Enabled"
    
    transition {
      days          = 30
      storage_class = "STANDARD_IA"
    }
    transition {
      days          = 90
      storage_class = "GLACIER"
    }
    expiration {
      days = 365
    }
  }
}

🧹 Cleanup Automation

🗑️ Unused Resources
Orphaned EBS volumes, unused EIPs
📦 Old Snapshots
EBS/RDS snapshots older than retention
🌙 Dev Environments
Auto-stop after business hours
📊 Cost Alerts
Budget alerts with SNS notifications
# Budget alert for cost monitoring
resource "aws_budgets_budget" "monthly" {
  name         = "anihpj-monthly-budget"
  budget_type  = "COST"
  limit_amount = "500"
  limit_unit   = "USD"
  time_unit    = "MONTHLY"

  notification {
    comparison_operator = "GREATER_THAN"
    threshold           = 80
    threshold_type      = "PERCENTAGE"
    notification_type   = "ACTUAL"
    subscriber_email_addresses = [var.alert_email]
  }
}

14.7 Anti-Patterns to Avoid

Common Terraform mistakes to avoid in production environments.

⚠️ Critical Anti-Patterns

❌ Hardcoded Secrets
Never commit passwords to repo
❌ Monolithic State
Split by environment/component
❌ Manual State Edits
Use terraform state commands
❌ No State Locking
Always use DynamoDB lock
Anti-Pattern ❌ Problem Better Approach ✅
Hardcoded values No reusability across environments Use variables with defaults
Monolithic state Slow operations, blast radius Split by component/environment
Manual state edits Corruption, drift risk Use terraform state mv/rm
Secrets in code Security breach potential AWS Secrets Manager/SSM
No state locking Race conditions, corruption DynamoDB locking table
Over-engineering Complexity, hard to maintain Start simple, evolve as needed
No version pinning Breaking changes on update Pin provider versions (~> x.y)
Ignoring count/for_each Duplicate code, errors Use dynamic resource creation

✅ Section 14 Key Takeaways

📝 Code Style
Consistent naming, organized files, comprehensive tagging
📦 Modules
Single responsibility, loose coupling, clear interfaces
🗄️ State
Remote backend, encryption, isolation, locking
🔐 Security
No secrets in code, least privilege, encrypted resources

📈 Terraform Maturity Model

L1
Initial
Manual, ad-hoc
L2
Managed
Remote state
L3
Defined
Modules, CI/CD
L4
Measured
Policy, testing
L5
Optimizing
Self-service

💡 Pro Tips from the Field

🎯 Start Small
Begin with one environment, then expand. Don't try to automate everything at once.
📖 Document Decisions
Use ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) for major infrastructure choices.
🔄 Plan Before Apply
Always review plan output. Save plans to file for CI/CD: terraform plan -out=tfplan
🏷️ Tag Everything
Use provider default_tags. Makes cost allocation and resource tracking effortless.
🔒 Lock Versions
Pin Terraform, providers, and modules. Use .terraform.lock.hcl in git.
🧪 Test in Dev First
Never experiment in production. Create isolated dev environments for testing changes.

📋 Best Practices Quick Reference

📁 File Organization
✓ main.tf → Resources
✓ variables.tf → Inputs
✓ outputs.tf → Exports
✓ providers.tf → Provider config
✓ locals.tf → Local values
✓ data.tf → Data sources
🏷️ Naming Pattern
✓ {project}-{component}-{env}
✓ snake_case for resources
✓ snake_case for variables
