✓ Descriptive, not abbreviated
✓ Consistent across modules
✓ 32 char limit for some AWS
🔐 Security Essentials
✓ No secrets in code
✓ Use Secrets Manager/SSM
✓ Encrypt state with KMS
✓ Private subnets for data
✓ Least privilege IAM
✓ Enable CloudTrail
🗄️ State Management
✓ Remote backend (S3)
✓ DynamoDB locking
✓ Isolate by env/component
✓ Enable versioning
✓ Never edit manually
✓ Use terraform_remote_state

🔀 Module vs Inline: Decision Flow

Will this be reused?
Yes ↓
Create Module
modules/
No ↓
Keep Inline
main.tf
💡 Rule of thumb: If you copy-paste code more than twice, it's time for a module.

🔄 The Golden Workflow

1️⃣ Write
→
2️⃣ Init
→
3️⃣ Validate
→
4️⃣ Plan
→
5️⃣ Review
→
6️⃣ Apply
⚠️ Never skip the Plan and Review steps in production!

15. Troubleshooting & Debugging

🛠️
🔧

Troubleshooting & Debugging

Master the art of diagnosing and resolving Terraform issues. From common errors to advanced recovery procedures.

6
Topic Areas
20+
Error Solutions
Battle
Tested

📍 In This Section

15.1 Common Errors 15.2 State Issues 15.3 Provider Issues 15.4 Performance 15.5 Debugging 15.6 Recovery

🔀 Troubleshooting Decision Tree

❌ Terraform Error?
Auth Error?
Check Credentials
State Lock?
Check DynamoDB
Cycle Error?
Use terraform graph

15.1 Common Errors & Solutions

🔐
Authentication
Credentials, IAM
🔒
State Lock
DynamoDB locks
🔄
Cycles
Dependencies
🚫
Permissions
IAM policies
⏱️
Rate Limits
API throttling

🔴 Error: Provider Configuration Issues

# Error: No valid credential sources found
# Cause: AWS credentials not configured

# Solutions:
# 1. Set environment variables
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="AKIA..."
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="..."

# 2. Use AWS CLI profile
export AWS_PROFILE=anihpj-dev

# 3. Check credentials file
cat ~/.aws/credentials

# 4. Use SSO login
aws sso login --profile anihpj-dev

🔴 Error: Resource Already Exists

# Error: Error creating S3 bucket: BucketAlreadyExists
# Cause: Resource exists but not in state

# Solutions:
# 1. Import existing resource
terraform import aws_s3_bucket.static my-existing-bucket

# 2. Use unique naming with random suffix
resource "random_id" "bucket_suffix" {
  byte_length = 4
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket" "static" {
  bucket = "anihpj-static-${random_id.bucket_suffix.hex}"
}

🔴 Error: State Lock

# Error: Error acquiring the state lock
# Cause: Previous operation didn't release lock

# Check who has the lock
aws dynamodb get-item \
  --table-name terraform-locks \
  --key '{"LockID":{"S":"anihpj/terraform.tfstate"}}'

# Force unlock (USE WITH CAUTION!)
terraform force-unlock LOCK_ID

# Prevent: Always let operations complete
# Never Ctrl+C during apply

🔴 Error: Cycle Detected

# Error: Cycle: aws_security_group.web, aws_security_group.db
# Cause: Circular dependency between resources

# Visualize dependencies
terraform graph | dot -Tpng > deps.png

# Solution: Break cycle with separate rule resources
resource "aws_security_group" "web" {
  name = "web-sg"
  # Don't reference db_sg here
}

resource "aws_security_group" "db" {
  name = "db-sg"
  # Don't reference web_sg here
}

# Use separate rules
resource "aws_security_group_rule" "web_to_db" {
  type                     = "egress"
  security_group_id        = aws_security_group.web.id
  source_security_group_id = aws_security_group.db.id
  from_port                = 5432
  to_port                  = 5432
  protocol                 = "tcp"
}

🔴 Error: Resource Not Found

# Error: Error: resource "aws_instance.web" not found in state
# Cause: Resource deleted outside Terraform or state mismatch

# Solution 1: Remove from state if no longer needed
terraform state rm aws_instance.web

# Solution 2: Import if resource exists in AWS
terraform import aws_instance.web i-0abc123def456

# Solution 3: Refresh state to detect changes
terraform refresh

# Find resource ID in AWS Console or CLI
aws ec2 describe-instances --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=anihpj-web"

🔴 Error: Permission Denied / Access Denied

# Error: AccessDenied: User is not authorized to perform: ec2:CreateVpc
# Cause: IAM policy missing required permissions

# Debug: Check current identity
aws sts get-caller-identity

# Debug: Simulate IAM policy
aws iam simulate-principal-policy \
  --policy-source-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789:user/terraform \
  --action-names ec2:CreateVpc

# Solution: Add missing IAM permissions
{
  "Effect": "Allow",
  "Action": [
    "ec2:CreateVpc",
    "ec2:DeleteVpc",
    "ec2:DescribeVpcs"
  ],
  "Resource": "*"
}

🔴 Error: Rate Limiting / Throttling

# Error: ThrottlingException: Rate exceeded
# Cause: Too many API calls to AWS

# Solution 1: Reduce parallelism
terraform apply -parallelism=5

# Solution 2: Add retry configuration in provider
provider "aws" {
  region      = var.aws_region
  retry_mode  = "adaptive"
  max_retries = 10
}

# Solution 3: Run during off-peak hours
# Solution 4: Request limit increase from AWS Support

15.2 State Issues

State management is critical. Learn to identify and resolve state-related problems.

💔 State Corruption
Invalid JSON, missing resources, partial writes
🔄 State Drift
Real infrastructure differs from state
🔗 Out of Sync
State doesn't match code or AWS
🗑️ Lost State
State file deleted or inaccessible
state-issues.sh - Diagnosing State Problems 
# =========================================
# State Corruption Recovery
# =========================================

# Check state file validity
terraform state list

# If error, restore from S3 versioning
aws s3api list-object-versions \
  --bucket anihpj-terraform-state \
  --prefix prod/terraform.tfstate

# Download previous version
aws s3api get-object \
  --bucket anihpj-terraform-state \
  --key prod/terraform.tfstate \
  --version-id "versionId123" \
  recovered-state.tfstate

# Verify and push
terraform state push recovered-state.tfstate

# =========================================
# State Drift Detection
# =========================================

# Detect drift (refresh without applying)
terraform plan -refresh-only

# Common drift causes:
# - Manual AWS Console changes
# - Other tools modifying resources
# - Auto-scaling events

# Sync state to reality
terraform apply -refresh-only -auto-approve

# =========================================
# State Out of Sync
# =========================================

# Force refresh
terraform refresh

# Remove stale resource from state
terraform state rm aws_instance.old_server

# Import existing resource
terraform import aws_s3_bucket.media my-bucket-name

✅ State Health Checklist

☑️ S3 versioning enabled
☑️ DynamoDB locking active
☑️ KMS encryption on
☑️ Backup automation

15.3 Provider Issues

Provider-related issues can block all operations. Here's how to resolve them.

🔢 Version Conflicts
Module requires different provider version than root.
terraform init -upgrade
📦 Provider Caching
Stale provider causing unexpected behavior.
rm -rf .terraform && terraform init
⏱️ Provider Timeouts
Long-running operations failing.
timeouts { create = "60m" }
🚦 API Rate Limits
Too many API calls, AWS throttling.
-parallelism=5
provider-fixes.tf - Provider Configuration 
# =========================================
# Version Constraint Best Practices
# =========================================

terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.5.0"
  
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"  # Allows 5.x, not 6.0
    }
  }
}

# =========================================
# Provider Timeout Configuration
# =========================================

resource "aws_db_instance" "main" {
  # ... configuration ...
  
  timeouts {
    create = "60m"  # RDS can take a while
    update = "60m"
    delete = "30m"
  }
}

resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" {
  # ... configuration ...
  
  timeouts {
    create = "30m"
    update = "60m"
    delete = "15m"
  }
}

# =========================================
# Rate Limiting Mitigation
# =========================================

provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region
  
  # Add retry configuration
  retry_mode  = "adaptive"
  max_retries = 10
}

15.4 Performance Issues

Optimize Terraform operations for faster plan and apply cycles.

⚡ Performance Optimization Levers

🔀
Parallelism
Default: 10
📦
State Size
Split large states
🎯
Targeting
-target flag
🔄
Refresh
-refresh=false
performance.sh - Speed Optimizations 
# =========================================
# Parallelism Tuning
# =========================================

# Increase parallelism (careful with rate limits)
terraform apply -parallelism=20

# Decrease for rate-limited APIs
terraform apply -parallelism=5

# =========================================
# Large State Optimization
# =========================================

# Check state size
ls -lh terraform.tfstate

# List resources in state
terraform state list | wc -l

# If > 500 resources, consider splitting:
# - By environment (dev/staging/prod)
# - By component (network/compute/data)
# - By team ownership

# =========================================
# Targeted Operations
# =========================================

# Plan only specific resource (for debugging)
terraform plan -target=aws_instance.web

# Apply only one module
terraform apply -target=module.vpc

# ⚠️ Warning: Always run full plan after targeted apply

# =========================================
# Skip Refresh (CI/CD optimization)
# =========================================

# Skip refresh when you know state is current
terraform plan -refresh=false

# Useful in CI when state just updated

💡 Performance Pro Tips

✓ Use -compact-warnings to reduce output
✓ Enable provider caching in CI/CD
✓ Split monolithic configs into modules
✓ Use data sources sparingly in loops

15.5 Debugging Techniques

Terminal - Debug Commands 
# =========================================
# Enable Verbose Logging
# =========================================

# Log levels: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR
export TF_LOG=DEBUG

# Log to file
export TF_LOG_PATH="./terraform.log"

# Provider-specific logging
export TF_LOG_PROVIDER=DEBUG
export TF_LOG_CORE=TRACE

# Run with logging
terraform plan

# Analyze logs
grep -i "error\|warn" terraform.log

# =========================================
# Debug Expressions
# =========================================

# Use terraform console
terraform console

> var.environment
"development"

> local.name_prefix
"anihpj-dev"

> aws_vpc.main.id
"vpc-0abc123"

> length(var.availability_zones)
3

# =========================================
# Visualize Dependencies
# =========================================

# Generate graph
terraform graph > graph.dot

# Filter for specific resource
terraform graph | grep -E "(aws_instance|aws_security)"

# Convert to image (requires graphviz)
terraform graph | dot -Tsvg > graph.svg

📊 TF_LOG Levels (Most → Least Verbose)

TRACE
DEBUG
INFO
WARN
ERROR

🖥️ terraform console Cheat Sheet

var.environment → Check variable values
local.name_prefix → Evaluate locals
aws_vpc.main.id → Get resource attrs
length(var.subnets) → Test functions

🎯 Resource Targeting

Target specific resources for debugging or partial applies. Use with caution!

# Target single resource
terraform plan -target=aws_instance.web
terraform apply -target=aws_instance.web

# Target entire module
terraform plan -target=module.vpc

# Target multiple resources
terraform plan -target=aws_instance.web -target=aws_security_group.web

# Target with index (for count/for_each)
terraform plan -target='aws_subnet.private[0]'
terraform plan -target='aws_subnet.private["us-east-1a"]'

# ⚠️ WARNING: Always run full plan after targeted operations
# Targeted applies can create inconsistent state

📊 terraform graph Analysis

Default
terraform graph
Plan Graph
-type=plan
Apply Graph
-type=apply 
# Generate and view dependency graph
terraform graph | dot -Tpng > graph.png

# Filter for specific resources (find cycles)
terraform graph | grep -E "aws_security_group"

# Generate SVG for better zoom
terraform graph | dot -Tsvg > graph.svg

# Use online viewer: https://dreampuf.github.io/GraphvizOnline/

15.6 Recovery Procedures

When things go wrong, follow these procedures to recover.

🟢 Minor: State Drift
terraform apply -refresh-only
🟡 Medium: Resource Mismatch
terraform import / state rm
🟠 High: State Corruption
Restore from S3 version
🔴 Critical: Lost State
Rebuild with imports
recovery-procedures.sh - Disaster Recovery 
# =========================================
# State Recovery from S3 Backup
# =========================================

# Step 1: List state versions (S3 versioning must be enabled)
aws s3api list-object-versions \
  --bucket anihpj-terraform-state \
  --prefix prod/terraform.tfstate

# Step 2: Download specific version
aws s3api get-object \
  --bucket anihpj-terraform-state \
  --key prod/terraform.tfstate \
  --version-id "VERSION_ID_HERE" \
  recovered.tfstate

# Step 3: Verify recovered state
terraform state list -state=recovered.tfstate

# Step 4: Push recovered state
terraform state push recovered.tfstate

# =========================================
# Manual State Editing (Last Resort!)
# =========================================

# Pull state locally
terraform state pull > terraform.tfstate.backup

# Edit (BE VERY CAREFUL)
# Only modify if you understand JSON schema

# Push edited state
terraform state push terraform.tfstate.backup

# =========================================
# Recreating Resources (Nuclear Option)
# =========================================

# 1. Tag all resources for discovery
aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources \
  --tag-filters Key=Project,Values=anihpj \
  --output json > resources.json

# 2. Generate import blocks (Terraform 1.5+)
# Create imports.tf with import blocks
import {
  to = aws_s3_bucket.media
  id = "anihpj-media-bucket"
}

# 3. Generate config from imports
terraform plan -generate-config-out=recovered.tf

# 4. Review and adjust generated code
# 5. Run terraform apply

🐍 Django anihpj Disaster Recovery Runbook

Step 1: Database
Restore RDS from automated snapshot
Step 2: State
Recover Terraform state from S3 versions
Step 3: Secrets
Verify Secrets Manager values intact
Step 4: Redeploy
terraform apply → ECS deploy
⚠️
Prevention is Better Than Recovery
  • Always enable S3 versioning on state bucket
  • Use DynamoDB locking to prevent conflicts
  • Never manually edit state files directly
  • Set up automated state backup to secondary region

🚨 Error Severity & Response Matrix

Error Type Severity Impact First Response
State Lock Medium Blocks deploys Check DynamoDB lock
State Corruption Critical Full outage risk Restore from S3 version
Auth Failure High All ops blocked aws sts get-caller-identity
Cycle Detected Medium Plan fails terraform graph analysis
Rate Limiting Low Slow deploys Reduce parallelism
State Drift Medium Unexpected changes terraform plan -refresh-only

⚡ Quick Fix Command Reference

🔓 Force Unlock State
terraform force-unlock LOCK_ID
🔄 Refresh State
terraform apply -refresh-only
📦 Import Resource
terraform import TYPE.NAME ID
🗑️ Remove from State
terraform state rm TYPE.NAME
🔍 Enable Debug Logs
export TF_LOG=DEBUG
🎯 Target Resource
terraform plan -target=TYPE.NAME

🔧 Systematic Troubleshooting Flow

1️⃣ Read Error
→
2️⃣ Check Logs
→
3️⃣ Verify State
↓
4️⃣ Check AWS
→
5️⃣ Isolate Issue
→
6️⃣ Apply Fix ✓

💡 Troubleshooting Pro Tips

📋 Save Plan Output
Always save plans for CI/CD: terraform plan -out=tfplan
🧪 Test in Console
Debug expressions before apply: terraform console
📊 Graph Dependencies
Find cycles visually: terraform graph | dot -Tpng
🔒 Check Identity First
Verify credentials: aws sts get-caller-identity
🗃️ State Backup First
Before risky ops: terraform state pull > backup.tfstate
⏱️ Set Longer Timeouts
For slow resources: timeouts { create = "60m" }

📋 Troubleshooting Quick Reference

🔍 Diagnosis Commands
✓ terraform validate
✓ terraform plan
✓ terraform state list
✓ terraform graph
✓ terraform console
🛠️ Fix Commands
✓ terraform init -upgrade
✓ terraform refresh
✓ terraform state rm
✓ terraform import
✓ terraform force-unlock
🌐 AWS Debug Commands
✓ aws sts get-caller-identity
✓ aws s3api list-object-versions
✓ aws dynamodb get-item
✓ aws iam simulate-principal-policy
✓ aws resourcegroupstaggingapi
⚙️ Environment Variables
✓ TF_LOG=DEBUG
✓ TF_LOG_PATH=./tf.log
✓ TF_LOG_PROVIDER=DEBUG
✓ TF_LOG_CORE=TRACE
✓ AWS_PROFILE=name

✅ Section 15 Key Takeaways

🔴 Errors
Read carefully, check logs, verify credentials
🗄️ State
Always backup, use versioning, never edit manually
🔧 Debug
TF_LOG for verbosity, console for testing, graph for deps
🚀 Recovery
S3 versions, import blocks, systematic approach

16. Django-Specific Infrastructure Patterns

🐍

Django + Terraform

Production-Ready AWS Infrastructure

Complete, battle-tested patterns for deploying the anihpj Django project with the jobpost app to AWS using Infrastructure as Code.

✓ VPC + Subnets ✓ ECS Fargate ✓ RDS PostgreSQL ✓ ElastiCache Redis ✓ S3 + CloudFront ✓ CI/CD Pipeline
🏗️
Complete Stack
🔐
Environment Vars
📦
Static Files
🗄️
Database
⚙️
Celery Workers
🚀
CI/CD Pipeline
📊
Monitoring
💰
Cost Optimization
🔒
Security

🐍 anihpj Django Stack Components

🌐 Web Tier
ALB + CloudFront + Route 53
🐍 App Tier
ECS Fargate + Django + Gunicorn
🗄️ Data Tier
RDS PostgreSQL + ElastiCache Redis
📦 Storage Tier
S3 Static + S3 Media
⚙️ Workers
Celery + SQS/Redis + Flower
🔐 Security
Secrets Manager + SSM + ACM

16.1 Complete Django Stack on AWS

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        AWS Cloud - anihpj                          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                     │
│    ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐                          │
│    │   Route 53   │────▶│  CloudFront  │                          │
│    │   DNS Zone   │     │     CDN      │                          │
│    └──────────────┘     └───────┬──────┘                          │
│                                 │                                   │
│         ┌───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┐          │
│         ▼                       ▼                       │          │
│    ┌─────────┐           ┌─────────────┐          ┌─────────┐     │
│    │   S3    │           │     ALB     │          │   S3    │     │
│    │ Static  │           │   (HTTPS)   │          │  Media  │     │
│    └─────────┘           └──────┬──────┘          └─────────┘     │
│                                 │                                   │
│    ┌────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┐         │
│    │                     VPC - 10.0.0.0/16               │         │
│    │  ┌──────────────────┬──────┴──────┬──────────────┐  │         │
│    │  │   Public Subnet  │             │ Public Subnet│  │         │
│    │  │    10.0.1.0/24   │             │  10.0.2.0/24 │  │         │
│    │  │  ┌─────────────┐ │             │┌───────────┐ │  │         │
│    │  │  │   NAT GW    │ │             ││  NAT GW   │ │  │         │
│    │  │  └─────────────┘ │             │└───────────┘ │  │         │
│    │  └──────────────────┴─────────────┴──────────────┘  │         │
│    │  ┌──────────────────┬─────────────┬──────────────┐  │         │
│    │  │  Private Subnet  │             │Private Subnet│  │         │
│    │  │   10.0.10.0/24   │             │ 10.0.20.0/24 │  │         │
│    │  │  ┌─────────────┐ │             │┌───────────┐ │  │         │
│    │  │  │    ECS      │ │             ││   ECS     │ │  │         │
│    │  │  │  (Django)   │ │             ││ (Celery)  │ │  │         │
│    │  │  └─────────────┘ │             │└───────────┘ │  │         │
│    │  └──────────────────┴─────────────┴──────────────┘  │         │
│    │  ┌──────────────────┬─────────────┬──────────────┐  │         │
│    │  │    DB Subnet     │             │  DB Subnet   │  │         │
│    │  │   10.0.100.0/24  │             │ 10.0.200.0/24│  │         │
│    │  │  ┌─────────────┐ │             │┌───────────┐ │  │         │
│    │  │  │    RDS      │ │◀───────────▶││ElastiCache│ │  │         │
│    │  │  │ (PostgreSQL)│ │             ││  (Redis)  │ │  │         │
│    │  │  └─────────────┘ │             │└───────────┘ │  │         │
│    │  └──────────────────┴─────────────┴──────────────┘  │         │
│    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘         │
│                                                                     │
│    ┌───────────────────┐  ┌───────────────────┐                    │
│    │  Secrets Manager  │  │    CloudWatch     │                    │
│    │   Django Secrets  │  │   Logs/Metrics    │                    │
│    └───────────────────┘  └───────────────────┘                    │
│                                                                     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
main.tf - Complete Django Infrastructure 
# =========================================
# Complete Django (anihpj) Infrastructure
# =========================================

terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.5"

  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }

  backend "s3" {
    bucket         = "anihpj-terraform-state"
    key            = "prod/terraform.tfstate"
    region         = "us-east-1"
    encrypt        = true
    dynamodb_table = "terraform-locks"
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region

  default_tags {
    tags = {
      Project     = "anihpj"
      Application = "jobpost"
      ManagedBy   = "Terraform"
      Environment = var.environment
    }
  }
}

# =========================================
# Networking Module
# =========================================
module "vpc" {
  source = "./modules/vpc"

  project_name        = "anihpj"
  environment         = var.environment
  vpc_cidr            = "10.0.0.0/16"
  availability_zones  = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"]

  public_subnet_cidrs  = ["10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.2.0/24"]
  private_subnet_cidrs = ["10.0.10.0/24", "10.0.20.0/24"]
  db_subnet_cidrs      = ["10.0.100.0/24", "10.0.200.0/24"]
}

# =========================================
# Database Module (for JobPost models)
# =========================================
module "database" {
  source = "./modules/rds-postgres"

  project_name   = "anihpj"
  environment    = var.environment
  vpc_id         = module.vpc.vpc_id
  db_subnet_ids  = module.vpc.db_subnet_ids

  db_name     = "anihpj"
  db_username = "anihpj_admin"
  # Password from Secrets Manager

  instance_class    = var.environment == "production" ? "db.r6g.large" : "db.t3.micro"
  allocated_storage = var.environment == "production" ? 100 : 20
  multi_az          = var.environment == "production"

  allowed_security_groups = [module.ecs.service_security_group_id]
}

# =========================================
# Cache Module (Django Cache + Celery)
# =========================================
module "elasticache" {
  source = "./modules/elasticache-redis"

  project_name  = "anihpj"
  environment   = var.environment
  vpc_id        = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids    = module.vpc.db_subnet_ids

  node_type       = var.environment == "production" ? "cache.r6g.large" : "cache.t3.micro"
  num_cache_nodes = var.environment == "production" ? 2 : 1

  allowed_security_groups = [module.ecs.service_security_group_id]
}

# =========================================
# Storage Module (Static & Media)
# =========================================
module "storage" {
  source = "./modules/s3-django"

  project_name = "anihpj"
  environment  = var.environment

  enable_static_hosting = true
  enable_media_bucket   = true

  cloudfront_oai_arn = module.cdn.origin_access_identity_arn
}

# =========================================
# ECS Module (Django + Celery)
# =========================================
module "ecs" {
  source = "./modules/ecs-django"

  project_name = "anihpj"
  environment  = var.environment
  vpc_id       = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids   = module.vpc.private_subnet_ids

  # Django Service
  django_image     = "${aws_ecr_repository.django.repository_url}:${var.image_tag}"
  django_cpu       = var.environment == "production" ? 512 : 256
  django_memory    = var.environment == "production" ? 1024 : 512
  django_count     = var.environment == "production" ? 2 : 1

  # Celery Worker
  celery_image     = "${aws_ecr_repository.django.repository_url}:${var.image_tag}"
  celery_cpu       = var.environment == "production" ? 512 : 256
  celery_memory    = var.environment == "production" ? 1024 : 512
  celery_count     = var.environment == "production" ? 2 : 1

  # Load Balancer
  alb_security_group_id = module.alb.security_group_id
  target_group_arn      = module.alb.target_group_arn

  # Environment Variables
  environment_variables = {
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "anihpj.settings"
    DATABASE_URL           = module.database.connection_url
    REDIS_URL              = module.elasticache.connection_url
    AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET     = module.storage.static_bucket_name
    AWS_S3_REGION          = var.aws_region
  }

  # Secrets
  secrets = {
    SECRET_KEY = module.secrets.django_secret_key_arn
    DB_PASSWORD = module.database.password_secret_arn
  }
}

# =========================================
# Load Balancer Module
# =========================================
module "alb" {
  source = "./modules/alb"

  project_name = "anihpj"
  environment  = var.environment
  vpc_id       = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids   = module.vpc.public_subnet_ids

  certificate_arn = module.acm.certificate_arn
  health_check_path = "/health/"
}

# =========================================
# CDN Module
# =========================================
module "cdn" {
  source = "./modules/cloudfront"

  project_name = "anihpj"
  environment  = var.environment

  alb_domain_name     = module.alb.dns_name
  static_bucket_domain = module.storage.static_bucket_domain
  media_bucket_domain  = module.storage.media_bucket_domain

  certificate_arn = module.acm_us_east_1.certificate_arn
  aliases         = var.domain_names
}

# =========================================
# DNS Module
# =========================================
module "dns" {
  source = "./modules/route53"

  domain_name            = var.root_domain
  cloudfront_domain_name = module.cdn.distribution_domain
  cloudfront_zone_id     = module.cdn.distribution_hosted_zone_id
}

16.2 Django Environment Variables with Terraform

🔐 Environment Variable Sources

🔑
Secrets Manager
SECRET_KEY, DB_PASS
⚙️
SSM Parameters
DEBUG, LOG_LEVEL
📋
Task Def Env
Non-sensitive
🔄
Auto Rotation
30-day cycle
All injected into ECS container at runtime → os.environ.get()
secrets.tf - Django Secrets Management 
# =========================================
# Django Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager
# =========================================

# Generate Django SECRET_KEY
resource "random_password" "django_secret_key" {
  length  = 50
  special = true
}

# Store Django secrets
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "django" {
  name                    = "anihpj/${var.environment}/django"
  recovery_window_in_days = var.environment == "production" ? 30 : 0

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Component   = "django-settings"
  }
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "django" {
  secret_id = aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.id

  secret_string = jsonencode({
    SECRET_KEY          = random_password.django_secret_key.result
    DATABASE_URL        = "postgres://${var.db_username}:${random_password.db_password.result}@${aws_db_instance.main.endpoint}/${var.db_name}"
    REDIS_URL           = "redis://${aws_elasticache_cluster.main.cache_nodes[0].address}:6379/0"

    # Email settings (for jobpost notifications)
    EMAIL_HOST          = "email-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com"
    EMAIL_HOST_USER     = aws_iam_access_key.ses.id
    EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD = aws_iam_access_key.ses.secret

    # AWS settings
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID     = aws_iam_access_key.django_app.id
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = aws_iam_access_key.django_app.secret
    AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET    = aws_s3_bucket.static.id
    AWS_S3_REGION         = var.aws_region

    # Social auth (for Author model)
    SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_KEY    = var.github_oauth_key
    SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET = var.github_oauth_secret
  })
}

# =========================================
# ECS Task Definition with Secrets
# =========================================

resource "aws_ecs_task_definition" "django" {
  family                   = "anihpj-django"
  requires_compatibilities = ["FARGATE"]
  network_mode             = "awsvpc"
  cpu                      = 512
  memory                   = 1024
  execution_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.arn
  task_role_arn            = aws_iam_role.ecs_task.arn

  container_definitions = jsonencode([
    {
      name  = "django"
      image = "${aws_ecr_repository.django.repository_url}:${var.image_tag}"

      portMappings = [{
        containerPort = 8000
        protocol      = "tcp"
      }]

      # Non-sensitive environment variables
      environment = [
        { name = "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", value = "anihpj.settings" },
        { name = "ALLOWED_HOSTS", value = var.allowed_hosts },
        { name = "DEBUG", value = tostring(var.environment != "production") },
      ]

      # Sensitive values from Secrets Manager
      secrets = [
        {
          name      = "SECRET_KEY"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:SECRET_KEY::"
        },
        {
          name      = "DATABASE_URL"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:DATABASE_URL::"
        },
        {
          name      = "REDIS_URL"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:REDIS_URL::"
        },
      ]

      logConfiguration = {
        logDriver = "awslogs"
        options = {
          awslogs-group         = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.django.name
          awslogs-region        = var.aws_region
          awslogs-stream-prefix = "django"
        }
      }
    }
  ])
}
anihpj/settings.py - Reading AWS Secrets 
# anihpj/settings.py - Production settings with AWS

import os
import json
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

def get_secret(secret_name: str) -> dict:
    """Retrieve secrets from AWS Secrets Manager."""
    session = boto3.session.Session()
    client = session.client(
        service_name='secretsmanager',
        region_name=os.environ.get('AWS_REGION', 'us-east-1')
    )

    try:
        response = client.get_secret_value(SecretId=secret_name)
        return json.loads(response['SecretString'])
    except ClientError as e:
        raise Exception(f"Failed to retrieve secret: {e}")

# Load secrets in production
if os.environ.get('DJANGO_ENV') == 'production':
    secrets = get_secret(f"anihpj/{os.environ['ENVIRONMENT']}/django")

    SECRET_KEY = secrets['SECRET_KEY']

    # Database for JobPost, Skills, Author, Location models
    import dj_database_url
    DATABASES = {
        'default': dj_database_url.parse(secrets['DATABASE_URL'])
    }

    # Cache configuration
    CACHES = {
        'default': {
            'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
            'LOCATION': secrets['REDIS_URL'],
            'OPTIONS': {
                'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            }
        }
    }

    # S3 Static files
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = secrets['AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID']
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = secrets['AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY']
    AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = secrets['AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET']
    AWS_S3_REGION_NAME = secrets['AWS_S3_REGION']
    STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto3.S3Boto3Storage'
else:
    # Development settings
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get('SECRET_KEY', 'dev-secret-key')
    DATABASES = {
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': BASE_DIR / 'db.sqlite3',
        }
    }
ssm-parameters.tf - Hierarchical Parameters 
# =========================================
# SSM Parameter Store - Hierarchical Config
# =========================================

# Standard Parameters (free tier - up to 10,000)
resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "django_debug" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/django/debug"
  description = "Django DEBUG setting"
  type        = "String"
  value       = var.environment == "production" ? "False" : "True"

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "allowed_hosts" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/django/allowed_hosts"
  description = "Django ALLOWED_HOSTS setting"
  type        = "StringList"
  value       = join(",", var.allowed_hosts)

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# Advanced Parameters (for larger values up to 8KB)
resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "django_settings" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/django/full_settings"
  description = "Complete Django settings JSON"
  type        = "String"
  tier        = "Advanced"  # Required for values > 4KB

  value = jsonencode({
    debug              = var.environment != "production"
    allowed_hosts      = var.allowed_hosts
    time_zone          = "UTC"
    language_code      = "en-us"
    static_url         = "/static/"
    media_url          = "/media/"
    csrf_cookie_secure = var.environment == "production"
    session_cookie_secure = var.environment == "production"
  })

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# SecureString for sensitive non-rotatable values
resource "aws_ssm_parameter" "api_keys" {
  name        = "/anihpj/${var.environment}/api_keys/maps"
  description = "Google Maps API Key"
  type        = "SecureString"
  value       = var.google_maps_api_key
  key_id      = aws_kms_key.ssm.key_id

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Sensitive   = "true"
  }
}

# Version tracking with data source
data "aws_ssm_parameter" "django_debug" {
  name            = aws_ssm_parameter.django_debug.name
  with_decryption = false
}

# Output current version
output "parameter_version" {
  value = data.aws_ssm_parameter.django_debug.version
}
secret-rotation.tf - Automatic Secret Rotation 
# =========================================
# Secrets Manager with Automatic Rotation
# =========================================

# Database password with rotation
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password_rotated" {
  name                    = "anihpj/${var.environment}/db-password-rotated"
  recovery_window_in_days = var.environment == "production" ? 30 : 0

  # Enable multi-region replication for DR
  dynamic "replica" {
    for_each = var.environment == "production" ? ["us-west-2"] : []
    content {
      region = replica.value
    }
  }

  tags = {
    Application  = "anihpj"
    RotationEnabled = "true"
  }
}

# Initial secret value
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password_initial" {
  secret_id = aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password_rotated.id
  secret_string = jsonencode({
    username = "anihpj_admin"
    password = random_password.db_password.result
    engine   = "postgres"
    host     = aws_db_instance.main.address
    port     = 5432
    dbname   = "anihpj"
  })
}

# Rotation configuration
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation" "db_password" {
  secret_id           = aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password_rotated.id
  rotation_lambda_arn = aws_lambda_function.secret_rotator.arn

  rotation_rules {
    automatically_after_days = 30
    schedule_expression      = "rate(30 days)"
  }
}

# Lambda function for rotation
resource "aws_lambda_function" "secret_rotator" {
  filename         = "lambda/secret-rotator.zip"
  function_name    = "anihpj-${var.environment}-secret-rotator"
  role             = aws_iam_role.lambda_rotator.arn
  handler          = "rotate_secret.handler"
  runtime          = "python3.11"
  timeout          = 30

  environment {
    variables = {
      SECRETS_MANAGER_ENDPOINT = "https://secretsmanager.${var.aws_region}.amazonaws.com"
    }
  }

  vpc_config {
    subnet_ids         = module.vpc.private_subnet_ids
    security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.lambda.id]
  }
}

# Lambda permission for Secrets Manager
resource "aws_lambda_permission" "secrets_manager" {
  statement_id  = "AllowSecretsManagerInvoke"
  action        = "lambda:InvokeFunction"
  function_name = aws_lambda_function.secret_rotator.function_name
  principal     = "secretsmanager.amazonaws.com"
}

📋 Environment Configuration Patterns

Parameter Path Development Staging Production
/anihpj/{env}/django/debug True True False
/anihpj/{env}/db/instance_class db.t3.micro db.t3.small db.r6g.large
/anihpj/{env}/cache/node_type cache.t3.micro cache.t3.small cache.r6g.large
/anihpj/{env}/ecs/task_count 1 2 4+
/anihpj/{env}/ssl/enabled Optional Required Required
env-config.tf - Variable Inheritance Pattern 
# =========================================
# Environment Variable Inheritance Pattern
# =========================================

# Base configuration (shared across all environments)
locals {
  base_config = {
    project_name     = "anihpj"
    aws_region       = "us-east-1"
    log_retention    = 30
    health_check_path = "/health/"

    # Django base settings
    django = {
      wsgi_application = "anihpj.wsgi.application"
      time_zone        = "UTC"
      language_code    = "en-us"
      use_i18n         = true
      use_tz           = true
    }
  }

  # Environment-specific overrides
  env_overrides = {
    development = {
      debug            = true
      log_level        = "DEBUG"
      db_multi_az      = false
      db_instance      = "db.t3.micro"
      cache_nodes      = 1
      min_tasks        = 1
      max_tasks        = 2
      backup_retention = 7
      deletion_protection = false
    }

    staging = {
      debug            = true
      log_level        = "INFO"
      db_multi_az      = false
      db_instance      = "db.t3.small"
      cache_nodes      = 1
      min_tasks        = 2
      max_tasks        = 4
      backup_retention = 14
      deletion_protection = false
    }

    production = {
      debug            = false
      log_level        = "WARNING"
      db_multi_az      = true
      db_instance      = "db.r6g.large"
      cache_nodes      = 2
      min_tasks        = 4
      max_tasks        = 20
      backup_retention = 35
      deletion_protection = true
    }
  }

  # Merged configuration with inheritance
  config = merge(
    local.base_config,
    local.env_overrides[var.environment]
  )
}

# Usage in resources
resource "aws_db_instance" "main" {
  instance_class       = local.config.db_instance
  multi_az             = local.config.db_multi_az
  backup_retention_period = local.config.backup_retention
  deletion_protection  = local.config.deletion_protection
  # ... other settings
}

16.4 Django Database Infrastructure

🗄️ Django Database Architecture

🐍
Django App
ORM Queries
↓ Write ↙ Read
🗄️
Primary RDS
Multi-AZ
↓ Replication
📖
Read Replica
Reports/Analytics
🔐 Encrypted at Rest
⏰ PITR 35 Days
🌍 Cross-Region Backup
rds.tf - PostgreSQL for Django Models 
# =========================================
# RDS PostgreSQL for anihpj Django Models
# Supports: JobPost, Skills, Author, Location
# =========================================

# Generate database password
resource "random_password" "db_password" {
  length  = 32
  special = false  # RDS special char limitations
}

# Store password in Secrets Manager
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "db_password" {
  name = "anihpj/${var.environment}/db-password"
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db_password" {
  secret_id     = aws_secretsmanager_secret.db_password.id
  secret_string = random_password.db_password.result
}

# Parameter group optimized for Django
resource "aws_db_parameter_group" "django" {
  name   = "anihpj-${var.environment}-django"
  family = "postgres15"

  # Django-optimized parameters
  parameter {
    name  = "log_statement"
    value = "all"
  }

  parameter {
    name  = "log_min_duration_statement"
    value = "1000"  # Log queries > 1 second
  }

  # Connection pooling for Django
  parameter {
    name  = "max_connections"
    value = "200"
  }

  # Full-text search for JobPost.description
  parameter {
    name  = "default_text_search_config"
    value = "pg_catalog.english"
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-django-params"
  }
}

# RDS Instance
resource "aws_db_instance" "main" {
  identifier = "anihpj-${var.environment}"

  # Engine
  engine               = "postgres"
  engine_version       = "15.4"
  instance_class       = var.db_instance_class
  parameter_group_name = aws_db_parameter_group.django.name

  # Storage
  allocated_storage     = var.db_allocated_storage
  max_allocated_storage = var.db_max_storage
  storage_type          = "gp3"
  storage_encrypted     = true
  kms_key_id            = aws_kms_key.rds.arn

  # Database
  db_name  = "anihpj"
  username = "anihpj_admin"
  password = random_password.db_password.result

  # Network
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.main.name
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.rds.id]
  publicly_accessible    = false
  port                   = 5432

  # High Availability (production)
  multi_az = var.environment == "production"

  # Backup
  backup_retention_period = var.environment == "production" ? 35 : 7
  backup_window           = "03:00-04:00"
  maintenance_window      = "Mon:04:00-Mon:05:00"

  # Performance Insights
  performance_insights_enabled          = var.environment == "production"
  performance_insights_retention_period = 7

  # Monitoring
  monitoring_interval = 60
  monitoring_role_arn = aws_iam_role.rds_monitoring.arn

  # Deletion protection
  deletion_protection       = var.environment == "production"
  skip_final_snapshot       = var.environment != "production"
  final_snapshot_identifier = var.environment == "production" ? "anihpj-final-${formatdate("YYYY-MM-DD", timestamp())}" : null

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-postgres"
    Application = "jobpost"
    Models      = "JobPost,Skills,Author,Location"
  }

  lifecycle {
    prevent_destroy = true  # Extra protection
  }
}

# Read Replica (production only)
resource "aws_db_instance" "replica" {
  count = var.environment == "production" ? 1 : 0

  identifier          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-replica"
  replicate_source_db = aws_db_instance.main.identifier

  instance_class = var.db_replica_instance_class
  multi_az       = false

  # Replicas don't need backup
  backup_retention_period = 0
  skip_final_snapshot     = true

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-${var.environment}-replica"
    Role = "read-replica"
  }
}

# Output connection string for Django
output "database_url" {
  value = "postgres://${aws_db_instance.main.username}:${random_password.db_password.result}@${aws_db_instance.main.endpoint}/${aws_db_instance.main.db_name}"
  sensitive = true
}
anihpj/db_router.py - Read Replica Router 
# anihpj/db_router.py
# Django Database Router for Read/Write Splitting

class PrimaryReplicaRouter:
    """
    Route read queries to replica, writes to primary.
    For anihpj JobPost model and related models.
    """

    def db_for_read(self, model, **hints):
        """Direct all read operations to the replica."""
        return 'replica'

    def db_for_write(self, model, **hints):
        """Direct all write operations to the primary."""
        return 'default'

    def allow_relation(self, obj1, obj2, **hints):
        """Allow relations between databases."""
        db_list = ('default', 'replica')
        if obj1._state.db in db_list and obj2._state.db in db_list:
            return True
        return None

    def allow_migrate(self, db, app_label, model_name=None, **hints):
        """Only migrate on the primary database."""
        return db == 'default'


# =========================================
# anihpj/settings.py - Database Configuration
# =========================================
DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',
        'HOST': os.environ.get('DB_PRIMARY_HOST'),
        'PORT': 5432,
        'NAME': 'anihpj',
        'USER': os.environ.get('DB_USER'),
        'PASSWORD': os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD'),
    },
    'replica': {
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',
        'HOST': os.environ.get('DB_REPLICA_HOST'),
        'PORT': 5432,
        'NAME': 'anihpj',
        'USER': os.environ.get('DB_USER'),
        'PASSWORD': os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD'),
    }
}

DATABASE_ROUTERS = ['anihpj.db_router.PrimaryReplicaRouter']
migration-strategy.md - Zero-Downtime Migrations 
# Zero-Downtime Migration Strategy for anihpj

## Phase 1: Schema-Safe Changes (Always Safe)
- Add nullable columns: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... NULL
- Add indexes concurrently: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY
- Add new tables

## Phase 2: Two-Step Column Operations

### Adding a NOT NULL column:
1. Deploy: Add column as nullable
2. Backfill: UPDATE in batches
3. Deploy: Add NOT NULL constraint

### Example for JobPost model:
```python
# Migration 1: Add nullable column
operations = [
    migrations.AddField(
        model_name='jobpost',
        name='remote_allowed',
        field=models.BooleanField(null=True, default=None),
    ),
]

# Migration 2: Backfill data
def backfill_remote_allowed(apps, schema_editor):
    JobPost = apps.get_model('jobpost', 'JobPost')
    JobPost.objects.filter(remote_allowed__isnull=True).update(remote_allowed=False)

operations = [
    migrations.RunPython(backfill_remote_allowed, migrations.RunPython.noop),
]

# Migration 3: Make NOT NULL (separate deployment)
operations = [
    migrations.AlterField(
        model_name='jobpost',
        name='remote_allowed',
        field=models.BooleanField(default=False),
    ),
]
```

## Phase 3: Column Removal (3-Step Process)
1. Stop using column in code (deploy)
2. Wait for all old code to drain
3. Remove column in migration (deploy)

## Django migrate --fake Rollback
```bash
# Rollback migration (schema already changed)
python manage.py migrate jobpost 0005 --fake

# Fix data manually, then re-migrate
python manage.py migrate jobpost
```
backup-recovery.tf - PITR & Cross-Region 
# =========================================
# Point-in-Time Recovery & Cross-Region Backup
# =========================================

# Enable automated backups (already in main RDS config)
# backup_retention_period = 35

# Restore from point-in-time (manual process via console/CLI)
# aws rds restore-db-instance-to-point-in-time \
#   --source-db-instance-identifier anihpj-production \
#   --target-db-instance-identifier anihpj-restored \
#   --restore-time "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"

# =========================================
# Cross-Region Automated Backup Replication
# =========================================

resource "aws_db_instance_automated_backups_replication" "cross_region" {
  count = var.environment == "production" ? 1 : 0

  source_db_instance_arn = aws_db_instance.main.arn
  retention_period       = 14
  kms_key_id             = aws_kms_key.rds_dr.arn

  # Replicate to DR region
  provider = aws.dr_region  # us-west-2
}

# KMS key in DR region for encrypted backups
resource "aws_kms_key" "rds_dr" {
  provider = aws.dr_region

  description             = "KMS key for RDS backup replication"
  deletion_window_in_days = 30
  enable_key_rotation     = true

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-rds-dr-key"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# =========================================
# Manual Snapshot for Before Major Changes
# =========================================

resource "aws_db_snapshot" "pre_migration" {
  count = var.create_pre_migration_snapshot ? 1 : 0

  db_instance_identifier = aws_db_instance.main.identifier
  db_snapshot_identifier = "anihpj-pre-migration-${formatdate("YYYY-MM-DD-hhmm", timestamp())}"

  tags = {
    Name      = "Pre-migration snapshot"
    CreatedBy = "Terraform"
  }
}
replica-monitoring.tf - Replica Lag Alarms 
# =========================================
# Read Replica Lag Monitoring
# =========================================

resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "replica_lag" {
  count = var.environment == "production" ? 1 : 0

  alarm_name          = "anihpj-replica-lag-high"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 3
  metric_name         = "ReplicaLag"
  namespace           = "AWS/RDS"
  period              = 60
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = 30  # 30 seconds

  dimensions = {
    DBInstanceIdentifier = aws_db_instance.replica[0].identifier
  }

  alarm_description = "RDS replica lag exceeds 30 seconds"
  alarm_actions     = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]
  ok_actions        = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Alert       = "replica-lag"
  }
}

# Failover automation (promote replica if primary fails)
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "primary_availability" {
  count = var.environment == "production" ? 1 : 0

  alarm_name          = "anihpj-primary-unavailable"
  comparison_operator = "LessThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 3
  metric_name         = "DatabaseConnections"
  namespace           = "AWS/RDS"
  period              = 60
  statistic           = "Sum"
  threshold           = 1

  dimensions = {
    DBInstanceIdentifier = aws_db_instance.main.identifier
  }

  alarm_description = "Primary RDS instance may be unavailable"
  alarm_actions     = [aws_sns_topic.critical_alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Alert       = "primary-availability"
  }
}

16.5 Django Celery Infrastructure

⚙️ Celery Task Flow Architecture

🐍
Django App
task.delay()
→
📨
Redis/SQS
Message Broker
→
⚙️
Celery Worker
ECS Fargate
→
✅
Result Backend
Redis/DB
⏰ Beat Scheduler → Periodic Tasks
❌ DLQ → Failed Tasks
🌸 Flower → Monitoring
celery.tf - Celery Workers & Redis 
# =========================================
# Celery Infrastructure for anihpj
# Background tasks: email notifications,
# JobPost expiry checks, report generation
# =========================================

# ElastiCache Redis for Celery
resource "aws_elasticache_subnet_group" "main" {
  name       = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  subnet_ids = var.db_subnet_ids
}

resource "aws_elasticache_parameter_group" "celery" {
  name   = "anihpj-celery-${var.environment}"
  family = "redis7"

  parameter {
    name  = "maxmemory-policy"
    value = "volatile-lru"
  }
}

resource "aws_elasticache_replication_group" "celery" {
  replication_group_id = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  description          = "Redis for Django cache and Celery"

  engine               = "redis"
  engine_version       = "7.0"
  node_type            = var.redis_node_type
  num_cache_clusters   = var.environment == "production" ? 2 : 1
  parameter_group_name = aws_elasticache_parameter_group.celery.name

  subnet_group_name  = aws_elasticache_subnet_group.main.name
  security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.redis.id]

  at_rest_encryption_enabled = true
  transit_encryption_enabled = true
  auth_token                 = random_password.redis_auth.result

  automatic_failover_enabled = var.environment == "production"
  multi_az_enabled           = var.environment == "production"

  port = 6379

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-celery-redis"
    Application = "celery"
  }
}

# Celery Worker ECS Service
resource "aws_ecs_service" "celery_worker" {
  name            = "anihpj-celery-worker"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.celery_worker.arn
  desired_count   = var.celery_worker_count

  capacity_provider_strategy {
    capacity_provider = "FARGATE_SPOT"
    weight            = 100  # Use Spot for cost savings
  }

  network_configuration {
    subnets          = var.private_subnet_ids
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.celery.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-celery-worker"
  }
}

# Celery Worker Task Definition
resource "aws_ecs_task_definition" "celery_worker" {
  family                   = "anihpj-celery-worker"
  requires_compatibilities = ["FARGATE"]
  network_mode             = "awsvpc"
  cpu                      = 512
  memory                   = 1024
  execution_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.arn
  task_role_arn            = aws_iam_role.ecs_task.arn

  container_definitions = jsonencode([
    {
      name    = "celery-worker"
      image   = "${aws_ecr_repository.django.repository_url}:${var.image_tag}"
      command = ["celery", "-A", "anihpj", "worker", "-l", "INFO", "-c", "4"]

      environment = [
        { name = "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", value = "anihpj.settings" },
        { name = "C_FORCE_ROOT", value = "true" },
      ]

      secrets = [
        {
          name      = "DATABASE_URL"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:DATABASE_URL::"
        },
        {
          name      = "CELERY_BROKER_URL"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:REDIS_URL::"
        },
      ]

      logConfiguration = {
        logDriver = "awslogs"
        options = {
          awslogs-group         = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.celery.name
          awslogs-region        = var.aws_region
          awslogs-stream-prefix = "worker"
        }
      }
    }
  ])
}

# Celery Beat (Scheduler) Service
resource "aws_ecs_service" "celery_beat" {
  name            = "anihpj-celery-beat"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.celery_beat.arn
  desired_count   = 1  # Only one beat scheduler!

  capacity_provider_strategy {
    capacity_provider = "FARGATE"
    weight            = 100  # Use on-demand for reliability
  }

  network_configuration {
    subnets          = var.private_subnet_ids
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.celery.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }
}

resource "aws_ecs_task_definition" "celery_beat" {
  family                   = "anihpj-celery-beat"
  requires_compatibilities = ["FARGATE"]
  network_mode             = "awsvpc"
  cpu                      = 256
  memory                   = 512
  execution_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.arn
  task_role_arn            = aws_iam_role.ecs_task.arn

  container_definitions = jsonencode([
    {
      name    = "celery-beat"
      image   = "${aws_ecr_repository.django.repository_url}:${var.image_tag}"
      command = ["celery", "-A", "anihpj", "beat", "-l", "INFO"]

      environment = [
        { name = "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", value = "anihpj.settings" },
      ]

      secrets = [
        {
          name      = "DATABASE_URL"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:DATABASE_URL::"
        },
        {
          name      = "CELERY_BROKER_URL"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:REDIS_URL::"
        },
      ]

      logConfiguration = {
        logDriver = "awslogs"
        options = {
          awslogs-group         = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.celery.name
          awslogs-region        = var.aws_region
          awslogs-stream-prefix = "beat"
        }
      }
    }
  ])
}
anihpj/celery.py - Celery Configuration 
# anihpj/celery.py
import os
from celery import Celery
from celery.schedules import crontab

os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'anihpj.settings')

app = Celery('anihpj')
app.config_from_object('django.conf:settings', namespace='CELERY')
app.autodiscover_tasks()

# Celery Beat schedule for JobPost tasks
app.conf.beat_schedule = {
    'expire-old-jobposts': {
        'task': 'jobpost.tasks.expire_old_posts',
        'schedule': crontab(hour=0, minute=0),  # Daily at midnight
    },
    'send-expiry-reminders': {
        'task': 'jobpost.tasks.send_expiry_reminders',
        'schedule': crontab(hour=9, minute=0),  # Daily at 9 AM
    },
    'generate-weekly-report': {
        'task': 'jobpost.tasks.generate_weekly_report',
        'schedule': crontab(hour=8, minute=0, day_of_week=1),  # Monday 8 AM
    },
}

# =========================================
# jobpost/tasks.py
# =========================================
from celery import shared_task
from django.utils import timezone
from django.core.mail import send_mail
from .models import JobPost, Author

@shared_task
def expire_old_posts():
    """Mark expired JobPosts as inactive."""
    expired = JobPost.objects.filter(
        expiry__lt=timezone.now(),
        is_active=True
    )
    count = expired.update(is_active=False)
    return f"Expired {count} job posts"

@shared_task
def send_expiry_reminders():
    """Notify Authors of soon-expiring posts."""
    soon_expiring = JobPost.objects.filter(
        expiry__range=[
            timezone.now(),
            timezone.now() + timezone.timedelta(days=3)
        ],
        is_active=True
    ).select_related('author__user')

    for post in soon_expiring:
        send_mail(
            subject=f'Your job post "{post.title}" expires soon',
            message=f'Your post expires on {post.expiry}.',
            from_email='noreply@anihpj.com',
            recipient_list=[post.author.user.email],
        )

    return f"Sent {soon_expiring.count()} reminders"
sqs-broker.tf - SQS as Celery Broker 
# =========================================
# SQS as Celery Broker (Alternative to Redis)
# =========================================

# Main task queue
resource "aws_sqs_queue" "celery" {
  name                       = "anihpj-${var.environment}-celery"
  delay_seconds              = 0
  max_message_size           = 262144  # 256 KB
  message_retention_seconds  = 345600  # 4 days
  receive_wait_time_seconds  = 10      # Long polling
  visibility_timeout_seconds = 300     # 5 minutes

  # Enable server-side encryption
  sqs_managed_sse_enabled = true

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-celery-queue"
    Application = "celery"
  }
}

# Dead Letter Queue
resource "aws_sqs_queue" "celery_dlq" {
  name                      = "anihpj-${var.environment}-celery-dlq"
  message_retention_seconds = 1209600  # 14 days

  tags = {
    Name    = "anihpj-celery-dlq"
    Purpose = "Failed Celery tasks"
  }
}

# Redrive policy (send failed messages to DLQ)
resource "aws_sqs_queue_redrive_policy" "celery" {
  queue_url = aws_sqs_queue.celery.id
  redrive_policy = jsonencode({
    deadLetterTargetArn = aws_sqs_queue.celery_dlq.arn
    maxReceiveCount     = 5  # Retry 5 times before DLQ
  })
}

# FIFO Queue for ordered tasks (e.g., report generation)
resource "aws_sqs_queue" "celery_fifo" {
  name                        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-celery.fifo"
  fifo_queue                  = true
  content_based_deduplication = true
  deduplication_scope         = "messageGroup"
  fifo_throughput_limit       = "perMessageGroupId"

  tags = {
    Name    = "anihpj-celery-fifo"
    Purpose = "Ordered Celery tasks"
  }
}

# Priority queues
resource "aws_sqs_queue" "celery_high_priority" {
  name                       = "anihpj-${var.environment}-celery-high"
  visibility_timeout_seconds = 60

  tags = {
    Name     = "anihpj-celery-high-priority"
    Priority = "high"
  }
}

# =========================================
# Celery SQS Configuration in Django
# =========================================
# In anihpj/settings.py:
# CELERY_BROKER_URL = 'sqs://'
# CELERY_BROKER_TRANSPORT_OPTIONS = {
#     'region': 'us-east-1',
#     'visibility_timeout': 300,
#     'polling_interval': 1,
#     'queue_name_prefix': 'anihpj-production-',
# }
flower.tf - Celery Flower Monitoring 
# =========================================
# Flower - Celery Monitoring Dashboard
# =========================================

resource "aws_ecs_service" "flower" {
  name            = "anihpj-flower"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.flower.arn
  desired_count   = 1

  capacity_provider_strategy {
    capacity_provider = "FARGATE"
    weight            = 100
  }

  network_configuration {
    subnets          = var.private_subnet_ids
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.flower.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }

  load_balancer {
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.flower.arn
    container_name   = "flower"
    container_port   = 5555
  }
}

resource "aws_ecs_task_definition" "flower" {
  family                   = "anihpj-flower"
  requires_compatibilities = ["FARGATE"]
  network_mode             = "awsvpc"
  cpu                      = 256
  memory                   = 512
  execution_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.arn
  task_role_arn            = aws_iam_role.ecs_task.arn

  container_definitions = jsonencode([
    {
      name  = "flower"
      image = "mher/flower:2.0"

      portMappings = [{
        containerPort = 5555
        protocol      = "tcp"
      }]

      environment = [
        # Basic auth for security
        { name = "FLOWER_BASIC_AUTH", value = "${var.flower_user}:${var.flower_password}" },
        { name = "FLOWER_PORT", value = "5555" },
        { name = "FLOWER_PERSISTENT", value = "True" },
        { name = "FLOWER_DB", value = "/data/flower.db" },
        # Prometheus metrics endpoint
        { name = "FLOWER_ENABLE_EVENTS", value = "True" },
      ]

      secrets = [
        {
          name      = "CELERY_BROKER_URL"
          valueFrom = "${aws_secretsmanager_secret.django.arn}:REDIS_URL::"
        },
      ]

      logConfiguration = {
        logDriver = "awslogs"
        options = {
          awslogs-group         = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.celery.name
          awslogs-region        = var.aws_region
          awslogs-stream-prefix = "flower"
        }
      }

      mountPoints = [{
        sourceVolume  = "flower-data"
        containerPath = "/data"
        readOnly      = false
      }]
    }
  ])

  volume {
    name = "flower-data"
    efs_volume_configuration {
      file_system_id = aws_efs_file_system.flower.id
    }
  }
}

# ALB Target Group for Flower
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "flower" {
  name        = "anihpj-flower"
  port        = 5555
  protocol    = "HTTP"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id
  target_type = "ip"

  health_check {
    enabled             = true
    path                = "/healthcheck"
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 3
    interval            = 30
    timeout             = 5
  }
}

# ALB Listener Rule for Flower (restricted access)
resource "aws_lb_listener_rule" "flower" {
  listener_arn = aws_lb_listener.https.arn
  priority     = 50

  action {
    type             = "forward"
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.flower.arn
  }

  condition {
    host_header {
      values = ["flower.anihpj.com"]
    }
  }

  # IP restriction for admin access only
  condition {
    source_ip {
      values = var.admin_ip_ranges
    }
  }
}
celery-autoscaling.tf - Worker Auto Scaling 
# =========================================
# Celery Worker Auto Scaling
# Scale based on SQS queue depth
# =========================================

resource "aws_appautoscaling_target" "celery_workers" {
  max_capacity       = 10
  min_capacity       = 1
  resource_id        = "service/${aws_ecs_cluster.main.name}/${aws_ecs_service.celery_worker.name}"
  scalable_dimension = "ecs:service:DesiredCount"
  service_namespace  = "ecs"
}

# Scale up when queue depth increases
resource "aws_appautoscaling_policy" "celery_scale_up" {
  name               = "celery-scale-up"
  policy_type        = "StepScaling"
  resource_id        = aws_appautoscaling_target.celery_workers.resource_id
  scalable_dimension = aws_appautoscaling_target.celery_workers.scalable_dimension
  service_namespace  = aws_appautoscaling_target.celery_workers.service_namespace

  step_scaling_policy_configuration {
    adjustment_type         = "ChangeInCapacity"
    cooldown                = 60
    metric_aggregation_type = "Average"

    step_adjustment {
      metric_interval_lower_bound = 0
      metric_interval_upper_bound = 100
      scaling_adjustment          = 1
    }

    step_adjustment {
      metric_interval_lower_bound = 100
      scaling_adjustment          = 2
    }
  }
}

# CloudWatch alarm for SQS queue depth
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "celery_queue_depth" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-celery-queue-depth"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 2
  metric_name         = "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible"
  namespace           = "AWS/SQS"
  period              = 60
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = 50  # Scale when > 50 messages

  dimensions = {
    QueueName = aws_sqs_queue.celery.name
  }

  alarm_actions = [aws_appautoscaling_policy.celery_scale_up.arn]
}

# Scale down when queue is empty
resource "aws_appautoscaling_policy" "celery_scale_down" {
  name               = "celery-scale-down"
  policy_type        = "StepScaling"
  resource_id        = aws_appautoscaling_target.celery_workers.resource_id
  scalable_dimension = aws_appautoscaling_target.celery_workers.scalable_dimension
  service_namespace  = aws_appautoscaling_target.celery_workers.service_namespace

  step_scaling_policy_configuration {
    adjustment_type         = "ChangeInCapacity"
    cooldown                = 300  # Longer cooldown for scale-down
    metric_aggregation_type = "Average"

    step_adjustment {
      metric_interval_upper_bound = 0
      scaling_adjustment          = -1
    }
  }
}

resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "celery_queue_empty" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-celery-queue-empty"
  comparison_operator = "LessThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 5
  metric_name         = "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible"
  namespace           = "AWS/SQS"
  period              = 60
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = 5

  dimensions = {
    QueueName = aws_sqs_queue.celery.name
  }

  alarm_actions = [aws_appautoscaling_policy.celery_scale_down.arn]
}

16.3 Django Static & Media Files

📦 Static & Media Files Flow

👤
User
→
🌐
CloudFront
CDN Cache
→
📁 /static/* → S3 Static
📷 /media/* → S3 Media
🐍 /* → ALB → Django
📦 collectstatic → S3
🔄 Lifecycle → Glacier
🔐 Signed URLs
storage.tf - S3 + CloudFront for Django 
# =========================================
# S3 Buckets for Django Static & Media
# =========================================

# Static files bucket (CSS, JS, images)
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "static" {
  bucket = "anihpj-static-${var.environment}-${random_id.bucket.hex}"

  tags = {
    Name    = "anihpj-static"
    Purpose = "Django collectstatic files"
  }
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "static" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.static.id
  versioning_configuration {
    status = "Enabled"
  }
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_cors_configuration" "static" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.static.id

  cors_rule {
    allowed_headers = ["*"]
    allowed_methods = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    allowed_origins = var.allowed_origins
    expose_headers  = ["ETag"]
    max_age_seconds = 3600
  }
}

# Media files bucket (user uploads)
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "media" {
  bucket = "anihpj-media-${var.environment}-${random_id.bucket.hex}"

  tags = {
    Name    = "anihpj-media"
    Purpose = "User uploaded files"
  }
}

# Block public access - use CloudFront
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "static" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.static.id

  block_public_acls       = true
  block_public_policy     = true
  ignore_public_acls      = true
  restrict_public_buckets = true
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "media" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.media.id

  block_public_acls       = true
  block_public_policy     = true
  ignore_public_acls      = true
  restrict_public_buckets = true
}

# CloudFront Origin Access Identity
resource "aws_cloudfront_origin_access_identity" "main" {
  comment = "OAI for anihpj ${var.environment}"
}

# Bucket policy for CloudFront
resource "aws_s3_bucket_policy" "static" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.static.id

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Sid    = "CloudFrontReadAccess"
        Effect = "Allow"
        Principal = {
          AWS = aws_cloudfront_origin_access_identity.main.iam_arn
        }
        Action   = "s3:GetObject"
        Resource = "${aws_s3_bucket.static.arn}/*"
      }
    ]
  })
}

# CloudFront Distribution
resource "aws_cloudfront_distribution" "main" {
  enabled             = true
  is_ipv6_enabled     = true
  comment             = "CDN for anihpj ${var.environment}"
  default_root_object = "index.html"
  price_class         = "PriceClass_100"

  aliases = var.environment == "production" ? [
    "static.anihpj.com",
    "media.anihpj.com"
  ] : []

  # Static files origin
  origin {
    domain_name = aws_s3_bucket.static.bucket_regional_domain_name
    origin_id   = "S3-static"

    s3_origin_config {
      origin_access_identity = aws_cloudfront_origin_access_identity.main.cloudfront_access_identity_path
    }
  }

  # Media files origin
  origin {
    domain_name = aws_s3_bucket.media.bucket_regional_domain_name
    origin_id   = "S3-media"

    s3_origin_config {
      origin_access_identity = aws_cloudfront_origin_access_identity.main.cloudfront_access_identity_path
    }
  }

  # ALB origin for Django app
  origin {
    domain_name = aws_lb.main.dns_name
    origin_id   = "ALB"

    custom_origin_config {
      http_port              = 80
      https_port             = 443
      origin_protocol_policy = "https-only"
      origin_ssl_protocols   = ["TLSv1.2"]
    }
  }

  # Static files behavior
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern     = "/static/*"
    target_origin_id = "S3-static"
    allowed_methods  = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    cached_methods   = ["GET", "HEAD"]

    forwarded_values {
      query_string = false
      cookies {
        forward = "none"
      }
    }

    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 86400     # 1 day
    max_ttl                = 31536000  # 1 year
    compress               = true
  }

  # Media files behavior
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern     = "/media/*"
    target_origin_id = "S3-media"
    allowed_methods  = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    cached_methods   = ["GET", "HEAD"]

    forwarded_values {
      query_string = false
      cookies {
        forward = "none"
      }
    }

    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 86400
    max_ttl                = 604800  # 1 week
    compress               = true
  }

  # Default behavior - Django app
  default_cache_behavior {
    target_origin_id       = "ALB"
    allowed_methods        = ["DELETE", "GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PATCH", "POST", "PUT"]
    cached_methods         = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"

    forwarded_values {
      query_string = true
      headers      = ["Host", "Origin", "Authorization"]
      cookies {
        forward = "all"
      }
    }

    min_ttl     = 0
    default_ttl = 0
    max_ttl     = 0
  }

  restrictions {
    geo_restriction {
      restriction_type = "none"
    }
  }

  viewer_certificate {
    acm_certificate_arn      = var.acm_certificate_arn
    ssl_support_method       = "sni-only"
    minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1.2_2021"
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-cdn"
  }
}
anihpj/settings.py - S3 Storage Configuration 
# anihpj/settings.py - S3 storage for static/media

# Install: pip install django-storages boto3

# =========================================
# Storage Backends
# =========================================
if os.environ.get('USE_S3', 'false').lower() == 'true':
    # S3 Settings
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = os.environ.get('AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID')
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = os.environ.get('AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY')
    AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = os.environ.get('AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME')
    AWS_S3_REGION_NAME = os.environ.get('AWS_S3_REGION', 'us-east-1')

    AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN = os.environ.get('CDN_DOMAIN')  # CloudFront
    AWS_S3_OBJECT_PARAMETERS = {
        'CacheControl': 'max-age=86400',
    }

    # Static files
    STATIC_LOCATION = 'static'
    STATIC_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/{STATIC_LOCATION}/'
    STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'anihpj.storage_backends.StaticStorage'

    # Media files
    MEDIA_LOCATION = 'media'
    MEDIA_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/{MEDIA_LOCATION}/'
    DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'anihpj.storage_backends.MediaStorage'
else:
    STATIC_URL = '/static/'
    MEDIA_URL = '/media/'

# =========================================
# anihpj/storage_backends.py
# =========================================
from storages.backends.s3boto3 import S3Boto3Storage

class StaticStorage(S3Boto3Storage):
    location = 'static'
    default_acl = 'private'
    file_overwrite = True
    custom_domain = False  # Use CloudFront

class MediaStorage(S3Boto3Storage):
    location = 'media'
    default_acl = 'private'
    file_overwrite = False
s3-lifecycle.tf - Lifecycle Rules & Optimization 
# =========================================
# S3 Lifecycle Rules for Django Assets
# =========================================

# Lifecycle rules for media bucket
resource "aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration" "media" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.media.id

  # Move old media to cheaper storage
  rule {
    id     = "transition-old-media"
    status = "Enabled"

    filter {
      prefix = "media/"
    }

    transition {
      days          = 90
      storage_class = "STANDARD_IA"
    }

    transition {
      days          = 365
      storage_class = "GLACIER"
    }
  }

  # Cleanup incomplete uploads
  rule {
    id     = "abort-incomplete-uploads"
    status = "Enabled"

    abort_incomplete_multipart_upload {
      days_after_initiation = 7
    }
  }

  # Delete old versions
  rule {
    id     = "delete-old-versions"
    status = "Enabled"

    noncurrent_version_expiration {
      noncurrent_days = 90
    }
  }
}

# Lifecycle for static bucket (less aggressive)
resource "aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration" "static" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.static.id

  rule {
    id     = "expire-old-static-versions"
    status = "Enabled"

    noncurrent_version_expiration {
      noncurrent_days = 30  # Keep old static files shorter
    }
  }
}
anihpj/utils/signed_urls.py - Signed URLs for Private Media 
# anihpj/utils/signed_urls.py
# Generate signed URLs for private media files

import boto3
from botocore.signers import CloudFrontSigner
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes, serialization
from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default_backend
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import padding
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import os

# =========================================
# CloudFront Signed URLs for Private Media
# =========================================

def get_cloudfront_signer():
    """Create CloudFront signer using private key."""
    key_id = os.environ.get('CLOUDFRONT_KEY_ID')
    private_key_path = os.environ.get('CLOUDFRONT_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH')

    with open(private_key_path, 'rb') as key_file:
        private_key = serialization.load_pem_private_key(
            key_file.read(),
            password=None,
            backend=default_backend()
        )

    def rsa_signer(message):
        return private_key.sign(
            message,
            padding.PKCS1v15(),
            hashes.SHA1()
        )

    return CloudFrontSigner(key_id, rsa_signer)


def generate_signed_url(object_key: str, expiration_minutes: int = 60) -> str:
    """Generate signed URL for private media file."""
    cdn_domain = os.environ.get('CDN_DOMAIN')
    url = f"https://{cdn_domain}/media/{object_key}"

    signer = get_cloudfront_signer()
    expire_date = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=expiration_minutes)

    signed_url = signer.generate_presigned_url(
        url,
        date_less_than=expire_date
    )

    return signed_url


# =========================================
# S3 Presigned URLs (Alternative Method)
# =========================================

def generate_s3_presigned_url(bucket: str, key: str, expiration: int = 3600) -> str:
    """Generate presigned URL directly from S3 (without CloudFront)."""
    s3_client = boto3.client(
        's3',
        region_name=os.environ.get('AWS_S3_REGION', 'us-east-1')
    )

    url = s3_client.generate_presigned_url(
        'get_object',
        Params={
            'Bucket': bucket,
            'Key': key
        },
        ExpiresIn=expiration
    )

    return url


# =========================================
# Usage in Django Views
# =========================================
# In jobpost/views.py:
#
# from anihpj.utils.signed_urls import generate_signed_url
#
# class ResumeDownloadView(LoginRequiredMixin, View):
#     def get(self, request, job_id):
#         job = get_object_or_404(JobPost, id=job_id)
#         if job.resume:
#             signed_url = generate_signed_url(job.resume.name)
#             return redirect(signed_url)
#         return Http404("Resume not found")
cloudfront-signing.tf - CloudFront Key Pair 
# =========================================
# CloudFront Key Pair for Signed URLs
# =========================================

# Generate RSA key for signing
resource "tls_private_key" "cloudfront" {
  algorithm = "RSA"
  rsa_bits  = 2048
}

# Store private key in Secrets Manager
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "cloudfront_private_key" {
  name = "anihpj/${var.environment}/cloudfront-private-key"
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "cloudfront_private_key" {
  secret_id     = aws_secretsmanager_secret.cloudfront_private_key.id
  secret_string = tls_private_key.cloudfront.private_key_pem
}

# CloudFront public key
resource "aws_cloudfront_public_key" "main" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-signing-key"
  encoded_key = tls_private_key.cloudfront.public_key_pem
  comment     = "Key for signing private media URLs"
}

# Key group for CloudFront distribution
resource "aws_cloudfront_key_group" "main" {
  name    = "anihpj-${var.environment}-key-group"
  items   = [aws_cloudfront_public_key.main.id]
  comment = "Key group for anihpj signed URLs"
}

# Update CloudFront behavior for private media
# Add to your CloudFront distribution
# trusted_key_groups = [aws_cloudfront_key_group.main.id]

16.6 Django CI/CD Pipeline with Terraform

🚀 Django CI/CD Pipeline Flow

📝
Push
→
🧪
Test
→
🐳
Build
→
📦
ECR Push
→
🏗️
Terraform
→
🗄️
Migrate
→
🚀
Deploy
🔵 Blue/Green
🔄 Rolling
🐤 Canary
.github/workflows/deploy.yml - Complete Django Pipeline 
# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
# Complete CI/CD for anihpj Django project

name: Deploy anihpj

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  ECR_REPOSITORY: anihpj
  TF_VERSION: 1.5.7

jobs:
  # =========================================
  # Test Django Application
  # =========================================
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: test_anihpj
          POSTGRES_USER: test_user
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test_pass
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
        ports:
          - 5432:5432

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install pytest pytest-django pytest-cov

      - name: Run migrations
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgres://test_user:test_pass@localhost:5432/test_anihpj
        run: python manage.py migrate

      - name: Run tests
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgres://test_user:test_pass@localhost:5432/test_anihpj
          SECRET_KEY: test-secret-key-for-ci
        run: |
