          pytest --cov=jobpost --cov-report=xml
          python manage.py check --deploy

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3

  # =========================================
  # Build & Push Docker Image
  # =========================================
  build:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push'
    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}

    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR
        id: ecr-login
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      - name: Generate image metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ steps.ecr-login.outputs.registry }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix=
            type=ref,event=branch

      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  # =========================================
  # Deploy Infrastructure with Terraform
  # =========================================
  terraform:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}

    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: terraform/environments/${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'prod' || 'staging' }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TF_VERSION }}

      - name: Terraform Init
        run: terraform init

      - name: Terraform Plan
        id: plan
        run: |
          terraform plan \
            -var="image_tag=${{ needs.build.outputs.image_tag }}" \
            -out=tfplan \
            -no-color
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Comment PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const output = `#### Terraform Plan 📖

            
Show Plan

            \`\`\`
            ${{ steps.plan.outputs.stdout }}
            \`\`\`

            
`;

            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: output
            });

      - name: Terraform Apply
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/develop'
        run: terraform apply -auto-approve tfplan

  # =========================================
  # Run Django Migrations
  # =========================================
  migrate:
    needs: terraform
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push'

    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Run migrations via ECS task
        run: |
          aws ecs run-task \
            --cluster anihpj-${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'prod' || 'staging' }} \
            --task-definition anihpj-migrate \
            --launch-type FARGATE \
            --network-configuration "awsvpcConfiguration={subnets=[${{ secrets.SUBNET_IDS }}],securityGroups=[${{ secrets.SG_ID }}]}" \
            --overrides '{"containerOverrides":[{"name":"django","command":["python","manage.py","migrate","--noinput"]}]}'

  # =========================================
  # Collect Static Files
  # =========================================
  collectstatic:
    needs: migrate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push'

    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Collect static files
        env:
          USE_S3: 'true'
          AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME: anihpj-static-${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'prod' || 'staging' }}
        run: python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

      - name: Invalidate CloudFront cache
        run: |
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            --distribution-id ${{ secrets.CLOUDFRONT_DIST_ID }} \
            --paths "/static/*"
codepipeline.tf - AWS CodePipeline Setup 
# =========================================
# AWS CodePipeline for anihpj Django
# =========================================

resource "aws_codepipeline" "main" {
  name     = "anihpj-${var.environment}-pipeline"
  role_arn = aws_iam_role.codepipeline.arn

  artifact_store {
    location = aws_s3_bucket.pipeline_artifacts.bucket
    type     = "S3"

    encryption_key {
      id   = aws_kms_key.pipeline.arn
      type = "KMS"
    }
  }

  # =========================================
  # Stage 1: Source
  # =========================================
  stage {
    name = "Source"

    action {
      name             = "Source"
      category         = "Source"
      owner            = "AWS"
      provider         = "CodeStarSourceConnection"
      version          = "1"
      output_artifacts = ["source_output"]

      configuration = {
        ConnectionArn    = aws_codestarconnections_connection.github.arn
        FullRepositoryId = "your-org/anihpj"
        BranchName       = var.environment == "production" ? "main" : "develop"
      }
    }
  }

  # =========================================
  # Stage 2: Build
  # =========================================
  stage {
    name = "Build"

    action {
      name             = "BuildAndTest"
      category         = "Build"
      owner            = "AWS"
      provider         = "CodeBuild"
      version          = "1"
      input_artifacts  = ["source_output"]
      output_artifacts = ["build_output"]

      configuration = {
        ProjectName = aws_codebuild_project.django.name
      }
    }
  }

  # =========================================
  # Stage 3: Manual Approval (Production)
  # =========================================
  dynamic "stage" {
    for_each = var.environment == "production" ? [1] : []
    content {
      name = "Approval"

      action {
        name     = "ManualApproval"
        category = "Approval"
        owner    = "AWS"
        provider = "Manual"
        version  = "1"

        configuration = {
          NotificationArn = aws_sns_topic.pipeline_approvals.arn
          CustomData      = "Please review the build artifacts and approve for production deployment."
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # =========================================
  # Stage 4: Deploy
  # =========================================
  stage {
    name = "Deploy"

    action {
      name            = "DeployToECS"
      category        = "Deploy"
      owner           = "AWS"
      provider        = "ECS"
      version         = "1"
      input_artifacts = ["build_output"]

      configuration = {
        ClusterName = aws_ecs_cluster.main.name
        ServiceName = aws_ecs_service.django.name
        FileName    = "imagedefinitions.json"
      }
    }
  }

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# SNS Topic for Approvals
resource "aws_sns_topic" "pipeline_approvals" {
  name = "anihpj-${var.environment}-pipeline-approvals"
}
blue-green.tf - ECS Blue/Green Deployments 
# =========================================
# ECS Blue/Green Deployment Configuration
# =========================================

# ECS Service with Blue/Green deployment controller
resource "aws_ecs_service" "django_bluegreen" {
  name            = "anihpj-django-bg"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.django.arn
  desired_count   = var.django_task_count

  # Blue/Green deployment controller
  deployment_controller {
    type = "CODE_DEPLOY"
  }

  network_configuration {
    subnets          = var.private_subnet_ids
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.ecs.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }

  load_balancer {
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.blue.arn
    container_name   = "django"
    container_port   = 8000
  }

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [task_definition, load_balancer]
  }
}

# Blue Target Group
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "blue" {
  name        = "anihpj-blue"
  port        = 8000
  protocol    = "HTTP"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id
  target_type = "ip"

  health_check {
    path                = "/health/"
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 3
    interval            = 30
  }
}

# Green Target Group
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "green" {
  name        = "anihpj-green"
  port        = 8000
  protocol    = "HTTP"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id
  target_type = "ip"

  health_check {
    path                = "/health/"
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 3
    interval            = 30
  }
}

# CodeDeploy Application
resource "aws_codedeploy_app" "django" {
  name             = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  compute_platform = "ECS"
}

# CodeDeploy Deployment Group
resource "aws_codedeploy_deployment_group" "django" {
  app_name               = aws_codedeploy_app.django.name
  deployment_group_name  = "anihpj-${var.environment}-dg"
  deployment_config_name = "CodeDeployDefault.ECSLinear10PercentEvery1Minutes"
  service_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.codedeploy.arn

  ecs_service {
    cluster_name = aws_ecs_cluster.main.name
    service_name = aws_ecs_service.django_bluegreen.name
  }

  load_balancer_info {
    target_group_pair_info {
      prod_traffic_route {
        listener_arns = [aws_lb_listener.https.arn]
      }

      target_group {
        name = aws_lb_target_group.blue.name
      }

      target_group {
        name = aws_lb_target_group.green.name
      }
    }
  }

  blue_green_deployment_config {
    deployment_ready_option {
      action_on_timeout = "CONTINUE_DEPLOYMENT"
      wait_time_in_minutes = 5
    }

    terminate_blue_instances_on_deployment_success {
      action                           = "TERMINATE"
      termination_wait_time_in_minutes = 10
    }
  }

  auto_rollback_configuration {
    enabled = true
    events  = ["DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE", "DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM"]
  }

  alarm_configuration {
    alarms  = [aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.ecs_errors.name]
    enabled = true
  }
}
rolling-update.tf - ECS Rolling Updates 
# =========================================
# ECS Rolling Update Configuration
# =========================================

resource "aws_ecs_service" "django_rolling" {
  name            = "anihpj-django"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.django.arn
  desired_count   = var.django_task_count

  # Rolling update deployment (default)
  deployment_controller {
    type = "ECS"
  }

  # Deployment configuration
  deployment_minimum_healthy_percent = 50
  deployment_maximum_percent         = 200

  # Deployment circuit breaker with rollback
  deployment_circuit_breaker {
    enable   = true
    rollback = true  # Auto-rollback on failure
  }

  network_configuration {
    subnets          = var.private_subnet_ids
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.ecs.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }

  load_balancer {
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.django.arn
    container_name   = "django"
    container_port   = 8000
  }

  # Health check grace period (time for container to start)
  health_check_grace_period_seconds = 60

  # Force new deployment on changes
  force_new_deployment = var.force_deployment

  # Capacity provider strategy for Spot instances
  capacity_provider_strategy {
    capacity_provider = "FARGATE"
    weight            = 1
    base              = 2  # Keep 2 on-demand for stability
  }

  capacity_provider_strategy {
    capacity_provider = "FARGATE_SPOT"
    weight            = 4  # 4x more Spot tasks
  }

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [desired_count]  # Managed by auto-scaling
  }
}

# ALB Target Group Health Check
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "django" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  port        = 8000
  protocol    = "HTTP"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id
  target_type = "ip"

  health_check {
    enabled             = true
    path                = "/health/"
    port                = "traffic-port"
    protocol            = "HTTP"
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 3
    timeout             = 5
    interval            = 30
    matcher             = "200"
  }

  # Deregistration delay for graceful shutdown
  deregistration_delay = 30

  # Sticky sessions for session-based auth
  stickiness {
    enabled         = true
    type            = "lb_cookie"
    cookie_duration = 86400  # 1 day
  }
}
canary-release.tf - Weighted Traffic Shifting 
# =========================================
# Canary Release with Weighted Target Groups
# =========================================

# Production Target Group (stable version)
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "production" {
  name        = "anihpj-production"
  port        = 8000
  protocol    = "HTTP"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id
  target_type = "ip"

  health_check {
    path = "/health/"
  }
}

# Canary Target Group (new version)
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "canary" {
  name        = "anihpj-canary"
  port        = 8000
  protocol    = "HTTP"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id
  target_type = "ip"

  health_check {
    path = "/health/"
  }
}

# Listener Rule with weighted routing
resource "aws_lb_listener_rule" "canary" {
  listener_arn = aws_lb_listener.https.arn
  priority     = 10

  action {
    type = "forward"

    forward {
      # 95% to production, 5% to canary
      target_group {
        arn    = aws_lb_target_group.production.arn
        weight = var.canary_enabled ? 95 : 100
      }

      target_group {
        arn    = aws_lb_target_group.canary.arn
        weight = var.canary_enabled ? 5 : 0
      }

      stickiness {
        enabled  = true
        duration = 600  # 10 minutes
      }
    }
  }

  condition {
    path_pattern {
      values = ["/*"]
    }
  }
}

# Canary ECS Service
resource "aws_ecs_service" "canary" {
  count = var.canary_enabled ? 1 : 0

  name            = "anihpj-canary"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.django_canary.arn
  desired_count   = 1  # Single task for canary

  network_configuration {
    subnets          = var.private_subnet_ids
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.ecs.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }

  load_balancer {
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.canary.arn
    container_name   = "django"
    container_port   = 8000
  }
}

# =========================================
# Canary Metrics Validation
# =========================================

# CloudWatch Alarm for Canary Error Rate
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "canary_errors" {
  count = var.canary_enabled ? 1 : 0

  alarm_name          = "anihpj-canary-error-rate"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 2
  threshold           = 5  # 5% error rate triggers alarm

  metric_query {
    id          = "errors"
    return_data = true

    metric {
      metric_name = "HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count"
      namespace   = "AWS/ApplicationELB"
      period      = 60
      stat        = "Sum"

      dimensions = {
        TargetGroup  = aws_lb_target_group.canary.arn_suffix
        LoadBalancer = aws_lb.main.arn_suffix
      }
    }
  }

  alarm_description = "Canary deployment error rate too high - consider rollback"
  alarm_actions     = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]
}

16.7 Django Monitoring & Observability

📊 Django Observability Stack

🐍
Django App
→
📋
CloudWatch
Logs
→
📈
CloudWatch
Metrics
→
🔔
Alarms
+ SNS
→
📱
Slack/
PagerDuty
monitoring.tf - CloudWatch Log Groups for Django 
# =========================================
# CloudWatch Log Groups - anihpj Django
# =========================================

# Application Logs
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "django_app" {
  name              = "/ecs/anihpj/django"
  retention_in_days = var.environment == "prod" ? 90 : 14

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Component   = "django"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Celery Worker Logs
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "celery_worker" {
  name              = "/ecs/anihpj/celery-worker"
  retention_in_days = var.environment == "prod" ? 90 : 14

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Component   = "celery"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Celery Beat Logs
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "celery_beat" {
  name              = "/ecs/anihpj/celery-beat"
  retention_in_days = var.environment == "prod" ? 90 : 14

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Component   = "celery-beat"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Access Logs (from ALB)
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "alb_access" {
  name              = "/alb/anihpj/${var.environment}"
  retention_in_days = 30

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Component   = "alb"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# =========================================
# Log Metric Filters - Error Detection
# =========================================

# 500 Error Counter
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_metric_filter" "django_500_errors" {
  name           = "anihpj-500-errors"
  log_group_name = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.django_app.name
  pattern        = "[timestamp, level=ERROR, ...]"

  metric_transformation {
    name          = "Django500Errors"
    namespace     = "Anihpj/Django"
    value         = "1"
    default_value = "0"
  }
}

# Slow Request Counter (>3s)
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_metric_filter" "slow_requests" {
  name           = "anihpj-slow-requests"
  log_group_name = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.django_app.name
  pattern        = "[timestamp, ..., response_time > 3000, ...]"

  metric_transformation {
    name          = "DjangoSlowRequests"
    namespace     = "Anihpj/Django"
    value         = "1"
    default_value = "0"
  }
}

# Database Connection Errors
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_metric_filter" "db_connection_errors" {
  name           = "anihpj-db-errors"
  log_group_name = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.django_app.name
  pattern        = "?OperationalError ?connection ?psycopg2"

  metric_transformation {
    name          = "DjangoDBErrors"
    namespace     = "Anihpj/Django"
    value         = "1"
    default_value = "0"
  }
}
alarms.tf - CloudWatch Alarms 
# =========================================
# SNS Topic for Alerts
# =========================================

resource "aws_sns_topic" "django_alerts" {
  name = "anihpj-${var.environment}-alerts"

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_sns_topic_subscription" "email_alerts" {
  topic_arn = aws_sns_topic.django_alerts.arn
  protocol  = "email"
  endpoint  = var.alert_email
}

# =========================================
# CloudWatch Alarms - anihpj
# =========================================

# High Error Rate Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "high_error_rate" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-high-error-rate"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 2
  metric_name         = "Django500Errors"
  namespace           = "Anihpj/Django"
  period              = 300
  statistic           = "Sum"
  threshold           = 10
  alarm_description   = "Django 500 errors > 10 in 5 minutes"

  alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.django_alerts.arn]
  ok_actions    = [aws_sns_topic.django_alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Severity    = "high"
  }
}

# ECS CPU Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "ecs_high_cpu" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-ecs-high-cpu"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 3
  metric_name         = "CPUUtilization"
  namespace           = "AWS/ECS"
  period              = 300
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = 80
  alarm_description   = "ECS CPU > 80% for 15 minutes"

  dimensions = {
    ClusterName = aws_ecs_cluster.main.name
    ServiceName = aws_ecs_service.django.name
  }

  alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.django_alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Severity    = "medium"
  }
}

# RDS CPU Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "rds_high_cpu" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-rds-high-cpu"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 3
  metric_name         = "CPUUtilization"
  namespace           = "AWS/RDS"
  period              = 300
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = 80
  alarm_description   = "RDS CPU > 80% for 15 minutes"

  dimensions = {
    DBInstanceIdentifier = aws_db_instance.main.id
  }

  alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.django_alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Severity    = "high"
  }
}

# RDS Storage Alarm
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "rds_low_storage" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-rds-low-storage"
  comparison_operator = "LessThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 1
  metric_name         = "FreeStorageSpace"
  namespace           = "AWS/RDS"
  period              = 300
  statistic           = "Average"
  threshold           = 5000000000  # 5GB
  alarm_description   = "RDS free storage < 5GB"

  dimensions = {
    DBInstanceIdentifier = aws_db_instance.main.id
  }

  alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.django_alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Severity    = "critical"
  }
}
xray.tf - X-Ray Distributed Tracing 
# =========================================
# AWS X-Ray for Django APM
# =========================================

# X-Ray sampling rule for Django
resource "aws_xray_sampling_rule" "django" {
  rule_name      = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  priority       = 1000
  version        = 1
  reservoir_size = 5
  fixed_rate     = 0.05  # 5% sampling
  url_path       = "*"
  host           = "*"
  http_method    = "*"
  service_type   = "*"
  service_name   = "anihpj-django"
  resource_arn   = "*"
}

# X-Ray group for service map filtering
resource "aws_xray_group" "django" {
  group_name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}"
  filter_expression = "service(id(name: \"anihpj-django\", type: \"client\"))"

  insights_configuration {
    insights_enabled      = true
    notifications_enabled = true
  }
}
anihpj/middleware/xray.py - X-Ray Middleware 
# anihpj/middleware/xray.py
# Custom X-Ray middleware for Django

from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder, patch_all
from aws_xray_sdk.ext.django.middleware import XRayMiddleware
import logging

# Patch AWS SDK, requests, and database libraries
patch_all()

class DjangoXRayMiddleware(XRayMiddleware):
    """
    Enhanced X-Ray middleware for anihpj Django app.
    Adds custom annotations and metadata.
    """

    def __call__(self, request):
        response = super().__call__(request)

        # Get the current segment
        segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
        if segment:
            # Add custom annotations (searchable in X-Ray)
            segment.put_annotation('environment', os.environ.get('ENVIRONMENT', 'dev'))
            segment.put_annotation('user_authenticated', request.user.is_authenticated)

            if request.user.is_authenticated:
                segment.put_annotation('user_id', str(request.user.id))

            # Add metadata (not searchable but useful)
            segment.put_metadata('request', {
                'path': request.path,
                'method': request.method,
                'content_type': request.content_type,
            })

            # Track JobPost views specifically
            if '/jobs/' in request.path:
                segment.put_annotation('feature', 'jobpost')

        return response


# =========================================
# anihpj/settings.py - X-Ray Configuration
# =========================================
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    # ...
    'aws_xray_sdk.ext.django',
]

MIDDLEWARE = [
    'anihpj.middleware.xray.DjangoXRayMiddleware',  # First!
    # ... other middleware
]

XRAY_RECORDER = {
    'AUTO_INSTRUMENT': True,
    'AWS_XRAY_TRACING_NAME': 'anihpj-django',
    'PLUGINS': ('ECSPlugin',),
    'SAMPLING': True,
    'SAMPLING_RULES': None,  # Use sampling rules from AWS
    'AWS_XRAY_CONTEXT_MISSING': 'LOG_ERROR',
    'DYNAMIC_NAMING': '*anihpj*',
}
anihpj/middleware/metrics.py - Custom Metrics Middleware 
# anihpj/middleware/metrics.py
# Django middleware for custom CloudWatch metrics

import time
import boto3
from django.conf import settings

class CloudWatchMetricsMiddleware:
    """
    Middleware to publish custom Django metrics to CloudWatch.
    Tracks request latency, errors, and business metrics.
    """

    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response
        self.cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
        self.namespace = 'Anihpj/Django'

    def __call__(self, request):
        start_time = time.time()

        response = self.get_response(request)

        # Calculate request duration
        duration = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000  # milliseconds

        # Prepare metrics
        metrics = []

        # Request latency metric with dimensions
        metrics.append({
            'MetricName': 'RequestLatency',
            'Value': duration,
            'Unit': 'Milliseconds',
            'Dimensions': [
                {'Name': 'Environment', 'Value': settings.ENVIRONMENT},
                {'Name': 'Endpoint', 'Value': self._get_endpoint(request)},
                {'Name': 'Method', 'Value': request.method},
            ]
        })

        # Request count
        metrics.append({
            'MetricName': 'RequestCount',
            'Value': 1,
            'Unit': 'Count',
            'Dimensions': [
                {'Name': 'Environment', 'Value': settings.ENVIRONMENT},
                {'Name': 'StatusCode', 'Value': str(response.status_code)},
            ]
        })

        # Error metrics
        if response.status_code >= 400:
            error_type = '4xx' if response.status_code < 500 else '5xx'
            metrics.append({
                'MetricName': f'{error_type}Errors',
                'Value': 1,
                'Unit': 'Count',
                'Dimensions': [
                    {'Name': 'Environment', 'Value': settings.ENVIRONMENT},
                    {'Name': 'Endpoint', 'Value': self._get_endpoint(request)},
                ]
            })

        # Publish metrics (async in production)
        self._publish_metrics(metrics)

        return response

    def _get_endpoint(self, request):
        """Normalize endpoint for metrics (remove IDs)."""
        path = request.path
        # Replace numeric IDs with placeholder
        import re
        return re.sub(r'/\d+/', '/{id}/', path)

    def _publish_metrics(self, metrics):
        """Publish metrics to CloudWatch."""
        try:
            self.cloudwatch.put_metric_data(
                Namespace=self.namespace,
                MetricData=metrics
            )
        except Exception as e:
            # Don't fail requests on metric errors
            import logging
            logging.error(f"Failed to publish metrics: {e}")
alerting.tf - PagerDuty & Slack Integration 
# =========================================
# Alert Routing - PagerDuty & Slack
# =========================================

# Critical alerts go to PagerDuty
resource "aws_sns_topic" "critical_alerts" {
  name = "anihpj-${var.environment}-critical"
}

# PagerDuty integration via HTTPS endpoint
resource "aws_sns_topic_subscription" "pagerduty" {
  count     = var.environment == "production" ? 1 : 0
  topic_arn = aws_sns_topic.critical_alerts.arn
  protocol  = "https"
  endpoint  = var.pagerduty_endpoint  # From PagerDuty service
}

# Warning alerts go to Slack
resource "aws_sns_topic" "warning_alerts" {
  name = "anihpj-${var.environment}-warnings"
}

# Lambda for Slack notifications
resource "aws_lambda_function" "slack_notifier" {
  filename         = "lambda/slack-notifier.zip"
  function_name    = "anihpj-${var.environment}-slack-notifier"
  role             = aws_iam_role.lambda_slack.arn
  handler          = "index.handler"
  runtime          = "python3.11"

  environment {
    variables = {
      SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL = var.slack_webhook_url
      SLACK_CHANNEL     = var.slack_channel
    }
  }
}

resource "aws_sns_topic_subscription" "slack" {
  topic_arn = aws_sns_topic.warning_alerts.arn
  protocol  = "lambda"
  endpoint  = aws_lambda_function.slack_notifier.arn
}

resource "aws_lambda_permission" "sns_slack" {
  statement_id  = "AllowSNSInvoke"
  action        = "lambda:InvokeFunction"
  function_name = aws_lambda_function.slack_notifier.function_name
  principal     = "sns.amazonaws.com"
  source_arn    = aws_sns_topic.warning_alerts.arn
}

# =========================================
# Alarm Actions by Severity
# =========================================

# Update existing alarms with appropriate topics
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "critical_error_rate" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-critical-errors"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 2
  metric_name         = "Django500Errors"
  namespace           = "Anihpj/Django"
  period              = 60
  statistic           = "Sum"
  threshold           = 50  # Critical: 50 errors/minute
  alarm_description   = "CRITICAL: Django 500 errors > 50/min"

  # Critical alerts to PagerDuty
  alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.critical_alerts.arn]
  ok_actions    = [aws_sns_topic.critical_alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Severity = "critical"
  }
}

resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "warning_error_rate" {
  alarm_name          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-warning-errors"
  comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 3
  metric_name         = "Django500Errors"
  namespace           = "Anihpj/Django"
  period              = 300
  statistic           = "Sum"
  threshold           = 10  # Warning: 10 errors/5min
  alarm_description   = "WARNING: Django 500 errors elevated"

  # Warnings to Slack
  alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.warning_alerts.arn]
  ok_actions    = [aws_sns_topic.warning_alerts.arn]

  tags = {
    Severity = "warning"
  }
}

✅ Django Monitoring Checklist

📋 Logs
  • Django app logs
  • Celery worker/beat logs
  • ALB access logs
  • RDS slow query logs
📈 Metrics
  • Request latency (P50, P99)
  • Error rates (4xx, 5xx)
  • Database connections
  • Cache hit ratio
🔔 Alarms
  • High error rate
  • CPU/Memory > 80%
  • Database storage low
  • Unhealthy targets
🔍 Tracing
  • X-Ray integration
  • Service maps
  • Request traces
  • Error analysis

16.8 Django Cost Optimization

💰 anihpj Cost Optimization Strategy

⚙️
Right-Size
Match resources to load
📅
Reserved
1-3yr commitments
📊
Auto-Scale
Scale with demand
🗑️
Lifecycle
Archive old data
🌙
Schedule
Off-hours shutdown

📊 anihpj Monthly Cost Estimates by Environment

Component Dev Staging Production
ECS Fargate (Django) $15 $30 $120
RDS PostgreSQL $15 (t3.micro) $30 (t3.small) $150 (r5.large)
ElastiCache Redis $0 (local) $15 (t3.micro) $75 (r6g.large)
ALB $20 $20 $25
S3 + CloudFront $5 $10 $50
NAT Gateway $0 (none) $35 (1 AZ) $70 (2 AZ)
Total (est.) ~$55/mo ~$140/mo ~$490/mo
cost-optimization.tf - Environment-Based Sizing 
# =========================================
# Cost Optimization - Right-Sizing by Env
# =========================================

locals {
  # Environment-based sizing for anihpj
  sizing = {
    dev = {
      ecs_cpu           = 256
      ecs_memory        = 512
      ecs_desired_count = 1
      rds_instance      = "db.t3.micro"
      rds_storage       = 20
      rds_multi_az      = false
      redis_node_type   = null  # Use local Redis
      nat_gateway_count = 0
    }
    staging = {
      ecs_cpu           = 512
      ecs_memory        = 1024
      ecs_desired_count = 1
      rds_instance      = "db.t3.small"
      rds_storage       = 50
      rds_multi_az      = false
      redis_node_type   = "cache.t3.micro"
      nat_gateway_count = 1
    }
    prod = {
      ecs_cpu           = 1024
      ecs_memory        = 2048
      ecs_desired_count = 2
      rds_instance      = "db.r5.large"
      rds_storage       = 100
      rds_multi_az      = true
      redis_node_type   = "cache.r6g.large"
      nat_gateway_count = 2
    }
  }

  env_config = local.sizing[var.environment]
}

# =========================================
# S3 Lifecycle - Media File Cost Savings
# =========================================

resource "aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration" "media_lifecycle" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.media.id

  # Move infrequently accessed files to IA after 30 days
  rule {
    id     = "media-intelligent-tiering"
    status = "Enabled"

    transition {
      days          = 30
      storage_class = "STANDARD_IA"
    }

    transition {
      days          = 90
      storage_class = "GLACIER_IR"
    }

    # Delete incomplete uploads after 7 days
    abort_incomplete_multipart_upload {
      days_after_initiation = 7
    }
  }

  # Expire old job post images after 2 years
  rule {
    id     = "expire-old-job-images"
    status = "Enabled"

    filter {
      prefix = "job-posts/"
    }

    expiration {
      days = 730  # 2 years
    }
  }
}

# =========================================
# Scheduled Scaling - Dev/Staging Shutdown
# =========================================

# Scale down dev/staging during off-hours
resource "aws_appautoscaling_scheduled_action" "scale_down_night" {
  count = var.environment != "prod" ? 1 : 0

  name               = "anihpj-${var.environment}-scale-down-night"
  service_namespace  = "ecs"
  resource_id        = "service/${aws_ecs_cluster.main.name}/${aws_ecs_service.django.name}"
  scalable_dimension = "ecs:service:DesiredCount"
  schedule           = "cron(0 22 ? * MON-FRI *)"  # 10 PM weekdays

  scalable_target_action {
    min_capacity = 0
    max_capacity = 0
  }
}

resource "aws_appautoscaling_scheduled_action" "scale_up_morning" {
  count = var.environment != "prod" ? 1 : 0

  name               = "anihpj-${var.environment}-scale-up-morning"
  service_namespace  = "ecs"
  resource_id        = "service/${aws_ecs_cluster.main.name}/${aws_ecs_service.django.name}"
  scalable_dimension = "ecs:service:DesiredCount"
  schedule           = "cron(0 8 ? * MON-FRI *)"  # 8 AM weekdays

  scalable_target_action {
    min_capacity = 1
    max_capacity = local.env_config.ecs_desired_count
  }
}
aurora-serverless.tf - Cost-Effective Dev/Staging DB 
# =========================================
# Aurora Serverless v2 for Dev/Staging
# Pay only for what you use - scales to 0!
# =========================================

resource "aws_rds_cluster" "serverless" {
  count = var.environment != "prod" ? 1 : 0

  cluster_identifier = "anihpj-${var.environment}-serverless"
  engine             = "aurora-postgresql"
  engine_mode        = "provisioned"
  engine_version     = "15.4"
  database_name      = "anihpj"
  master_username    = "anihpj_admin"
  master_password    = random_password.db_password.result

  # Serverless v2 scaling
  serverlessv2_scaling_configuration {
    min_capacity = 0.5   # Scale to near-zero
    max_capacity = 4.0   # Max 4 ACUs for dev/staging
  }

  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.rds.id]
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.main.name

  skip_final_snapshot = true
  storage_encrypted   = true

  # Auto-pause after 5 minutes of inactivity
  enable_http_endpoint = true  # Data API for serverless

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-aurora"
    CostCenter  = "development"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_rds_cluster_instance" "serverless" {
  count = var.environment != "prod" ? 1 : 0

  cluster_identifier = aws_rds_cluster.serverless[0].id
  instance_class     = "db.serverless"
  engine             = aws_rds_cluster.serverless[0].engine
  engine_version     = aws_rds_cluster.serverless[0].engine_version

  # Cost: ~$43/month at 0.5 ACU average usage vs $115/month for db.t3.small
}
cloudfront-optimization.tf - Cache Hit Ratio 
# =========================================
# CloudFront Cost Optimization
# Higher cache hit ratio = lower origin costs
# =========================================

# Optimized cache policy for static files
resource "aws_cloudfront_cache_policy" "static_optimized" {
  name        = "anihpj-static-optimized"
  comment     = "Optimized caching for Django static files"
  default_ttl = 86400     # 1 day default
  max_ttl     = 31536000  # 1 year max
  min_ttl     = 3600      # 1 hour min

  parameters_in_cache_key_and_forwarded_to_origin {
    cookies_config {
      cookie_behavior = "none"  # Don't vary by cookies
    }

    headers_config {
      header_behavior = "none"  # Don't vary by headers
    }

    query_strings_config {
      query_string_behavior = "whitelist"
      query_strings {
        items = ["v", "version"]  # Only cache-busting params
      }
    }

    enable_accept_encoding_brotli = true
    enable_accept_encoding_gzip   = true
  }
}

# Use PriceClass_100 for US/EU only (cheaper)
resource "aws_cloudfront_distribution" "optimized" {
  # ... other config

  # Cost optimization: PriceClass_100 is cheapest
  # PriceClass_100: US, Canada, Europe
  # PriceClass_200: + Asia, Africa, Middle East
  # PriceClass_All: All edge locations
  price_class = var.environment == "prod" ? "PriceClass_200" : "PriceClass_100"

  # Enable Origin Shield (reduces origin requests)
  origin {
    origin_id   = "S3-static"
    domain_name = aws_s3_bucket.static.bucket_regional_domain_name

    origin_shield {
      enabled              = true
      origin_shield_region = "us-east-1"  # Closest to S3
    }
  }
}

# =========================================
# Data Transfer Cost Reduction
# =========================================

# Use VPC endpoints to avoid NAT Gateway charges
resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "s3" {
  vpc_id            = var.vpc_id
  service_name      = "com.amazonaws.${var.aws_region}.s3"
  vpc_endpoint_type = "Gateway"
  route_table_ids   = var.private_route_table_ids

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-s3-endpoint"
    Cost = "NAT-bypass"
  }
}

resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "ecr_api" {
  vpc_id              = var.vpc_id
  service_name        = "com.amazonaws.${var.aws_region}.ecr.api"
  vpc_endpoint_type   = "Interface"
  subnet_ids          = var.private_subnet_ids
  security_group_ids  = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id]
  private_dns_enabled = true

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-ecr-api-endpoint"
  }
}

resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "ecr_dkr" {
  vpc_id              = var.vpc_id
  service_name        = "com.amazonaws.${var.aws_region}.ecr.dkr"
  vpc_endpoint_type   = "Interface"
  subnet_ids          = var.private_subnet_ids
  security_group_ids  = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id]
  private_dns_enabled = true

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-ecr-dkr-endpoint"
  }
}

resource "aws_vpc_endpoint" "logs" {
  vpc_id              = var.vpc_id
  service_name        = "com.amazonaws.${var.aws_region}.logs"
  vpc_endpoint_type   = "Interface"
  subnet_ids          = var.private_subnet_ids
  security_group_ids  = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id]
  private_dns_enabled = true

  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-logs-endpoint"
  }
}

# Cost estimate: VPC endpoints ~$7.50/month each
# vs NAT Gateway ~$32/month + $0.045/GB data
# Break-even at ~500GB/month data transfer
💡 Quick Wins (Up to 40% savings)
  • Schedule dev/staging shutdown
  • Use t3/t4g instances for non-prod
  • Enable S3 Intelligent-Tiering
  • Skip NAT Gateway in dev
📅 Long-Term (Up to 72% savings)
  • Reserved Instances (1-3yr)
  • Savings Plans for Fargate
  • RDS Reserved Instances
  • CloudFront committed use
⚠️ Cost Traps to Avoid
  • NAT Gateway data charges
  • Unattached EBS volumes
  • Orphaned snapshots
  • Overprovisioned RDS storage

16.9 Django Security Best Practices

🔒 anihpj Security Layers

🌐 Edge: WAF + CloudFront + ACM SSL
↓
🔀 Load Balancer: ALB + Security Groups
↓
🐍 Application: Django + HTTPS + CORS
↓
🗄️ Data: RDS Encryption + Secrets Manager
security-groups.tf - Least Privilege Network Access 
# =========================================
# Security Groups - anihpj Least Privilege
# =========================================

# ALB Security Group - Public facing
resource "aws_security_group" "alb" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-alb-sg"
  description = "ALB security group - HTTPS only"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id

  # HTTPS from anywhere (WAF filters traffic first)
  ingress {
    from_port   = 443
    to_port     = 443
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
    description = "HTTPS from internet"
  }

  # HTTP redirect only
  ingress {
    from_port   = 80
    to_port     = 80
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
    description = "HTTP redirect to HTTPS"
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-alb-sg"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# ECS Security Group - Private
resource "aws_security_group" "ecs" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-ecs-sg"
  description = "ECS tasks security group"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id

  # Only from ALB
  ingress {
    from_port       = 8000
    to_port         = 8000
    protocol        = "tcp"
    security_groups = [aws_security_group.alb.id]
    description     = "Django from ALB only"
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
    description = "Outbound for pip, AWS APIs"
  }

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-ecs-sg"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# RDS Security Group - Database tier
resource "aws_security_group" "rds" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-rds-sg"
  description = "RDS PostgreSQL security group"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id

  # Only from ECS tasks
  ingress {
    from_port       = 5432
    to_port         = 5432
    protocol        = "tcp"
    security_groups = [aws_security_group.ecs.id]
    description     = "PostgreSQL from ECS only"
  }

  # No egress needed for RDS
  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-rds-sg"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# Redis Security Group
resource "aws_security_group" "redis" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-redis-sg"
  description = "ElastiCache Redis security group"
  vpc_id      = aws_vpc.main.id

  ingress {
    from_port       = 6379
    to_port         = 6379
    protocol        = "tcp"
    security_groups = [aws_security_group.ecs.id]
    description     = "Redis from ECS only"
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-redis-sg"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}
secrets.tf - Secrets Manager for Django 
# =========================================
# Secrets Manager - anihpj Django Secrets
# =========================================

# Django SECRET_KEY
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "django_secret_key" {
  name        = "anihpj/${var.environment}/django-secret-key"
  description = "Django SECRET_KEY for anihpj"

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "django_secret_key" {
  secret_id     = aws_secretsmanager_secret.django_secret_key.id
  secret_string = var.django_secret_key  # Passed via TF_VAR
}

# Database Credentials
resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret" "database_credentials" {
  name        = "anihpj/${var.environment}/database-credentials"
  description = "RDS PostgreSQL credentials for anihpj"

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

resource "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "database_credentials" {
  secret_id = aws_secretsmanager_secret.database_credentials.id
  secret_string = jsonencode({
    username = aws_db_instance.main.username
    password = var.db_password
    host     = aws_db_instance.main.address
    port     = aws_db_instance.main.port
    database = aws_db_instance.main.db_name
    url      = "postgresql://${aws_db_instance.main.username}:${var.db_password}@${aws_db_instance.main.address}:${aws_db_instance.main.port}/${aws_db_instance.main.db_name}"
  })
}

# IAM Policy for ECS to read secrets
resource "aws_iam_policy" "ecs_secrets_access" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-ecs-secrets-access"
  description = "Allow ECS tasks to read Secrets Manager secrets"

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = [
          "secretsmanager:GetSecretValue"
        ]
        Resource = [
          aws_secretsmanager_secret.django_secret_key.arn,
          aws_secretsmanager_secret.database_credentials.arn
        ]
      }
    ]
  })
}
waf.tf - AWS WAF for Django 
# =========================================
# AWS WAF for anihpj Django Application
# =========================================

resource "aws_wafv2_web_acl" "main" {
  name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-waf"
  description = "WAF rules for anihpj Django app"
  scope       = "CLOUDFRONT"  # or "REGIONAL" for ALB

  default_action {
    allow {}
  }

  # AWS Managed Rules - Core Rule Set
  rule {
    name     = "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet"
    priority = 1

    override_action {
      none {}
    }

    statement {
      managed_rule_group_statement {
        name        = "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet"
        vendor_name = "AWS"
      }
    }

    visibility_config {
      cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
      metric_name                = "CommonRuleSetMetric"
      sampled_requests_enabled   = true
    }
  }

  # SQL Injection Protection
  rule {
    name     = "AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet"
    priority = 2

    override_action {
      none {}
    }

    statement {
      managed_rule_group_statement {
        name        = "AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet"
        vendor_name = "AWS"
      }
    }

    visibility_config {
      cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
      metric_name                = "SQLiRuleSetMetric"
      sampled_requests_enabled   = true
    }
  }

  # Rate Limiting (per IP)
  rule {
    name     = "RateLimitRule"
    priority = 3

    action {
      block {}
    }

    statement {
      rate_based_statement {
        limit              = 2000  # requests per 5 minutes
        aggregate_key_type = "IP"
      }
    }

    visibility_config {
      cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
      metric_name                = "RateLimitMetric"
      sampled_requests_enabled   = true
    }
  }

  # Block known bad bots
  rule {
    name     = "AWSManagedRulesBotControlRuleSet"
    priority = 4

    override_action {
      none {}
    }

    statement {
      managed_rule_group_statement {
        name        = "AWSManagedRulesBotControlRuleSet"
        vendor_name = "AWS"
      }
    }

    visibility_config {
      cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
      metric_name                = "BotControlMetric"
      sampled_requests_enabled   = true
    }
  }

  visibility_config {
    cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
    metric_name                = "anihpjWAFMetric"
    sampled_requests_enabled   = true
  }

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Associate WAF with CloudFront
resource "aws_wafv2_web_acl_association" "cloudfront" {
  resource_arn = aws_cloudfront_distribution.main.arn
  web_acl_arn  = aws_wafv2_web_acl.main.arn
}
network-security.tf - VPC Flow Logs & NACLs 
# =========================================
# VPC Flow Logs for Network Monitoring
# =========================================

resource "aws_flow_log" "vpc" {
  vpc_id          = aws_vpc.main.id
  traffic_type    = "ALL"
  iam_role_arn    = aws_iam_role.flow_logs.arn
  log_destination = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.vpc_flow_logs.arn

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-flow-logs"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "vpc_flow_logs" {
  name              = "/vpc/anihpj/${var.environment}/flow-logs"
  retention_in_days = 30

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# =========================================
# Network ACLs - Defense in Depth
# =========================================

# Public Subnet NACL
resource "aws_network_acl" "public" {
  vpc_id     = aws_vpc.main.id
  subnet_ids = aws_subnet.public[*].id

  # Allow HTTP/HTTPS inbound
  ingress {
    protocol   = "tcp"
    rule_no    = 100
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 80
    to_port    = 80
  }

  ingress {
    protocol   = "tcp"
    rule_no    = 110
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 443
    to_port    = 443
  }

  # Allow ephemeral ports (return traffic)
  ingress {
    protocol   = "tcp"
    rule_no    = 120
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 1024
    to_port    = 65535
  }

  # Allow all outbound
  egress {
    protocol   = "-1"
    rule_no    = 100
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
    from_port  = 0
    to_port    = 0
  }

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-public-nacl"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# Database Subnet NACL (restrictive)
resource "aws_network_acl" "database" {
  vpc_id     = aws_vpc.main.id
  subnet_ids = aws_subnet.database[*].id

  # Only allow PostgreSQL from private subnets
  ingress {
    protocol   = "tcp"
    rule_no    = 100
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = var.private_subnet_cidr
    from_port  = 5432
    to_port    = 5432
  }

  # Only allow Redis from private subnets
  ingress {
    protocol   = "tcp"
    rule_no    = 110
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = var.private_subnet_cidr
    from_port  = 6379
    to_port    = 6379
  }

  # Return traffic
  egress {
    protocol   = "tcp"
    rule_no    = 100
    action     = "allow"
    cidr_block = var.private_subnet_cidr
    from_port  = 1024
    to_port    = 65535
  }

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-${var.environment}-database-nacl"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}
compliance.tf - CloudTrail, GuardDuty, Security Hub 
# =========================================
# CloudTrail - API Audit Logging
# =========================================

resource "aws_cloudtrail" "main" {
  name                          = "anihpj-${var.environment}-trail"
  s3_bucket_name                = aws_s3_bucket.cloudtrail.id
  include_global_service_events = true
  is_multi_region_trail         = true
  enable_logging                = true

  # Log data events for S3 (media uploads)
  event_selector {
    read_write_type           = "All"
    include_management_events = true

    data_resource {
      type   = "AWS::S3::Object"
      values = ["${aws_s3_bucket.media.arn}/"]
    }
  }

  # Encrypt logs
  kms_key_id = aws_kms_key.cloudtrail.arn

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
    Compliance  = "audit"
  }
}

# =========================================
# GuardDuty - Threat Detection
# =========================================

resource "aws_guardduty_detector" "main" {
  enable = true

  datasources {
    s3_logs {
      enable = true
    }
    kubernetes {
      audit_logs {
        enable = false  # Not using EKS
      }
    }
  }

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# =========================================
# Security Hub - Centralized Security View
# =========================================

resource "aws_securityhub_account" "main" {
  enable_default_standards = true
}

# Enable AWS Foundational Security Best Practices
resource "aws_securityhub_standards_subscription" "aws_foundational" {
  depends_on    = [aws_securityhub_account.main]
  standards_arn = "arn:aws:securityhub:${var.aws_region}::standards/aws-foundational-security-best-practices/v/1.0.0"
}

# Enable CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark
resource "aws_securityhub_standards_subscription" "cis" {
  depends_on    = [aws_securityhub_account.main]
  standards_arn = "arn:aws:securityhub:${var.aws_region}::standards/cis-aws-foundations-benchmark/v/1.4.0"
}

# =========================================
# AWS Config Rules for Compliance
# =========================================

resource "aws_config_rule" "s3_bucket_public_read_prohibited" {
  name = "s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited"

  source {
    owner             = "AWS"
    source_identifier = "S3_BUCKET_PUBLIC_READ_PROHIBITED"
  }

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Compliance  = "data-protection"
  }
}

resource "aws_config_rule" "rds_storage_encrypted" {
  name = "rds-storage-encrypted"

  source {
    owner             = "AWS"
    source_identifier = "RDS_STORAGE_ENCRYPTED"
  }

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Compliance  = "encryption"
  }
}

resource "aws_config_rule" "secretsmanager_rotation_enabled" {
  name = "secretsmanager-rotation-enabled-check"

  source {
    owner             = "AWS"
    source_identifier = "SECRETSMANAGER_ROTATION_ENABLED_CHECK"
  }

  tags = {
    Application = "anihpj"
    Compliance  = "secrets"
  }
}

🛡️ Django + AWS Security Checklist

🌐 Network
✓ VPC with private subnets
✓ Security group least privilege
✓ No public IPs on ECS/RDS
✓ VPC Flow Logs enabled
🔐 Secrets
✓ Secrets Manager for keys
✓ SSM Parameter Store
✓ No hardcoded credentials
✓ Rotation policies
🗄️ Data
✓ RDS encryption at rest
✓ TLS in transit
✓ S3 bucket encryption
✓ Backup encryption
🐍 Django
✓ DEBUG=False in prod
✓ ALLOWED_HOSTS set
✓ CSRF protection
✓ Security middleware
🔍 Audit
✓ CloudTrail enabled
✓ S3 access logging
✓ RDS audit logs
✓ GuardDuty active
🛡️ Edge
✓ WAF with rules
✓ ACM SSL/TLS
✓ CloudFront HTTPS only
✓ Rate limiting

🐍 Django Production Security Settings

# anihpj/settings/production.py

DEBUG = False
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['.anihpj.com', '.elb.amazonaws.com']

# Security Headers
SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True
SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000  # 1 year
SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = True
SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD = True
SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF = True
SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER = True

# Session Security
SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True
SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY = True
SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE = 'Lax'
CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True

# CORS (for jobpost API)
CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS = [
    "https://anihpj.com",
    "https://www.anihpj.com",
]

📋 Django + AWS Terraform Quick Reference

🏗️ Infrastructure
✓ VPC + 3-tier subnets
✓ ECS Fargate for Django
✓ RDS PostgreSQL
✓ ElastiCache Redis
✓ ALB + CloudFront
🔐 Configuration
✓ SSM for settings
✓ Secrets Manager for keys
✓ S3 for static/media
✓ CloudFront CDN
✓ ACM SSL certificates
⚙️ Operations
✓ GitHub Actions CI/CD
✓ Blue/Green deploys
✓ Auto-scaling
✓ Health checks
✓ Rolling updates
📊 Observability
✓ CloudWatch Logs
✓ Custom metrics
✓ Alarms + SNS
✓ X-Ray tracing
✓ Dashboards

⚡ Essential Commands for anihpj Deployment

🚀 Deploy Infrastructure
terraform apply -var-file=prod.tfvars
🔄 Run Migrations
aws ecs run-task --task-definition migrate
📦 Collect Static
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
🔍 Check Logs
aws logs tail /ecs/anihpj/django --follow
🗑️ Invalidate Cache
aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths "/*"
🔐 Get Secrets
aws secretsmanager get-secret-value --secret-id anihpj/prod/db

✅ Section 16 Key Takeaways

🏗️ Architecture
3-tier VPC, ECS Fargate, RDS, ElastiCache
🔐 Secrets
SSM + Secrets Manager, never hardcode
🚀 CI/CD
GitHub Actions + ECS Blue/Green deploys
💰 Cost
Right-size by env, schedule shutdowns, use RI

17. Reference & Cheatsheets

📚

Quick Reference Guide

Your Terraform Cheatsheet Collection

Essential commands, syntax patterns, and templates for rapid Terraform development. Bookmark this section for quick lookups during your infrastructure projects.

⚡ CLI Commands 📝 HCL Syntax ☁️ AWS Resources 📋 Templates ⚖️ Comparisons
⚡
CLI Commands
📝
HCL Syntax
☁️
AWS Provider
📋
Templates
⚖️
Comparisons
